subject: Mathematics
explanation: Given, I = {tex}\int \frac { x ^ { 2 } } { \left( x ^ { 2 } + 4 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 9 \right) } d x{/tex} Consider, {tex}\frac { x ^ { 2 } } { \left( x ^ { 2 } + 4 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 9 \right) }{/tex} Put, {tex}x^2 = t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac { x ^ { 2 } } { \left( x ^ { 2 } + 4 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 9 \right) } = \frac { t } { ( t + 4 ) ( t + 9 ) }{/tex} Using partial fractions, {tex}\frac { t } { ( t + 4 ) ( t + 9 ) } = \frac { A } { ( t + 4 ) } + \frac { B } { ( t + 9 ) }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow t = A(t + 9) + B(t + 4){/tex} Putting t = -9, {tex}\Rightarrow-9 = -5B{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad B = \frac { 9 } { 5 }{/tex} Putting t = -4, {tex}\Rightarrow - 4 = 5 A {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow A = \frac { - 4 } { 5 }{/tex} Thus, {tex}\frac { t } { ( t + 4 ) ( t + 9 ) } = \frac { - 4 } { 5 ( t + 4 ) } + \frac { 9 } { 5 ( t + 9 ) }{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad I = \int \frac { x ^ { 2 } } { \left( x ^ { 2 } + 4 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 9 \right) } d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { - 4 } { 5 \left( x ^ { 2 } + 4 \right) } d x + \int \frac { 9 } { 5 \left( x ^ { 2 } + 9 \right) } d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac { - 4 } { 5 } \int \frac { d x } { x ^ { 2 } + ( 2 ) ^ { 2 } } + \frac { 9 } { 5 } \int \frac { d x } { x ^ { 2 } + ( 3 ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex}= \frac { - 4 } { 5 } \cdot \frac { 1 } { 2 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { x } { 2 } \right) + \frac { 9 } { 5 } \cdot \frac { 1 } { 3 } \cdot \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { x } { 3 } \right) + C{/tex} {tex}\left[ \because \int \frac { 1 } { x ^ { 2 } + a ^ { 2 } } d x = \frac { 1 } { a } \tan ^ { - 1 } \frac { x } { a } + C \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 3 } { 5 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { x } { 3 } \right) - \frac { 2 } { 5 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { x } { 2 } \right) + C{/tex}
solution: Given, I = {tex}\int \frac { x ^ { 2 } } { \left( x ^ { 2 } + 4 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 9 \right) } d x{/tex} Consider, {tex}\frac { x ^ { 2 } } { \left( x ^ { 2 } + 4 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 9 \right) }{/tex} Put, {tex}x^2 = t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac { x ^ { 2 } } { \left( x ^ { 2 } + 4 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 9 \right) } = \frac { t } { ( t + 4 ) ( t + 9 ) }{/tex} Using partial fractions, {tex}\frac { t } { ( t + 4 ) ( t + 9 ) } = \frac { A } { ( t + 4 ) } + \frac { B } { ( t + 9 ) }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow t = A(t + 9) + B(t + 4){/tex} Putting t = -9, {tex}\Rightarrow-9 = -5B{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad B = \frac { 9 } { 5 }{/tex} Putting t = -4, {tex}\Rightarrow - 4 = 5 A {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow A = \frac { - 4 } { 5 }{/tex} Thus, {tex}\frac { t } { ( t + 4 ) ( t + 9 ) } = \frac { - 4 } { 5 ( t + 4 ) } + \frac { 9 } { 5 ( t + 9 ) }{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad I = \int \frac { x ^ { 2 } } { \left( x ^ { 2 } + 4 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 9 \right) } d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { - 4 } { 5 \left( x ^ { 2 } + 4 \right) } d x + \int \frac { 9 } { 5 \left( x ^ { 2 } + 9 \right) } d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac { - 4 } { 5 } \int \frac { d x } { x ^ { 2 } + ( 2 ) ^ { 2 } } + \frac { 9 } { 5 } \int \frac { d x } { x ^ { 2 } + ( 3 ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex}= \frac { - 4 } { 5 } \cdot \frac { 1 } { 2 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { x } { 2 } \right) + \frac { 9 } { 5 } \cdot \frac { 1 } { 3 } \cdot \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { x } { 3 } \right) + C{/tex} {tex}\left[ \because \int \frac { 1 } { x ^ { 2 } + a ^ { 2 } } d x = \frac { 1 } { a } \tan ^ { - 1 } \frac { x } { a } + C \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 3 } { 5 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { x } { 3 } \right) - \frac { 2 } { 5 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { x } { 2 } \right) + C{/tex}
solution_html:
Given, I = {tex}\int \frac { x ^ { 2 } } { \left( x ^ { 2 } + 4 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 9 \right) } d x{/tex}
Consider, {tex}\frac { x ^ { 2 } } { \left( x ^ { 2 } + 4 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 9 \right) }{/tex}
Put, {tex}x^2 = t{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac { x ^ { 2 } } { \left( x ^ { 2 } + 4 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 9 \right) } = \frac { t } { ( t + 4 ) ( t + 9 ) }{/tex}
Using partial fractions, 
 {tex}\frac { t } { ( t + 4 ) ( t + 9 ) } = \frac { A } { ( t + 4 ) } + \frac { B } { ( t + 9 ) }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow  t = A(t + 9) + B(t + 4){/tex}
Putting t = -9, 
{tex}\Rightarrow-9 = -5B{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad B = \frac { 9 } { 5 }{/tex}
Putting t = -4, 
{tex}\Rightarrow - 4 = 5 A {/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow A = \frac { - 4 } { 5 }{/tex}
Thus, {tex}\frac { t } { ( t + 4 ) ( t + 9 ) } = \frac { - 4 } { 5 ( t + 4 ) } + \frac { 9 } { 5 ( t + 9 ) }{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad I = \int \frac { x ^ { 2 } } { \left( x ^ { 2 } + 4 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 9 \right) } d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \frac { - 4 } { 5 \left( x ^ { 2 } + 4 \right) } d x + \int \frac { 9 } { 5 \left( x ^ { 2 } + 9 \right) } d x{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { - 4 } { 5 } \int \frac { d x } { x ^ { 2 } + ( 2 ) ^ { 2 } } + \frac { 9 } { 5 } \int \frac { d x } { x ^ { 2 } + ( 3 ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { - 4 } { 5 } \cdot \frac { 1 } { 2 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { x } { 2 } \right) + \frac { 9 } { 5 } \cdot \frac { 1 } { 3 } \cdot \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { x } { 3 } \right) + C{/tex}
{tex}\left[ \because \int \frac { 1 } { x ^ { 2 } + a ^ { 2 } } d x = \frac { 1 } { a } \tan ^ { - 1 } \frac { x } { a } + C \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 3 } { 5 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { x } { 3 } \right) - \frac { 2 } { 5 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { x } { 2 } \right) + C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Given, {tex}I= \int _ { - 2 } ^ { 2 } \frac { x ^ { 2 } } { 1 + 5 ^ { x } } d x{/tex} ...(i) {tex} = \int _ { - 2 } ^ { 2 } \frac { ( 2 - 2 - x ) ^ { 2 } } { 1 + 5 ^ { 2 - 2 - x } } d x{/tex} {tex}[ \int _ { b } ^ { a } x d x = \int _ { b } ^ { a }(a+b- x) d x ]{/tex} {tex}I= \int _ { - 2 } ^ { 2 } \frac { x ^ { 2 } } { 1 + 5 ^ { - x } } d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \int _ { - 2 } ^ { 2 } \frac { 5 ^ { x } } { 5 ^ { x } + 1 } x ^ { 2 } d x{/tex} ...(ii) Adding Equations (i) and (ii), we get {tex}2I =\int _ { - 2 } ^ { 2 } \left( \frac { 1 + 5 ^ { x } } { 5 ^ { x } + 1 } \right) x ^ { 2 } d x = \int _ { - 2 } ^ { 2 } x ^ { 2 } d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 I = 2 \int _ { 0 } ^ { 2 } x ^ { 2 } d x{/tex} {tex}\Big[x^2{/tex} is even function {tex}\therefore \int _ { - 2 } ^ { 2 } x ^ { 2 } d x = 2 \int _ { 0 } ^ { 2 } x ^ { 2 } d x \Big]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\left[ \frac { x ^ { 3 } } { 3 } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 2 } {/tex} = {tex}\frac { 1 } { 3 } \left( 2 ^ { 3 } - 0 \right) {/tex} = {tex}\frac { 8 } { 3 }{/tex}
solution: Given, {tex}I= \int _ { - 2 } ^ { 2 } \frac { x ^ { 2 } } { 1 + 5 ^ { x } } d x{/tex} ...(i) {tex} = \int _ { - 2 } ^ { 2 } \frac { ( 2 - 2 - x ) ^ { 2 } } { 1 + 5 ^ { 2 - 2 - x } } d x{/tex} {tex}[ \int _ { b } ^ { a } x d x = \int _ { b } ^ { a }(a+b- x) d x ]{/tex} {tex}I= \int _ { - 2 } ^ { 2 } \frac { x ^ { 2 } } { 1 + 5 ^ { - x } } d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \int _ { - 2 } ^ { 2 } \frac { 5 ^ { x } } { 5 ^ { x } + 1 } x ^ { 2 } d x{/tex} ...(ii) Adding Equations (i) and (ii), we get {tex}2I =\int _ { - 2 } ^ { 2 } \left( \frac { 1 + 5 ^ { x } } { 5 ^ { x } + 1 } \right) x ^ { 2 } d x = \int _ { - 2 } ^ { 2 } x ^ { 2 } d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 I = 2 \int _ { 0 } ^ { 2 } x ^ { 2 } d x{/tex} {tex}\Big[x^2{/tex} is even function {tex}\therefore \int _ { - 2 } ^ { 2 } x ^ { 2 } d x = 2 \int _ { 0 } ^ { 2 } x ^ { 2 } d x \Big]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\left[ \frac { x ^ { 3 } } { 3 } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 2 } {/tex} = {tex}\frac { 1 } { 3 } \left( 2 ^ { 3 } - 0 \right) {/tex} = {tex}\frac { 8 } { 3 }{/tex}
solution_html:
Given, {tex}I= \int _ { - 2 } ^ { 2 } \frac { x ^ { 2 } } { 1 + 5 ^ { x } } d x{/tex} ...(i)
{tex} = \int _ { - 2 } ^ { 2 } \frac { ( 2 - 2 - x ) ^ { 2 } } { 1 + 5 ^ { 2 - 2 - x } } d x{/tex}   {tex}[ \int _ { b } ^ { a } x d x = \int _ { b } ^ { a }(a+b- x) d x ]{/tex}
{tex}I= \int _ { - 2 } ^ { 2 } \frac { x ^ { 2 } } { 1 + 5 ^ { - x } } d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \int _ { - 2 } ^ { 2 } \frac { 5 ^ { x } } { 5 ^ { x } + 1 } x ^ { 2 } d x{/tex} ...(ii)
Adding Equations (i) and (ii), we get 
 {tex}2I =\int _ { - 2 } ^ { 2 } \left( \frac { 1 + 5 ^ { x } } { 5 ^ { x } + 1 } \right) x ^ { 2 } d x = \int _ { - 2 } ^ { 2 } x ^ { 2 } d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 I = 2 \int _ { 0 } ^ { 2 } x ^ { 2 } d x{/tex}   {tex}\Big[x^2{/tex} is even function  {tex}\therefore \int _ { - 2 } ^ { 2 } x ^ { 2 } d x = 2 \int _ { 0 } ^ { 2 } x ^ { 2 } d x \Big]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\left[ \frac { x ^ { 3 } } { 3 } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 2 } {/tex}
= {tex}\frac { 1 } { 3 } \left( 2 ^ { 3 } - 0 \right) {/tex}
= {tex}\frac { 8 } { 3 }{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex} sin 2x tan -1 (sin x) dx. = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex} 2 sin x cos x tan -1 (sin x) dx Put sin x = t so that cos x dx = dt Thus, I = {tex}2 \int\limits_0^1{/tex} t tan -1 t dt = {tex}2\left[\left|\frac{\text t^2}{2} \tan ^{-1} \mathrm{t}\right|_0^1\right.{/tex} - {tex}\left.\int\limits_0^1 \frac{1}{1+\mathrm{t}^2} \cdot \frac{t^2}{2} \mathrm{dt}\right]{/tex} = {tex}2 \cdot \frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} - {tex}\int\limits_0^1 \frac{\mathrm{t}^2}{1+\mathrm{t}^2} \mathrm{dt}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}-\int\limits_0^1\left[1-\frac{1}{1+t^2}\right] \mathrm{dt}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}-|\mathrm{t}|_0^1{/tex} + {tex}\left|\tan ^{-1} \mathrm{t}\right|_0^1{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}-1+\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}-1{/tex} ////////////////
solution: Let I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex} sin 2x tan -1 (sin x) dx. = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex} 2 sin x cos x tan -1 (sin x) dx Put sin x = t so that cos x dx = dt Thus, I = {tex}2 \int\limits_0^1{/tex} t tan -1 t dt = {tex}2\left[\left|\frac{\text t^2}{2} \tan ^{-1} \mathrm{t}\right|_0^1\right.{/tex} - {tex}\left.\int\limits_0^1 \frac{1}{1+\mathrm{t}^2} \cdot \frac{t^2}{2} \mathrm{dt}\right]{/tex} = {tex}2 \cdot \frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} - {tex}\int\limits_0^1 \frac{\mathrm{t}^2}{1+\mathrm{t}^2} \mathrm{dt}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}-\int\limits_0^1\left[1-\frac{1}{1+t^2}\right] \mathrm{dt}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}-|\mathrm{t}|_0^1{/tex} + {tex}\left|\tan ^{-1} \mathrm{t}\right|_0^1{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}-1+\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}-1{/tex} ////////////////
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex}sin 2x tan-1(sin x) dx.
= {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex}2 sin x cos x tan-1 (sin x) dx
Put sin x = t so that cos x dx = dt
Thus, I = {tex}2 \int\limits_0^1{/tex}t tan-1 t dt
= {tex}2\left[\left|\frac{\text t^2}{2} \tan ^{-1} \mathrm{t}\right|_0^1\right.{/tex} - {tex}\left.\int\limits_0^1 \frac{1}{1+\mathrm{t}^2} \cdot \frac{t^2}{2} \mathrm{dt}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}2 \cdot \frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} - {tex}\int\limits_0^1 \frac{\mathrm{t}^2}{1+\mathrm{t}^2} \mathrm{dt}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}-\int\limits_0^1\left[1-\frac{1}{1+t^2}\right] \mathrm{dt}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}-|\mathrm{t}|_0^1{/tex} + {tex}\left|\tan ^{-1} \mathrm{t}\right|_0^1{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}-1+\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}-1{/tex}
////////////////

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let I = {tex}\int \frac{1}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex} . Then, {tex}I=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int \frac{\sin (a-b)}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int \frac{\sin \{(x-b)-(x-a)\}}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int \frac{\sin (x-b) \cos (x-a)-\cos (x-b) \sin (x-a)}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int{/tex} {tan (x - b) - tan (x - a)} dx {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}{/tex} {-log e |cos(x - b)| + log e |cos(x - a) |} + c {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \log _{e}\left[\frac{\cos (x-a)}{\cos (x-b)}\right]+C{/tex}
solution: Let I = {tex}\int \frac{1}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex} . Then, {tex}I=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int \frac{\sin (a-b)}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int \frac{\sin \{(x-b)-(x-a)\}}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int \frac{\sin (x-b) \cos (x-a)-\cos (x-b) \sin (x-a)}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int{/tex} {tan (x - b) - tan (x - a)} dx {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}{/tex} {-log e |cos(x - b)| + log e |cos(x - a) |} + c {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \log _{e}\left[\frac{\cos (x-a)}{\cos (x-b)}\right]+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int \frac{1}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex}. Then,
{tex}I=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int \frac{\sin (a-b)}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int \frac{\sin \{(x-b)-(x-a)\}}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int \frac{\sin (x-b) \cos (x-a)-\cos (x-b) \sin (x-a)}{\cos (x-a) \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int{/tex} {tan (x - b) - tan (x - a)} dx
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}{/tex} {-loge |cos(x - b)| + loge |cos(x - a) |} + c
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \log _{e}\left[\frac{\cos (x-a)}{\cos (x-b)}\right]+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: let the given integral be, l = {tex}\int \frac{1}{\cos (x-a) \cdot \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int \frac{\sin (a-b)}{\cos (x-a) \cdot \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int \frac{\sin [(x-b)-(x-a)]}{\cos (x-a) \cdot \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{\sin (x-b) \cdot \cos (x-a)-\cos (x-b) \cdot \sin (x-a)}{\cos (x-a) \cdot \cos (x-b)}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}{/tex} {tex}\int\left[\frac{\sin (x-b) \cdot \cos (x-a)}{\cos (x-a) \cdot \cos (x-b)}-\frac{\cos (x-b) \cdot \sin (x-a)}{\cos (x-a) \cdot \cos (x-b)}\right] d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int[\tan (x-b)-\tan (x-a)] d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int \tan (x-b) d x-\int \tan (x-a) d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}{/tex} [ln |sec(x - b)| - ln|sec(x - a)|] + C {tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}{/tex} [ln |cos(x - a) - ln|cos(x - b|] + C {tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}\left[\ln \left|\frac{\cos (x-a)}{\cos (x-b)}\right|\right]+C{/tex}
solution: let the given integral be, l = {tex}\int \frac{1}{\cos (x-a) \cdot \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int \frac{\sin (a-b)}{\cos (x-a) \cdot \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int \frac{\sin [(x-b)-(x-a)]}{\cos (x-a) \cdot \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{\sin (x-b) \cdot \cos (x-a)-\cos (x-b) \cdot \sin (x-a)}{\cos (x-a) \cdot \cos (x-b)}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}{/tex} {tex}\int\left[\frac{\sin (x-b) \cdot \cos (x-a)}{\cos (x-a) \cdot \cos (x-b)}-\frac{\cos (x-b) \cdot \sin (x-a)}{\cos (x-a) \cdot \cos (x-b)}\right] d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int[\tan (x-b)-\tan (x-a)] d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int \tan (x-b) d x-\int \tan (x-a) d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}{/tex} [ln |sec(x - b)| - ln|sec(x - a)|] + C {tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}{/tex} [ln |cos(x - a) - ln|cos(x - b|] + C {tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}\left[\ln \left|\frac{\cos (x-a)}{\cos (x-b)}\right|\right]+C{/tex}
solution_html:
let the given integral be,

l = {tex}\int \frac{1}{\cos (x-a) \cdot \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int \frac{\sin (a-b)}{\cos (x-a) \cdot \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int \frac{\sin [(x-b)-(x-a)]}{\cos (x-a) \cdot \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{\sin (x-b) \cdot \cos (x-a)-\cos (x-b) \cdot \sin (x-a)}{\cos (x-a) \cdot \cos (x-b)}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}{/tex} {tex}\int\left[\frac{\sin (x-b) \cdot \cos (x-a)}{\cos (x-a) \cdot \cos (x-b)}-\frac{\cos (x-b) \cdot \sin (x-a)}{\cos (x-a) \cdot \cos (x-b)}\right] d x{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int[\tan (x-b)-\tan (x-a)] d x{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int \tan (x-b) d x-\int \tan (x-a) d x{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}{/tex} [ln |sec(x - b)| - ln|sec(x - a)|] + C
{tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}{/tex}[ln |cos(x - a) - ln|cos(x - b|] + C
{tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}\left[\ln \left|\frac{\cos (x-a)}{\cos (x-b)}\right|\right]+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{{{\sin }^{ - 1}}x}}{{{{\left( {1 - {x^2}} \right)}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}dx} = \int {\frac{{{{\sin }^{ - 1}}x}}{{\left( {1 - {x^2}} \right)\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}dx}{/tex} Put {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}x = t \Rightarrow \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}dx = dt{/tex} and {tex}x = \sin t \Rightarrow 1 - {x^2} = {\cos ^2}t{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \cos t = \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} {/tex} {tex}\therefore I = \int {\frac{t}{{{{\cos }^2}t}}dt = \int {t.{{\sec }^2}tdt} } {/tex} {tex}= t.\int {{{\sec }^2}tdt - \int {\left( {\frac{d}{{dt}}t.\int {{{\sec }^2}tdt} } \right)} dt}{/tex} {tex}= t.\tan t - \int {1.\tan tdt}{/tex} {tex}= t.\tan t + \log \left| {\cos t} \right| + C{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \int {\tan xdx = - \log \left| {\cos x} \right| + C} } \right]{/tex} {tex}= {\sin ^{ - 1}}x.\frac{x}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} + \log \left| {\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} } \right| + C{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{{{\sin }^{ - 1}}x}}{{{{\left( {1 - {x^2}} \right)}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}dx} = \int {\frac{{{{\sin }^{ - 1}}x}}{{\left( {1 - {x^2}} \right)\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}dx}{/tex} Put {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}x = t \Rightarrow \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}dx = dt{/tex} and {tex}x = \sin t \Rightarrow 1 - {x^2} = {\cos ^2}t{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \cos t = \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} {/tex} {tex}\therefore I = \int {\frac{t}{{{{\cos }^2}t}}dt = \int {t.{{\sec }^2}tdt} } {/tex} {tex}= t.\int {{{\sec }^2}tdt - \int {\left( {\frac{d}{{dt}}t.\int {{{\sec }^2}tdt} } \right)} dt}{/tex} {tex}= t.\tan t - \int {1.\tan tdt}{/tex} {tex}= t.\tan t + \log \left| {\cos t} \right| + C{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \int {\tan xdx = - \log \left| {\cos x} \right| + C} } \right]{/tex} {tex}= {\sin ^{ - 1}}x.\frac{x}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} + \log \left| {\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} } \right| + C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int {\frac{{{{\sin }^{ - 1}}x}}{{{{\left( {1 - {x^2}} \right)}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}dx} = \int {\frac{{{{\sin }^{ - 1}}x}}{{\left( {1 - {x^2}} \right)\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}dx}{/tex}

Put {tex}{\sin ^{ - 1}}x = t \Rightarrow \frac{1}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}dx = dt{/tex}

and {tex}x = \sin t \Rightarrow 1 - {x^2} = {\cos ^2}t{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \cos t = \sqrt {1 - {x^2}} {/tex}

{tex}\therefore I = \int {\frac{t}{{{{\cos }^2}t}}dt = \int {t.{{\sec }^2}tdt} } {/tex}

{tex}= t.\int {{{\sec }^2}tdt - \int {\left( {\frac{d}{{dt}}t.\int {{{\sec }^2}tdt} } \right)} dt}{/tex}

{tex}= t.\tan t - \int {1.\tan tdt}{/tex}

{tex}= t.\tan t + \log \left| {\cos t} \right| + C{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \int {\tan xdx = - \log \left| {\cos x} \right| + C} } \right]{/tex}

{tex}= {\sin ^{ - 1}}x.\frac{x}{{\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }} + \log \left| {\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} } \right| + C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Evaluate̤:{tex}\int e^{x}\left(\frac{1-\sin x}{1-\cos x}\right) d x{/tex}.
question_text: Evaluate̤: {tex}\int e^{x}\left(\frac{1-\sin x}{1-\cos x}\right) d x{/tex} .
explanation: We have {tex}=I=\int e^{x} \cdot\left(\frac{1-\sin x}{1-\cos x}\right) d x=\int e^{x} \cdot\left\{\frac{1}{(1-\cos x)}-\frac{\sin x}{(1-\cos x)}\right\} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int e^{x} \cdot\left\{\frac{1}{2 \sin ^{2}(x / 2)}-\frac{2 \sin (x / 2) \cos (x / 2)}{2 \sin ^{2}(x / 2)}\right\} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int e^{x} \cdot\left\{\frac{1}{2} \csc ^{2} \frac{x}{2}-\cot \frac{x}{2}\right\} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int e^{x} \cdot\left\{-\cot \frac{x}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \csc ^{2} \frac{x}{2}\right\} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int {/tex} e x . {f(x) + f'(x)} dx where {tex}f(x)=-\cot \frac{x}{2}{/tex} and {tex}f^{\prime}(x)=\frac{1}{2} cosec ^{2} \frac{x}{2}{/tex} = -e x (cot {tex}\frac x 2 {/tex} ) +c
solution: We have {tex}=I=\int e^{x} \cdot\left(\frac{1-\sin x}{1-\cos x}\right) d x=\int e^{x} \cdot\left\{\frac{1}{(1-\cos x)}-\frac{\sin x}{(1-\cos x)}\right\} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int e^{x} \cdot\left\{\frac{1}{2 \sin ^{2}(x / 2)}-\frac{2 \sin (x / 2) \cos (x / 2)}{2 \sin ^{2}(x / 2)}\right\} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int e^{x} \cdot\left\{\frac{1}{2} \csc ^{2} \frac{x}{2}-\cot \frac{x}{2}\right\} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int e^{x} \cdot\left\{-\cot \frac{x}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \csc ^{2} \frac{x}{2}\right\} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int {/tex} e x . {f(x) + f'(x)} dx where {tex}f(x)=-\cot \frac{x}{2}{/tex} and {tex}f^{\prime}(x)=\frac{1}{2} cosec ^{2} \frac{x}{2}{/tex} = -e x (cot {tex}\frac x 2 {/tex} ) +c

{tex}=\int e^{x} \cdot\left\{\frac{1}{2 \sin ^{2}(x / 2)}-\frac{2 \sin (x / 2) \cos (x / 2)}{2 \sin ^{2}(x / 2)}\right\} d x{/tex}

={tex}\int {/tex}ex . {f(x) + f'(x)} dx

= -ex (cot {tex}\frac x 2 {/tex}) +c

subject: Mathematics
explanation: I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left( {1 + \tan x} \right)} dx{/tex} ... (1) = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left[ {1 + \tan \left( {\frac{\pi }{4} - x} \right)} \right]} dx\,\,[by\,\,{P_4}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left[ {1 + \frac{{\tan \frac{\pi }{4} - \tan x}}{{1 + \tan \frac{\pi }{4}.\tan x}}} \right]} dx{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left[ {1 + \frac{{1 - \tan x}}{{1 + \tan x}}} \right]} dx{/tex} {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left[ {\frac{{1 + \tan x + 1 - \tan x}}{{1 + \tan x}}} \right]} dx{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left[ {\frac{2}{{1 + \tan x}}} \right]} dx{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /4} {\log 2} dx - \int_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left( {1 + \tan x} \right)dx} {/tex} = {tex}\log 2\left[ x \right]_0^{\pi /4} - I{/tex} 2I = {tex}\log 2\left[ {\frac{\pi }{4} - 0} \right]{/tex} I = {tex}\log 2.\frac{\pi }{8}{/tex}
solution: I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left( {1 + \tan x} \right)} dx{/tex} ... (1) = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left[ {1 + \tan \left( {\frac{\pi }{4} - x} \right)} \right]} dx\,\,[by\,\,{P_4}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left[ {1 + \frac{{\tan \frac{\pi }{4} - \tan x}}{{1 + \tan \frac{\pi }{4}.\tan x}}} \right]} dx{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left[ {1 + \frac{{1 - \tan x}}{{1 + \tan x}}} \right]} dx{/tex} {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left[ {\frac{{1 + \tan x + 1 - \tan x}}{{1 + \tan x}}} \right]} dx{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left[ {\frac{2}{{1 + \tan x}}} \right]} dx{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /4} {\log 2} dx - \int_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left( {1 + \tan x} \right)dx} {/tex} = {tex}\log 2\left[ x \right]_0^{\pi /4} - I{/tex} 2I = {tex}\log 2\left[ {\frac{\pi }{4} - 0} \right]{/tex} I = {tex}\log 2.\frac{\pi }{8}{/tex}
solution_html:
I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left( {1 + \tan x} \right)} dx{/tex} ... (1)
= {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left[ {1 + \tan \left( {\frac{\pi }{4} - x} \right)} \right]} dx\,\,[by\,\,{P_4}{/tex}
= {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left[ {1 + \frac{{\tan \frac{\pi }{4} - \tan x}}{{1 + \tan \frac{\pi }{4}.\tan x}}} \right]} dx{/tex}
= {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left[ {1 + \frac{{1 - \tan x}}{{1 + \tan x}}} \right]} dx{/tex}
{tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left[ {\frac{{1 + \tan x + 1 - \tan x}}{{1 + \tan x}}} \right]} dx{/tex}
= {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left[ {\frac{2}{{1 + \tan x}}} \right]} dx{/tex}
= {tex}\int\limits_0^{\pi /4} {\log 2} dx - \int_0^{\pi /4} {\log \left( {1 + \tan x} \right)dx} {/tex}
= {tex}\log 2\left[ x \right]_0^{\pi /4} - I{/tex}
2I = {tex}\log 2\left[ {\frac{\pi }{4} - 0} \right]{/tex}
I = {tex}\log 2.\frac{\pi }{8}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let I = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sqrt{\sin x} \cos ^{5} x d x{/tex} Let sinx = t then we get, cos x dx = dt.Also, when x = 0, t = 0 and when x = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2^{\prime}}{/tex} t = 1. Consider cos 5 x = cos 4 x {tex}\times{/tex} cosx = (1 - sin 2 x) 2 {tex}\times{/tex} cosx (Using sin 2 x + cos 2 x = 1) Hence, I = {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \sqrt{x}\left(1-x^{2}\right)^{2} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \sqrt{x} d x+\int_{0}^{1} x^{\frac{9}{2}} d x-2 \int_{0}^{1} x^{\frac{5}{2}} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\left.\frac{2}{3} t^{\frac{3}{2}}\right|_{0} ^{1}+\left.\frac{2}{11} t^{\frac{11}{2}}\right|_{0} ^{1}-\left.\frac{4}{7} t^{\frac{7}{2}}\right|_{0} ^{1} {/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{3}+\frac{2}{11}-\frac{4}{7}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{64}{231}{/tex}
solution: Let I = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sqrt{\sin x} \cos ^{5} x d x{/tex} Let sinx = t then we get, cos x dx = dt.Also, when x = 0, t = 0 and when x = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2^{\prime}}{/tex} t = 1. Consider cos 5 x = cos 4 x {tex}\times{/tex} cosx = (1 - sin 2 x) 2 {tex}\times{/tex} cosx (Using sin 2 x + cos 2 x = 1) Hence, I = {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \sqrt{x}\left(1-x^{2}\right)^{2} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \sqrt{x} d x+\int_{0}^{1} x^{\frac{9}{2}} d x-2 \int_{0}^{1} x^{\frac{5}{2}} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\left.\frac{2}{3} t^{\frac{3}{2}}\right|_{0} ^{1}+\left.\frac{2}{11} t^{\frac{11}{2}}\right|_{0} ^{1}-\left.\frac{4}{7} t^{\frac{7}{2}}\right|_{0} ^{1} {/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{3}+\frac{2}{11}-\frac{4}{7}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{64}{231}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sqrt{\sin x} \cos ^{5} x d x{/tex}
Let sinx = t then we get, cos x dx = dt.Also,
when x = 0, t = 0
and
when x = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2^{\prime}}{/tex} t = 1.
Consider cos5x = cos4x {tex}\times{/tex} cosx = (1 - sin2x)2{tex}\times{/tex} cosx (Using sin2x + cos2x = 1)
Hence,
I = {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \sqrt{x}\left(1-x^{2}\right)^{2} d x{/tex}
= {tex}\int_{0}^{1} \sqrt{x} d x+\int_{0}^{1} x^{\frac{9}{2}} d x-2 \int_{0}^{1} x^{\frac{5}{2}} d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\left.\frac{2}{3} t^{\frac{3}{2}}\right|_{0} ^{1}+\left.\frac{2}{11} t^{\frac{11}{2}}\right|_{0} ^{1}-\left.\frac{4}{7} t^{\frac{7}{2}}\right|_{0} ^{1} {/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2}{3}+\frac{2}{11}-\frac{4}{7}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{64}{231}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let {tex}I=\int\limits_{\frac 13}^{1} \frac{\left(\text x-\text x^{3}\right)^{\frac 1 3}}{\text x^{4}} \text {dx}{/tex} , then we have {tex}I=\int\limits_{\frac{1}{3}}^{1} \frac{\left(\frac{1}{\text x^{2}}-1\right)^{\frac 13}}{\text x^{3}} \text {dx}{/tex} {tex}\text { Let } \frac{1}{x^{2}}-1=t{/tex} then {tex}\frac{-2}{x^{3}} d x=d t{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{1}{3} \Rightarrow t=8 \text { and } x=1 \Rightarrow t=0{/tex} {tex}I=-\frac{1}{2} \int_{8}^{0}(t)^{\frac 13} d t{/tex} {tex}I=-\frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{t^{8}}{\frac 4 3}\right]_{8}^{0}{/tex} {tex}I=-\frac{1}{2}[0-12]=6{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}I=\int\limits_{\frac 13}^{1} \frac{\left(\text x-\text x^{3}\right)^{\frac 1 3}}{\text x^{4}} \text {dx}{/tex} , then we have {tex}I=\int\limits_{\frac{1}{3}}^{1} \frac{\left(\frac{1}{\text x^{2}}-1\right)^{\frac 13}}{\text x^{3}} \text {dx}{/tex} {tex}\text { Let } \frac{1}{x^{2}}-1=t{/tex} then {tex}\frac{-2}{x^{3}} d x=d t{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{1}{3} \Rightarrow t=8 \text { and } x=1 \Rightarrow t=0{/tex} {tex}I=-\frac{1}{2} \int_{8}^{0}(t)^{\frac 13} d t{/tex} {tex}I=-\frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{t^{8}}{\frac 4 3}\right]_{8}^{0}{/tex} {tex}I=-\frac{1}{2}[0-12]=6{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I=\int\limits_{\frac 13}^{1} \frac{\left(\text x-\text x^{3}\right)^{\frac 1 3}}{\text x^{4}} \text {dx}{/tex} , then we have
{tex}I=\int\limits_{\frac{1}{3}}^{1} \frac{\left(\frac{1}{\text x^{2}}-1\right)^{\frac 13}}{\text x^{3}} \text {dx}{/tex}
{tex}\text { Let } \frac{1}{x^{2}}-1=t{/tex}  then {tex}\frac{-2}{x^{3}} d x=d t{/tex}
{tex}x=\frac{1}{3} \Rightarrow t=8 \text { and } x=1 \Rightarrow t=0{/tex}
{tex}I=-\frac{1}{2} \int_{8}^{0}(t)^{\frac 13} d t{/tex}
{tex}I=-\frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{t^{8}}{\frac 4 3}\right]_{8}^{0}{/tex}
{tex}I=-\frac{1}{2}[0-12]=6{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let {tex}I = \int\limits_0^\pi {\frac{x}{{1 + \sin x}}} dx{/tex} ...(i) and {tex}I = \int\limits_0^\pi {\frac{{\left( {\pi - x} \right)}}{{1 + \sin \left( {\pi - x} \right)}}} dx = \int_0^\pi {\frac{{\pi - x}}{{1 + \sin x}}dx} {/tex} ...(ii) On adding Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get {tex}2I = \pi \int_0^\pi {\frac{1}{{1 + \sin x}}} dx{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int_0^\pi {\frac{{\left( {1 - \sin x} \right)dx}}{{\left( {1 + \sin x} \right)\left( {1 - \sin x} \right)}}}{/tex} {tex}= \pi \int_0^\pi {\frac{{\left( {1 - \sin x} \right)dx}}{{{{\cos }^2}x}}}{/tex} {tex}= \pi \int_0^\pi {\left( {{{\sec }^2}x - \tan x.\sec x} \right)dx}{/tex} {tex}= \pi \int\limits_0^\pi {{{\sec }^2}xdx} - \pi \int\limits_0^\pi {\sec x\tan xdx}{/tex} {tex}= \pi \left( {\tan x} \right)_0^\pi -\pi \left( {\sec x} \right)_0^\pi {/tex} {tex} = \pi \left( {\tan \pi - \tan 0) -\pi (\sec \pi - \sec 0} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2I = \pi \left( {0 - 0 )- \pi(-1 - 1} \right) = 2\pi{/tex} {tex}2I = 2\pi{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\pi{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}I = \int\limits_0^\pi {\frac{x}{{1 + \sin x}}} dx{/tex} ...(i) and {tex}I = \int\limits_0^\pi {\frac{{\left( {\pi - x} \right)}}{{1 + \sin \left( {\pi - x} \right)}}} dx = \int_0^\pi {\frac{{\pi - x}}{{1 + \sin x}}dx} {/tex} ...(ii) On adding Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get {tex}2I = \pi \int_0^\pi {\frac{1}{{1 + \sin x}}} dx{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int_0^\pi {\frac{{\left( {1 - \sin x} \right)dx}}{{\left( {1 + \sin x} \right)\left( {1 - \sin x} \right)}}}{/tex} {tex}= \pi \int_0^\pi {\frac{{\left( {1 - \sin x} \right)dx}}{{{{\cos }^2}x}}}{/tex} {tex}= \pi \int_0^\pi {\left( {{{\sec }^2}x - \tan x.\sec x} \right)dx}{/tex} {tex}= \pi \int\limits_0^\pi {{{\sec }^2}xdx} - \pi \int\limits_0^\pi {\sec x\tan xdx}{/tex} {tex}= \pi \left( {\tan x} \right)_0^\pi -\pi \left( {\sec x} \right)_0^\pi {/tex} {tex} = \pi \left( {\tan \pi - \tan 0) -\pi (\sec \pi - \sec 0} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2I = \pi \left( {0 - 0 )- \pi(-1 - 1} \right) = 2\pi{/tex} {tex}2I = 2\pi{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\pi{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int\limits_0^\pi {\frac{x}{{1 + \sin x}}} dx{/tex} ...(i)
and {tex}I = \int\limits_0^\pi {\frac{{\left( {\pi - x} \right)}}{{1 + \sin \left( {\pi - x} \right)}}} dx = \int_0^\pi {\frac{{\pi - x}}{{1 + \sin x}}dx} {/tex} ...(ii)
On adding Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get
{tex}2I = \pi \int_0^\pi {\frac{1}{{1 + \sin x}}} dx{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int_0^\pi {\frac{{\left( {1 - \sin x} \right)dx}}{{\left( {1 + \sin x} \right)\left( {1 - \sin x} \right)}}}{/tex}
{tex}= \pi \int_0^\pi {\frac{{\left( {1 - \sin x} \right)dx}}{{{{\cos }^2}x}}}{/tex}
{tex}= \pi \int_0^\pi {\left( {{{\sec }^2}x - \tan x.\sec x} \right)dx}{/tex}
{tex}= \pi \int\limits_0^\pi {{{\sec }^2}xdx} - \pi \int\limits_0^\pi {\sec x\tan xdx}{/tex}
{tex}= \pi \left( {\tan x} \right)_0^\pi -\pi \left( {\sec x} \right)_0^\pi {/tex}
{tex} = \pi \left( {\tan \pi - \tan 0) -\pi (\sec \pi - \sec 0} \right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 2I = \pi \left( {0 - 0 )- \pi(-1 - 1} \right) = 2\pi{/tex}
{tex}2I = 2\pi{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\pi{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate: {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac \pi 2} \frac{\text x \sin \text x \cos \text x}{\sin ^4 \text x+\cos ^4 \text x} \text {dx}{/tex}
explanation: I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\text x \sin \text x \cos \text x}{\sin ^4 \text x+\cos ^4 \text x} \text {dx}{/tex} ...(1) using {tex}\int\limits_0^a{/tex} f(x) dx = {tex}\int\limits_0^a{/tex} f(a - x) dx I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\text x\right) \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\text x\right) \cos \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\text x\right)}{\sin ^4\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\text x\right)+\cos ^4\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\text x\right)} \text {dx}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\text x\right) \cos \text x \sin \text x}{\cos ^4 \text x+\sin ^4 \text x} \text {dx}{/tex} ...(2) adding (1) and (2) 2I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2} \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin \text x \cos \text x}{\sin ^4 \text x+\cos ^4 \text x} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\pi \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{\sin \text x \cos \text x}{\sin ^4 \text x+\cos ^4 \text x} \text {dx}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2} \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{\tan \text x \sec ^2 \text x}{\left(\tan ^2 \text x\right)^2+1} \text {dx}{/tex} ( {tex}\because{/tex} dividing by cos 4 x) Putting tan 2 x = t gives I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4} \int\limits_0^1 \frac{1}{\text t^2+1} \text {dt}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi^2}{16}{/tex}
solution: I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\text x \sin \text x \cos \text x}{\sin ^4 \text x+\cos ^4 \text x} \text {dx}{/tex} ...(1) using {tex}\int\limits_0^a{/tex} f(x) dx = {tex}\int\limits_0^a{/tex} f(a - x) dx I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\text x\right) \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\text x\right) \cos \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\text x\right)}{\sin ^4\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\text x\right)+\cos ^4\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\text x\right)} \text {dx}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\text x\right) \cos \text x \sin \text x}{\cos ^4 \text x+\sin ^4 \text x} \text {dx}{/tex} ...(2) adding (1) and (2) 2I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2} \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin \text x \cos \text x}{\sin ^4 \text x+\cos ^4 \text x} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\pi \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{\sin \text x \cos \text x}{\sin ^4 \text x+\cos ^4 \text x} \text {dx}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2} \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{\tan \text x \sec ^2 \text x}{\left(\tan ^2 \text x\right)^2+1} \text {dx}{/tex} ( {tex}\because{/tex} dividing by cos 4 x) Putting tan 2 x = t gives I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4} \int\limits_0^1 \frac{1}{\text t^2+1} \text {dt}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi^2}{16}{/tex}
solution_html:
I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\text x \sin \text x \cos \text x}{\sin ^4 \text x+\cos ^4 \text x} \text {dx}{/tex} ...(1)
using {tex}\int\limits_0^a{/tex}f(x) dx = {tex}\int\limits_0^a{/tex}f(a - x) dx
I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\text x\right) \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\text x\right) \cos \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\text x\right)}{\sin ^4\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\text x\right)+\cos ^4\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\text x\right)} \text {dx}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\text x\right) \cos \text x \sin \text x}{\cos ^4 \text x+\sin ^4 \text x} \text {dx}{/tex} ...(2)
adding (1) and (2)
2I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2} \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin \text x \cos \text x}{\sin ^4 \text x+\cos ^4 \text x} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\pi \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{\sin \text x \cos \text x}{\sin ^4 \text x+\cos ^4 \text x} \text {dx}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2} \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{\tan \text x \sec ^2 \text x}{\left(\tan ^2 \text x\right)^2+1} \text {dx}{/tex} ({tex}\because{/tex} dividing by cos4 x)
Putting tan2 x = t gives I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4} \int\limits_0^1 \frac{1}{\text t^2+1} \text {dt}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi^2}{16}{/tex}

explanation: Let I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{4}}{/tex} log (1 + tan x)dx ...(i) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{4}}{/tex} log {tex}\left(1+\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}-\text x\right)\right) \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{4}}{/tex} log {tex}\left(\frac{2}{1+\tan \text x}\right) \text {dx}{/tex} ...(ii) Add (i) and (ii), 2I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{4}}{/tex} log 2 dx = log 2 {tex}\cdot{/tex} {tex}\text x]_0^{\frac {\pi}{4}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4} {/tex} log 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{8}{/tex} log 2 //////////
solution: Let I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{4}}{/tex} log (1 + tan x)dx ...(i) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{4}}{/tex} log {tex}\left(1+\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}-\text x\right)\right) \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{4}}{/tex} log {tex}\left(\frac{2}{1+\tan \text x}\right) \text {dx}{/tex} ...(ii) Add (i) and (ii), 2I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{4}}{/tex} log 2 dx = log 2 {tex}\cdot{/tex} {tex}\text x]_0^{\frac {\pi}{4}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4} {/tex} log 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{8}{/tex} log 2 //////////
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{4}}{/tex}log (1 + tan x)dx ...(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{4}}{/tex}log {tex}\left(1+\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}-\text x\right)\right) \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{4}}{/tex}log {tex}\left(\frac{2}{1+\tan \text x}\right) \text {dx}{/tex} ...(ii)
Add (i) and (ii), 2I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{4}}{/tex}log 2 dx = log 2{tex}\cdot{/tex}{tex}\text x]_0^{\frac {\pi}{4}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4} {/tex}log 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{8}{/tex}log 2

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find {tex}\int \text e^{\cot ^{-1} \text x}\left(\frac{1-\text x+\text x^2}{1+\text x^2}\right) \mathrm{dx}{/tex}.
question_text: Find {tex}\int \text e^{\cot ^{-1} \text x}\left(\frac{1-\text x+\text x^2}{1+\text x^2}\right) \mathrm{dx}{/tex} .
explanation: Put cot -1 x = t {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = cot t and {tex}\frac{1}{1+\text x^2} \text {dx}{/tex} = -dt {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\int \text e^{\cot ^{-1} \text x}\left(\frac{1-\text x+\text x^2}{1+\text x^2}\right) \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}-\int \text e^\text t{/tex} (1 - cot t + cot 2 t)dt = {tex}\int \text e^\text t{/tex} (cot t - cosec 2 t)dt = e t cot t + c = {tex}\text {xe}^{\cot ^{-1} \text x}{/tex} + c ///////
solution: Put cot -1 x = t {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = cot t and {tex}\frac{1}{1+\text x^2} \text {dx}{/tex} = -dt {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\int \text e^{\cot ^{-1} \text x}\left(\frac{1-\text x+\text x^2}{1+\text x^2}\right) \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}-\int \text e^\text t{/tex} (1 - cot t + cot 2 t)dt = {tex}\int \text e^\text t{/tex} (cot t - cosec 2 t)dt = e t cot t + c = {tex}\text {xe}^{\cot ^{-1} \text x}{/tex} + c ///////
solution_html:
Put cot-1 x = t {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = cot t and {tex}\frac{1}{1+\text x^2} \text {dx}{/tex} = -dt
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\int \text e^{\cot ^{-1} \text x}\left(\frac{1-\text x+\text x^2}{1+\text x^2}\right) \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}-\int \text e^\text t{/tex}(1 - cot t + cot2 t)dt = {tex}\int \text e^\text t{/tex}(cot t - cosec2 t)dt
= et cot t + c
= {tex}\text {xe}^{\cot ^{-1} \text x}{/tex} + c
///////

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let I = {tex}\int_{\log \sqrt{2}}^{\log \sqrt{3}} \frac{1}{\left(e^\text x+e^{-\text x}\right)\left(e^\text x-e^{-\text x}\right)} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int_{\log \sqrt{2}}^{\log \sqrt{3}} \frac{\text e^{2 \text x}}{\left(\text e^{2 \text x}\right)^2-1} \text {dx}{/tex} Put e 2x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} e 2x dx = {tex}\frac{1}{2} {/tex} dt, Upper limit = 3, Lower Limit = 2 {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int\limits_2^3 \frac{1}{\text t^2-1} \text {dt}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log \left|\frac{t-1}{t+1}\right|_2^3{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{4}\left[\log \frac{2}{4}-\log \frac{1}{3}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log \frac{3}{2}{/tex} //////////
solution: Let I = {tex}\int_{\log \sqrt{2}}^{\log \sqrt{3}} \frac{1}{\left(e^\text x+e^{-\text x}\right)\left(e^\text x-e^{-\text x}\right)} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int_{\log \sqrt{2}}^{\log \sqrt{3}} \frac{\text e^{2 \text x}}{\left(\text e^{2 \text x}\right)^2-1} \text {dx}{/tex} Put e 2x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} e 2x dx = {tex}\frac{1}{2} {/tex} dt, Upper limit = 3, Lower Limit = 2 {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int\limits_2^3 \frac{1}{\text t^2-1} \text {dt}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log \left|\frac{t-1}{t+1}\right|_2^3{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{4}\left[\log \frac{2}{4}-\log \frac{1}{3}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log \frac{3}{2}{/tex} //////////
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int_{\log \sqrt{2}}^{\log \sqrt{3}} \frac{1}{\left(e^\text x+e^{-\text x}\right)\left(e^\text x-e^{-\text x}\right)} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int_{\log \sqrt{2}}^{\log \sqrt{3}} \frac{\text e^{2 \text x}}{\left(\text e^{2 \text x}\right)^2-1} \text {dx}{/tex}
Put e2x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} e2x dx = {tex}\frac{1}{2} {/tex}dt, Upper limit = 3, Lower Limit = 2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int\limits_2^3 \frac{1}{\text t^2-1} \text {dt}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log \left|\frac{t-1}{t+1}\right|_2^3{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{4}\left[\log \frac{2}{4}-\log \frac{1}{3}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log \frac{3}{2}{/tex}
//////////

explanation: I = {tex}\int\limits_{-1}^1{/tex} |x 4 - x| dx = {tex}\int\limits_{-1}^1{/tex} (x 4 - x) dx - {tex}\int\limits_0^1{/tex} (x 4 - x) dx = {tex}\left.\left.\left(\frac{\text x^5}{5}-\frac{\text x^2}{3}\right)\right]_{-1}^0-\left(\frac{\text x^5}{5}-\frac{\text x^2}{2}\right)\right]_0^1{/tex} = {tex}\frac{7}{10}+\frac{3}{10}{/tex} = 1 ///////

solution_html:
I = {tex}\int\limits_{-1}^1{/tex}|x4 - x| dx = {tex}\int\limits_{-1}^1{/tex}(x4 - x) dx - {tex}\int\limits_0^1{/tex}(x4 - x) dx
= {tex}\left.\left.\left(\frac{\text x^5}{5}-\frac{\text x^2}{3}\right)\right]_{-1}^0-\left(\frac{\text x^5}{5}-\frac{\text x^2}{2}\right)\right]_0^1{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{7}{10}+\frac{3}{10}{/tex} = 1

solution: {tex}\int \frac{\text x+2}{\sqrt{\text x^2-4 \text x-5}} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 \text x-4}{\sqrt{\text x^2-4 \text x-5}} \text {dx}{/tex} + {tex}4 \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{(\text x-2)^2-3^2}} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{\text x^2-4 \text x-5}+4 \log {/tex} {tex}\left|\text x-2+\sqrt{\text x^2-4 \text x-5}\right|+\text c{/tex} ///////

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Evaluate {tex}\int\limits_{-\mathrm{a}}^{\mathrm{a}}{/tex}f(x) dx, where f(x) = {tex}\frac{9^\text x}{1+9^\text x}{/tex}.
question_text: Evaluate {tex}\int\limits_{-\mathrm{a}}^{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} f(x) dx, where f(x) = {tex}\frac{9^\text x}{1+9^\text x}{/tex} .
explanation: I = {tex}\int\limits_{-\text a}^\text a \frac{9^\text x}{1+9^\text x} \text {dx}{/tex} ...(i) Replacing 'x' by -a + a - x I = {tex}\int\limits_{-\text a}^\text a \frac{9^{\text a-\text a-\text x}}{1+9^{\text a-\text a-\text x}} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_{-\text a}^\text a \frac{9^{-\text x}}{1+9^{-\text x}} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_{-\text a}^\text a \frac{1}{1+9^\text x} \text {dx}{/tex} ...(ii) Adding (i) (ii) we get, 2I = {tex}\int\limits_{-a}^a \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\mathbf{x}]_{-\mathbf{a}}^{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} = 2a {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = a //////////
solution: I = {tex}\int\limits_{-\text a}^\text a \frac{9^\text x}{1+9^\text x} \text {dx}{/tex} ...(i) Replacing 'x' by -a + a - x I = {tex}\int\limits_{-\text a}^\text a \frac{9^{\text a-\text a-\text x}}{1+9^{\text a-\text a-\text x}} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_{-\text a}^\text a \frac{9^{-\text x}}{1+9^{-\text x}} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_{-\text a}^\text a \frac{1}{1+9^\text x} \text {dx}{/tex} ...(ii) Adding (i) (ii) we get, 2I = {tex}\int\limits_{-a}^a \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\mathbf{x}]_{-\mathbf{a}}^{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} = 2a {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = a //////////
solution_html:
I = {tex}\int\limits_{-\text a}^\text a \frac{9^\text x}{1+9^\text x} \text {dx}{/tex} ...(i)
Replacing 'x' by -a + a - x
I = {tex}\int\limits_{-\text a}^\text a \frac{9^{\text a-\text a-\text x}}{1+9^{\text a-\text a-\text x}} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_{-\text a}^\text a \frac{9^{-\text x}}{1+9^{-\text x}} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_{-\text a}^\text a \frac{1}{1+9^\text x} \text {dx}{/tex} ...(ii)
Adding (i) (ii) we get, 2I = {tex}\int\limits_{-a}^a \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\mathbf{x}]_{-\mathbf{a}}^{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} = 2a {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = a
//////////

explanation: Let I = {tex}\int\limits_1^\text e \frac{1}{\sqrt{4 \text x^2-(\mathrm{x} \log \mathrm{x})^2}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_1^\text e \frac{1}{\text x \sqrt{4-(\log \mathrm{x})^2}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} [Let log x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{\text x}{/tex} dx = dt] = {tex}\int\limits_0^1 \frac{\text {dt}}{\sqrt{4-\mathrm{t}^2}}{/tex} = {tex}\left.\sin ^{-1} \frac{\mathrm{t}}{2}\right|_0 ^1=\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex} /////////
solution: Let I = {tex}\int\limits_1^\text e \frac{1}{\sqrt{4 \text x^2-(\mathrm{x} \log \mathrm{x})^2}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_1^\text e \frac{1}{\text x \sqrt{4-(\log \mathrm{x})^2}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} [Let log x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{\text x}{/tex} dx = dt] = {tex}\int\limits_0^1 \frac{\text {dt}}{\sqrt{4-\mathrm{t}^2}}{/tex} = {tex}\left.\sin ^{-1} \frac{\mathrm{t}}{2}\right|_0 ^1=\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex} /////////
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int\limits_1^\text e \frac{1}{\sqrt{4 \text x^2-(\mathrm{x} \log \mathrm{x})^2}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex}
= {tex}\int\limits_1^\text e \frac{1}{\text x \sqrt{4-(\log \mathrm{x})^2}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} [Let log x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{\text x}{/tex}dx = dt]
= {tex}\int\limits_0^1 \frac{\text {dt}}{\sqrt{4-\mathrm{t}^2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\left.\sin ^{-1} \frac{\mathrm{t}}{2}\right|_0 ^1=\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: I = {tex}\int \frac{\cos \text x}{3 \sin \text x-4 \sin ^3 \text x} \text {dx}{/tex} Let sin x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} cos x dx = dt I = {tex}\int \frac{\text {dt}}{3 t-4 t^3}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{\text t^3\left(\frac{3}{\text t^2}-4\right)} \text {dt}{/tex} Let {tex}\frac{3}{\text t^2}-4{/tex} = z {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}-\frac{6}{\mathrm{t}^3}{/tex} dt = dz I = {tex}-\frac{1}{6} \int \frac{\text {dz}}{\text z}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{6}{/tex} log |z| + C = {tex}-\frac{1}{6}{/tex} log |3 cosec 2 x - 4| + C //////////
solution: I = {tex}\int \frac{\cos \text x}{3 \sin \text x-4 \sin ^3 \text x} \text {dx}{/tex} Let sin x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} cos x dx = dt I = {tex}\int \frac{\text {dt}}{3 t-4 t^3}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{\text t^3\left(\frac{3}{\text t^2}-4\right)} \text {dt}{/tex} Let {tex}\frac{3}{\text t^2}-4{/tex} = z {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}-\frac{6}{\mathrm{t}^3}{/tex} dt = dz I = {tex}-\frac{1}{6} \int \frac{\text {dz}}{\text z}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{6}{/tex} log |z| + C = {tex}-\frac{1}{6}{/tex} log |3 cosec 2 x - 4| + C //////////
solution_html:
I = {tex}\int \frac{\cos \text x}{3 \sin \text x-4 \sin ^3 \text x} \text {dx}{/tex}
Let sin x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} cos x dx = dt
I = {tex}\int \frac{\text {dt}}{3 t-4 t^3}{/tex}
= {tex}\int \frac{1}{\text t^3\left(\frac{3}{\text t^2}-4\right)} \text {dt}{/tex}
Let {tex}\frac{3}{\text t^2}-4{/tex} = z {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}-\frac{6}{\mathrm{t}^3}{/tex}dt = dz
I = {tex}-\frac{1}{6} \int \frac{\text {dz}}{\text z}{/tex}
= {tex}-\frac{1}{6}{/tex}log |z| + C
= {tex}-\frac{1}{6}{/tex}log |3 cosec2 x - 4| + C

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let I = {tex}\int{/tex} x 2 log (x 2 + 1) dx = log (x 2 + 1) {tex} \cdot {/tex} {tex}\frac{\text x^3}{3}-\int \frac{2 \text x}{\text x^2+1} \cdot \frac{\text x^3}{3} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text x^3}{3} {/tex} log (x 2 + 1) - {tex}\frac{2}{3} \int \frac{\text x^4}{\text x^2+1} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text x^3}{3} {/tex} log (x 2 + 1) - {tex}\frac{2}{3} \int{/tex} (x 2 - 1 + {tex}\frac{1}{x^2+1}{/tex} ) dx = {tex}\frac{\text x^3}{3} {/tex} log (x 2 + 1) - {tex}\frac{2}{3}\left[\frac{\text x^3}{3}-\text x+\tan ^{-1} \text x\right]{/tex} + C /////////
solution: Let I = {tex}\int{/tex} x 2 log (x 2 + 1) dx = log (x 2 + 1) {tex} \cdot {/tex} {tex}\frac{\text x^3}{3}-\int \frac{2 \text x}{\text x^2+1} \cdot \frac{\text x^3}{3} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text x^3}{3} {/tex} log (x 2 + 1) - {tex}\frac{2}{3} \int \frac{\text x^4}{\text x^2+1} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text x^3}{3} {/tex} log (x 2 + 1) - {tex}\frac{2}{3} \int{/tex} (x 2 - 1 + {tex}\frac{1}{x^2+1}{/tex} ) dx = {tex}\frac{\text x^3}{3} {/tex} log (x 2 + 1) - {tex}\frac{2}{3}\left[\frac{\text x^3}{3}-\text x+\tan ^{-1} \text x\right]{/tex} + C /////////
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int{/tex}x2 log (x2 + 1) dx
= log (x2 + 1){tex} \cdot {/tex}{tex}\frac{\text x^3}{3}-\int \frac{2 \text x}{\text x^2+1} \cdot \frac{\text x^3}{3} \text {dx}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\text x^3}{3} {/tex}log (x2 + 1) - {tex}\frac{2}{3} \int \frac{\text x^4}{\text x^2+1} \text {dx}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\text x^3}{3} {/tex}log (x2 + 1) - {tex}\frac{2}{3} \int{/tex}(x2 - 1 + {tex}\frac{1}{x^2+1}{/tex}) dx
= {tex}\frac{\text x^3}{3} {/tex}log (x2 + 1) - {tex}\frac{2}{3}\left[\frac{\text x^3}{3}-\text x+\tan ^{-1} \text x\right]{/tex} + C

explanation: I = {tex}\int\limits_{\frac {-\pi}{2}}^{\frac {\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}}{\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}+\cos ^{100} \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} I = {tex}2 \int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}}{\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}+\cos ^{100} \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} as f(x) = {tex}\frac{\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}}{\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}+\cos ^{100} \mathrm{x}}{/tex} is even I = {tex}2 \int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}} \frac{\cos ^{100} \mathrm{x}}{\cos ^{100} \mathrm{x}+\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} using {tex}\int\limits_0^a{/tex} f(x) dx = {tex}\int\limits_0^a{/tex} f(a - x)dx 2I = {tex}2 \int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}+\cos ^{100} \mathrm{x}}{\cos ^{100} \mathrm{x}+\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} = {tex}2 \int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}} \text {dx}{/tex} I = {tex}\left.\mathrm{x}\right|_0 ^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} ////////
solution: I = {tex}\int\limits_{\frac {-\pi}{2}}^{\frac {\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}}{\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}+\cos ^{100} \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} I = {tex}2 \int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}}{\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}+\cos ^{100} \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} as f(x) = {tex}\frac{\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}}{\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}+\cos ^{100} \mathrm{x}}{/tex} is even I = {tex}2 \int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}} \frac{\cos ^{100} \mathrm{x}}{\cos ^{100} \mathrm{x}+\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} using {tex}\int\limits_0^a{/tex} f(x) dx = {tex}\int\limits_0^a{/tex} f(a - x)dx 2I = {tex}2 \int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}+\cos ^{100} \mathrm{x}}{\cos ^{100} \mathrm{x}+\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} = {tex}2 \int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}} \text {dx}{/tex} I = {tex}\left.\mathrm{x}\right|_0 ^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} ////////
solution_html:
I = {tex}\int\limits_{\frac {-\pi}{2}}^{\frac {\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}}{\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}+\cos ^{100} \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex}
I = {tex}2 \int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}}{\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}+\cos ^{100} \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} as f(x) = {tex}\frac{\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}}{\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}+\cos ^{100} \mathrm{x}}{/tex} is even
I = {tex}2 \int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}} \frac{\cos ^{100} \mathrm{x}}{\cos ^{100} \mathrm{x}+\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} using {tex}\int\limits_0^a{/tex}f(x) dx = {tex}\int\limits_0^a{/tex}f(a - x)dx
2I = {tex}2 \int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}+\cos ^{100} \mathrm{x}}{\cos ^{100} \mathrm{x}+\sin ^{100} \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} = {tex}2 \int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}} \text {dx}{/tex}
I = {tex}\left.\mathrm{x}\right|_0 ^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let I = {tex}\int \frac{\text x^2+\text x+1}{(\text x+1)^2(\text x+2)} \text {dx}{/tex} Here {tex}\frac{\text x^2+\text x+1}{(\text x+1)^2(\text x+2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text A}{(\text x+1)}+\frac{\text B}{(\text x+1)^2}+\frac{\text C}{\text x+2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 + x + 1 = A(x + 1)(x + 2) + B(x + 2) + C(x + 1) 2 On comparing, we get A = -2, B = 1 and C = 3. {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\int \frac{-2 \mathrm{dx}}{\text x+1}+\int \frac{\mathrm{dx}}{(\text x+1)^2}{/tex} + {tex}3 \int \frac{\mathrm{dx}}{\text x+2}{/tex} = -2 log |x + 1| - {tex}\frac{1}{\text x+1}{/tex} + 3 log |x + 2| + C //////////
solution: Let I = {tex}\int \frac{\text x^2+\text x+1}{(\text x+1)^2(\text x+2)} \text {dx}{/tex} Here {tex}\frac{\text x^2+\text x+1}{(\text x+1)^2(\text x+2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text A}{(\text x+1)}+\frac{\text B}{(\text x+1)^2}+\frac{\text C}{\text x+2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 + x + 1 = A(x + 1)(x + 2) + B(x + 2) + C(x + 1) 2 On comparing, we get A = -2, B = 1 and C = 3. {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\int \frac{-2 \mathrm{dx}}{\text x+1}+\int \frac{\mathrm{dx}}{(\text x+1)^2}{/tex} + {tex}3 \int \frac{\mathrm{dx}}{\text x+2}{/tex} = -2 log |x + 1| - {tex}\frac{1}{\text x+1}{/tex} + 3 log |x + 2| + C //////////
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int \frac{\text x^2+\text x+1}{(\text x+1)^2(\text x+2)} \text {dx}{/tex}
Here {tex}\frac{\text x^2+\text x+1}{(\text x+1)^2(\text x+2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text A}{(\text x+1)}+\frac{\text B}{(\text x+1)^2}+\frac{\text C}{\text x+2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 + x + 1 = A(x + 1)(x + 2) + B(x + 2) + C(x + 1)2
On comparing, we get
A = -2, B = 1 and C = 3.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\int \frac{-2 \mathrm{dx}}{\text x+1}+\int \frac{\mathrm{dx}}{(\text x+1)^2}{/tex} + {tex}3 \int \frac{\mathrm{dx}}{\text x+2}{/tex}
= -2 log |x + 1| - {tex}\frac{1}{\text x+1}{/tex} + 3 log |x + 2| + C

//////////

subject: Mathematics
explanation: I = {tex}\int\limits_{\frac \pi 4}^{\frac \pi 2} \text e^{2 \text x}\left(\frac{1-\sin 2 \text x}{1-\cos 2 \text x}\right) \text {dx}{/tex} Put 2x = t so that 2 dx = dt When x = {tex}\frac \pi 2{/tex} , t = {tex}\pi{/tex} ; x = {tex}\frac \pi 4{/tex} , t = {tex}\frac \pi 2{/tex} Thus I = {tex}\int\limits_{\frac \pi 2}^\pi \text e^\text t\left(\frac{1-\sin \mathrm{t}}{1-\cos \text t}\right) \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_{\frac \pi 2}^\pi \text e^\text t\left(\frac{1-2 \sin \frac {\mathrm{t}}{2} \cos \frac {\mathrm{t}}{2}}{2 \sin ^2 \frac t2}\right) \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int\limits_{\frac{\pi}{2}}^\pi \text e^\text t\left(\frac{1}{2} \operatorname{cosec}^2 \frac{t}{2}-\cot \frac{t}{2}\right){/tex} dt = {tex}-\frac{1}{2}\left|\text e^\text t \cot \frac{\text t}{2}\right|_{\frac \pi 2}^\pi{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \text e^{\frac \pi 2}{/tex} . ////////
solution: I = {tex}\int\limits_{\frac \pi 4}^{\frac \pi 2} \text e^{2 \text x}\left(\frac{1-\sin 2 \text x}{1-\cos 2 \text x}\right) \text {dx}{/tex} Put 2x = t so that 2 dx = dt When x = {tex}\frac \pi 2{/tex} , t = {tex}\pi{/tex} ; x = {tex}\frac \pi 4{/tex} , t = {tex}\frac \pi 2{/tex} Thus I = {tex}\int\limits_{\frac \pi 2}^\pi \text e^\text t\left(\frac{1-\sin \mathrm{t}}{1-\cos \text t}\right) \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_{\frac \pi 2}^\pi \text e^\text t\left(\frac{1-2 \sin \frac {\mathrm{t}}{2} \cos \frac {\mathrm{t}}{2}}{2 \sin ^2 \frac t2}\right) \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int\limits_{\frac{\pi}{2}}^\pi \text e^\text t\left(\frac{1}{2} \operatorname{cosec}^2 \frac{t}{2}-\cot \frac{t}{2}\right){/tex} dt = {tex}-\frac{1}{2}\left|\text e^\text t \cot \frac{\text t}{2}\right|_{\frac \pi 2}^\pi{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \text e^{\frac \pi 2}{/tex} . ////////
solution_html:
I = {tex}\int\limits_{\frac \pi 4}^{\frac \pi 2} \text e^{2 \text x}\left(\frac{1-\sin 2 \text x}{1-\cos 2 \text x}\right) \text {dx}{/tex}
Put 2x = t so that 2 dx = dt
When x = {tex}\frac \pi 2{/tex}, t = {tex}\pi{/tex}; x = {tex}\frac \pi 4{/tex}, t = {tex}\frac \pi 2{/tex}
Thus
I = {tex}\int\limits_{\frac \pi 2}^\pi \text e^\text t\left(\frac{1-\sin \mathrm{t}}{1-\cos \text t}\right) \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{2}{/tex}
= {tex}\int\limits_{\frac \pi 2}^\pi \text e^\text t\left(\frac{1-2 \sin \frac {\mathrm{t}}{2} \cos \frac {\mathrm{t}}{2}}{2 \sin ^2 \frac t2}\right) \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int\limits_{\frac{\pi}{2}}^\pi \text e^\text t\left(\frac{1}{2} \operatorname{cosec}^2 \frac{t}{2}-\cot \frac{t}{2}\right){/tex}dt
= {tex}-\frac{1}{2}\left|\text e^\text t \cot \frac{\text t}{2}\right|_{\frac \pi 2}^\pi{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{2} \text e^{\frac \pi 2}{/tex}.

explanation: Let e x = t, so that e x dx = dt. Then, I = {tex}\int \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\sqrt{5-4 \mathrm{t}-\mathrm{t}^2}}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{\text {dt}}{\sqrt{3^2-(t+2)^2}}{/tex} = {tex}\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{\text t+2}{3}\right)+\text C{/tex} = {tex}\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{\text e^\text x+2}{3}\right)+\text C{/tex} ///////////
solution: Let e x = t, so that e x dx = dt. Then, I = {tex}\int \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\sqrt{5-4 \mathrm{t}-\mathrm{t}^2}}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{\text {dt}}{\sqrt{3^2-(t+2)^2}}{/tex} = {tex}\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{\text t+2}{3}\right)+\text C{/tex} = {tex}\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{\text e^\text x+2}{3}\right)+\text C{/tex} ///////////
solution_html:
Let ex = t, so that ex dx = dt. Then,
I = {tex}\int \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\sqrt{5-4 \mathrm{t}-\mathrm{t}^2}}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{\text {dt}}{\sqrt{3^2-(t+2)^2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{\text t+2}{3}\right)+\text C{/tex}
= {tex}\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{\text e^\text x+2}{3}\right)+\text C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{1}{1+\mathrm{e}^{\sin \mathrm{x}}} \mathrm {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{1}{1+\mathrm{e}^{\sin (2 \pi-\mathrm{x})}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{1}{1+e^{-\sin x}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{e^{\sin \text x}}{e^{\sin \text x}+1} \text {dx}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{e^{\sin \text x}+1}{e^{\sin \text x}+1} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{2 \pi}{/tex} 1 {tex}\cdot{/tex} dx = {tex}2\pi{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\pi{/tex} //////////
solution: Let I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{1}{1+\mathrm{e}^{\sin \mathrm{x}}} \mathrm {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{1}{1+\mathrm{e}^{\sin (2 \pi-\mathrm{x})}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{1}{1+e^{-\sin x}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{e^{\sin \text x}}{e^{\sin \text x}+1} \text {dx}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{e^{\sin \text x}+1}{e^{\sin \text x}+1} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{2 \pi}{/tex} 1 {tex}\cdot{/tex} dx = {tex}2\pi{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\pi{/tex} //////////
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{1}{1+\mathrm{e}^{\sin \mathrm{x}}} \mathrm {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{1}{1+\mathrm{e}^{\sin (2 \pi-\mathrm{x})}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex}
= {tex}\int\limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{1}{1+e^{-\sin x}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{e^{\sin \text x}}{e^{\sin \text x}+1} \text {dx}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{e^{\sin \text x}+1}{e^{\sin \text x}+1} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{2 \pi}{/tex}1{tex}\cdot{/tex} dx = {tex}2\pi{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\pi{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate: {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex} [log (sin x) - log (2 cos x)] dx.
explanation: Let I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} [log sin x - log (2 cos x)] dx = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} log {tex}\left(\frac{\tan \text x}{2}\right){/tex} dx Using property {tex}\int\limits_0^a{/tex} f(x) dx = {tex}\int\limits_0^a{/tex} f(a - x)dx We get, I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} log {tex}\left(\frac{\cot \mathrm{x}}{2}\right){/tex} dx {tex}\therefore{/tex} 2I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} log {tex}\left(\frac{\tan \mathrm{x}}{2} \times \frac{\cot \mathrm{x}}{2}\right){/tex} dx = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} log {tex}\left(\frac{1}{4}\right){/tex} dx 2I = {tex}\log \left(\frac{1}{4}\right)\mathrm{x} \lvert^{\frac \pi 2}_0{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2} \log \frac{1}{4}{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} log {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} OR {tex}-\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} log 2 /////////
solution: Let I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} [log sin x - log (2 cos x)] dx = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} log {tex}\left(\frac{\tan \text x}{2}\right){/tex} dx Using property {tex}\int\limits_0^a{/tex} f(x) dx = {tex}\int\limits_0^a{/tex} f(a - x)dx We get, I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} log {tex}\left(\frac{\cot \mathrm{x}}{2}\right){/tex} dx {tex}\therefore{/tex} 2I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} log {tex}\left(\frac{\tan \mathrm{x}}{2} \times \frac{\cot \mathrm{x}}{2}\right){/tex} dx = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} log {tex}\left(\frac{1}{4}\right){/tex} dx 2I = {tex}\log \left(\frac{1}{4}\right)\mathrm{x} \lvert^{\frac \pi 2}_0{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2} \log \frac{1}{4}{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} log {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} OR {tex}-\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} log 2 /////////
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex}[log sin x - log (2 cos x)] dx = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex}log {tex}\left(\frac{\tan \text x}{2}\right){/tex}dx
Using property {tex}\int\limits_0^a{/tex} f(x) dx = {tex}\int\limits_0^a{/tex} f(a - x)dx
We get, I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex}log {tex}\left(\frac{\cot \mathrm{x}}{2}\right){/tex}dx
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 2I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex}log {tex}\left(\frac{\tan \mathrm{x}}{2} \times \frac{\cot \mathrm{x}}{2}\right){/tex}dx = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex}log {tex}\left(\frac{1}{4}\right){/tex}dx
2I = {tex}\log \left(\frac{1}{4}\right)\mathrm{x} \lvert^{\frac \pi 2}_0{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2} \log \frac{1}{4}{/tex}
I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} log {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} OR {tex}-\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} log 2
/////////

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate: {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex} e x sin x dx
explanation: Let I = {tex}\int{/tex} e x sin x dx = e x sin x - {tex}\int{/tex} cos x e x dx = e x sin x - cos x e x - I {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\text I}{2} {/tex} e x (sin x - cos x) {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} e x sin x dx = {tex}\frac{1}{2} e^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} or {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} ( {tex}e^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} + 1) ////////
solution: Let I = {tex}\int{/tex} e x sin x dx = e x sin x - {tex}\int{/tex} cos x e x dx = e x sin x - cos x e x - I {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\text I}{2} {/tex} e x (sin x - cos x) {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} e x sin x dx = {tex}\frac{1}{2} e^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} or {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} ( {tex}e^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} + 1) ////////
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int{/tex}ex sin x dx
= ex sin x -{tex}\int{/tex} cos x ex dx
= ex sin x - cos x ex - I
{tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\text I}{2} {/tex}ex(sin x - cos x)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex}ex sin x dx = {tex}\frac{1}{2} e^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} or {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}({tex}e^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} + 1)
////////

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex} |sin x - cox x| dx = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex} (cos x - sin x)dx + {tex}\int\limits_{\frac {\pi}{4}}^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} (sin x - cos x)dx = (cos x + sin x) {tex}|_0 ^{\frac {\pi}{4}}{/tex} + (- cos x - sin x) {tex}|_{\frac {\pi}{4}} ^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} = ( {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} - 1) -1 + {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} = {tex}2 \sqrt{2}{/tex} - 2 //////////
solution: Let I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex} |sin x - cox x| dx = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex} (cos x - sin x)dx + {tex}\int\limits_{\frac {\pi}{4}}^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} (sin x - cos x)dx = (cos x + sin x) {tex}|_0 ^{\frac {\pi}{4}}{/tex} + (- cos x - sin x) {tex}|_{\frac {\pi}{4}} ^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex} = ( {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} - 1) -1 + {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} = {tex}2 \sqrt{2}{/tex} - 2 //////////
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex} |sin x - cox x| dx
= {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex}(cos x - sin x)dx + {tex}\int\limits_{\frac {\pi}{4}}^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex}(sin x - cos x)dx
= (cos x + sin x){tex}|_0 ^{\frac {\pi}{4}}{/tex} + (- cos x - sin x){tex}|_{\frac {\pi}{4}} ^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex}
= ({tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} - 1) -1 + {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex}
= {tex}2 \sqrt{2}{/tex} - 2
//////////

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let I = {tex}\int \frac{\text {dx}}{\sqrt{\text x}(\sqrt{\text x}+1)(\sqrt{\text x}+2)}{/tex} Put {tex} \sqrt{x}{/tex} = t {tex}\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{\text x}}{/tex} dx = dt {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}2\int \frac{\text {dt}}{(\text t+1)(\text t+2)}{/tex} {tex} \frac{\text {1}}{(\text t+1)(\text t+2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{A}{t+1}+\frac{B}{t+2}{/tex} 1 = A (t+2) + B(t+1) put t=-1, 1= A(1) {tex}\implies{/tex} A = 1 Put t=-2, 1 = B(2) {tex}\implies{/tex} B=-2 {tex} \frac{\text {1}}{(\text t+1)(\text t+2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{t+1}-\frac{1}{t+2}{/tex} I= {tex}2\int\left(\frac{1}{\text t+1}-\frac{1}{\text t+2}\right){/tex} dt = {tex}2[\int\frac{dt}{\text t+1}-\int\frac{dt}{\text t+2}]{/tex} = 2[log |t + 1| - log |t + 2|] + C = [log {tex}(\sqrt {\text x} + 1){/tex} – log {tex}(\sqrt {\text x} + 2){/tex} ] + C or 2 log {tex}\left(\frac{\sqrt{\text x}+1}{\sqrt{\text x}+2}\right){/tex} + C
solution: Let I = {tex}\int \frac{\text {dx}}{\sqrt{\text x}(\sqrt{\text x}+1)(\sqrt{\text x}+2)}{/tex} Put {tex} \sqrt{x}{/tex} = t {tex}\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{\text x}}{/tex} dx = dt {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}2\int \frac{\text {dt}}{(\text t+1)(\text t+2)}{/tex} {tex} \frac{\text {1}}{(\text t+1)(\text t+2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{A}{t+1}+\frac{B}{t+2}{/tex} 1 = A (t+2) + B(t+1) put t=-1, 1= A(1) {tex}\implies{/tex} A = 1 Put t=-2, 1 = B(2) {tex}\implies{/tex} B=-2 {tex} \frac{\text {1}}{(\text t+1)(\text t+2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{t+1}-\frac{1}{t+2}{/tex} I= {tex}2\int\left(\frac{1}{\text t+1}-\frac{1}{\text t+2}\right){/tex} dt = {tex}2[\int\frac{dt}{\text t+1}-\int\frac{dt}{\text t+2}]{/tex} = 2[log |t + 1| - log |t + 2|] + C = [log {tex}(\sqrt {\text x} + 1){/tex} – log {tex}(\sqrt {\text x} + 2){/tex} ] + C or 2 log {tex}\left(\frac{\sqrt{\text x}+1}{\sqrt{\text x}+2}\right){/tex} + C
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int \frac{\text {dx}}{\sqrt{\text x}(\sqrt{\text x}+1)(\sqrt{\text x}+2)}{/tex}

Put {tex} \sqrt{x}{/tex} = t 
{tex}\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{\text x}}{/tex}dx = dt
{tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}2\int \frac{\text {dt}}{(\text t+1)(\text t+2)}{/tex}

 {tex} \frac{\text {1}}{(\text t+1)(\text t+2)}{/tex}= {tex}\frac{A}{t+1}+\frac{B}{t+2}{/tex} 
1 = A (t+2) + B(t+1)
put t=-1, 1= A(1) {tex}\implies{/tex}A = 1
Put t=-2, 1 = B(2) {tex}\implies{/tex} B=-2 
{tex} \frac{\text {1}}{(\text t+1)(\text t+2)}{/tex}  = {tex}\frac{1}{t+1}-\frac{1}{t+2}{/tex}

I= {tex}2\int\left(\frac{1}{\text t+1}-\frac{1}{\text t+2}\right){/tex}dt
= {tex}2[\int\frac{dt}{\text t+1}-\int\frac{dt}{\text t+2}]{/tex}
= 2[log |t + 1| - log |t + 2|] + C
= [log {tex}(\sqrt {\text x} + 1){/tex} – log {tex}(\sqrt {\text x} + 2){/tex}] + C
or 2 log {tex}\left(\frac{\sqrt{\text x}+1}{\sqrt{\text x}+2}\right){/tex} + C

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let I = {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\sin ^3 \text x \cos (\text x-\alpha)}} \text {dx}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\frac{\sin ^4 \text x} {sin x}\cos (\text x-\alpha)}} \text {dx}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sin ^2 \text x\sqrt{ co\cossec(\text x-\alpha)}} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{\operatorname{cosec}^2 \text x}{\sqrt{\sin \alpha+\cos \alpha \cot x}} \text {dx}{/tex} Put sin {tex}\alpha{/tex} + cos {tex}\alpha{/tex} cot x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} cosec 2 xdx = {tex}-\frac{1}{\cos \alpha} \text {dt}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}-\int \frac{1}{\cos \alpha \sqrt{\text t}} \text {dt}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{2 \sqrt{t}}{\cos \alpha}+\text c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}-\frac{2}{\cos \alpha} \sqrt{\sin \alpha+\cos \alpha \cot \text x}+\text c{/tex}
solution: Let I = {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\sin ^3 \text x \cos (\text x-\alpha)}} \text {dx}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\frac{\sin ^4 \text x} {sin x}\cos (\text x-\alpha)}} \text {dx}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sin ^2 \text x\sqrt{ co\cossec(\text x-\alpha)}} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{\operatorname{cosec}^2 \text x}{\sqrt{\sin \alpha+\cos \alpha \cot x}} \text {dx}{/tex} Put sin {tex}\alpha{/tex} + cos {tex}\alpha{/tex} cot x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} cosec 2 xdx = {tex}-\frac{1}{\cos \alpha} \text {dt}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}-\int \frac{1}{\cos \alpha \sqrt{\text t}} \text {dt}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{2 \sqrt{t}}{\cos \alpha}+\text c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}-\frac{2}{\cos \alpha} \sqrt{\sin \alpha+\cos \alpha \cot \text x}+\text c{/tex}
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\sin ^3 \text x \cos (\text x-\alpha)}} \text {dx}{/tex}

{tex}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\frac{\sin ^4 \text x} {sin x}\cos (\text x-\alpha)}} \text {dx}{/tex}
{tex}\int \frac{1}{\sin ^2 \text x\sqrt{ co\cossec(\text x-\alpha)}} \text {dx}{/tex}

= {tex}\int \frac{\operatorname{cosec}^2 \text x}{\sqrt{\sin \alpha+\cos \alpha \cot x}} \text {dx}{/tex}
Put sin {tex}\alpha{/tex} + cos {tex}\alpha{/tex}cot x = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} cosec2 xdx = {tex}-\frac{1}{\cos \alpha} \text {dt}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}-\int \frac{1}{\cos \alpha \sqrt{\text t}} \text {dt}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{2 \sqrt{t}}{\cos \alpha}+\text c{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}-\frac{2}{\cos \alpha} \sqrt{\sin \alpha+\cos \alpha \cot \text x}+\text c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find: {tex}\int \frac{\text e^\text x}{\sqrt{\text e^{2 \text x}-4 \text e^\text x-5}} \text {dx}{/tex}
question_text: Find: {tex}\int \frac{\text e^\text x}{\sqrt{\text e^{2 \text x}-4 \text e^\text x-5}} \text {dx}{/tex}
explanation: Let e x = t. Then e x dx = dt Given integral becomes {tex}\int \frac{\text {dt}}{\sqrt{t^2-4 t-5}}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{(t-2)^2-3^2}}{/tex} = log |(t - 2) + {tex}\sqrt{t^2-4 \mathrm{t}-5}{/tex} | + C = log |e x - 2 + {tex}\sqrt{\mathrm{e}^{2 x}-4 \mathrm{e}^x-5}{/tex} | + C
solution: Let e x = t. Then e x dx = dt Given integral becomes {tex}\int \frac{\text {dt}}{\sqrt{t^2-4 t-5}}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{(t-2)^2-3^2}}{/tex} = log |(t - 2) + {tex}\sqrt{t^2-4 \mathrm{t}-5}{/tex} | + C = log |e x - 2 + {tex}\sqrt{\mathrm{e}^{2 x}-4 \mathrm{e}^x-5}{/tex} | + C
solution_html:
Let ex = t. Then exdx = dt
Given integral becomes
{tex}\int \frac{\text {dt}}{\sqrt{t^2-4 t-5}}{/tex}
= {tex}\int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{(t-2)^2-3^2}}{/tex}
= log |(t - 2) + {tex}\sqrt{t^2-4 \mathrm{t}-5}{/tex}| + C
= log |ex - 2 + {tex}\sqrt{\mathrm{e}^{2 x}-4 \mathrm{e}^x-5}{/tex}| + C

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let {tex}\frac{2}{(1-\text x)\left(1+\text x^2\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text A}{1-\text x}+\frac{\text B \text x+\text C}{1+\text x^2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = 1, B = 1, C = 1 Hence, I = {tex}\int \frac{2}{(1-\text x)\left(1-\text x^2\right)} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\left[\frac{1}{1-\text x}+\frac{\text x+1}{1+\text x^2}\right] \mathrm{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{1-\text x} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} + {tex}\int \frac{\text x+1}{\text x^2+1} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{1-\text x} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 \text x}{\text x^2+1} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} + {tex}\int \frac{1}{\text x^2+1} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} = -log |1 - x| + {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} log (x 2 + 1) + tan -1 (x) + C
solution: Let {tex}\frac{2}{(1-\text x)\left(1+\text x^2\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text A}{1-\text x}+\frac{\text B \text x+\text C}{1+\text x^2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = 1, B = 1, C = 1 Hence, I = {tex}\int \frac{2}{(1-\text x)\left(1-\text x^2\right)} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\left[\frac{1}{1-\text x}+\frac{\text x+1}{1+\text x^2}\right] \mathrm{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{1-\text x} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} + {tex}\int \frac{\text x+1}{\text x^2+1} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{1-\text x} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 \text x}{\text x^2+1} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} + {tex}\int \frac{1}{\text x^2+1} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} = -log |1 - x| + {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} log (x 2 + 1) + tan -1 (x) + C
solution_html:
Let {tex}\frac{2}{(1-\text x)\left(1+\text x^2\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text A}{1-\text x}+\frac{\text B \text x+\text C}{1+\text x^2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = 1, B = 1, C = 1
Hence, I = {tex}\int \frac{2}{(1-\text x)\left(1-\text x^2\right)} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\left[\frac{1}{1-\text x}+\frac{\text x+1}{1+\text x^2}\right] \mathrm{dx}{/tex}
= {tex}\int \frac{1}{1-\text x} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} + {tex}\int \frac{\text x+1}{\text x^2+1} \mathrm{dx}{/tex}
= {tex}\int \frac{1}{1-\text x} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 \text x}{\text x^2+1} \mathrm{dx}{/tex} + {tex}\int \frac{1}{\text x^2+1} \mathrm{dx}{/tex}
= -log |1 - x| + {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}log (x2 + 1) + tan-1(x) + C

subject: Mathematics
explanation: I = {tex}\int \frac{2\text x^2+1}{\text x^2\left(\text x^2+4\right)}\text {dx}{/tex} Let {tex}\frac{2 \text x^2+1}{\text x^2\left(\text x^2+4\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2\text y+1}{\text y(\text y+4)}{/tex} , where x 2 = y Put {tex}\frac{2\text y+1}{\text y(\text y+4)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text A}{\text y}+\frac{\text B}{\text y+4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2y + 1 = A(y + 4) + By {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} , B = {tex}\frac{7}{4}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{2 \text y+1}{\text y(\text y+4)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4 y}+\frac{7}{4(y+4)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4 x^2}+\frac{7}{4\left(x^2+4\right)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \int \frac{1}{x^2} \text {dx}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{7}{4} \int \frac{1}{x^2+4} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{4 \text x}+\frac{7}{8}{/tex} tan -1 {tex}\left(\frac{\text x}{2}\right)+\text C{/tex}
solution: I = {tex}\int \frac{2\text x^2+1}{\text x^2\left(\text x^2+4\right)}\text {dx}{/tex} Let {tex}\frac{2 \text x^2+1}{\text x^2\left(\text x^2+4\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2\text y+1}{\text y(\text y+4)}{/tex} , where x 2 = y Put {tex}\frac{2\text y+1}{\text y(\text y+4)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text A}{\text y}+\frac{\text B}{\text y+4}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2y + 1 = A(y + 4) + By {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} , B = {tex}\frac{7}{4}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{2 \text y+1}{\text y(\text y+4)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4 y}+\frac{7}{4(y+4)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4 x^2}+\frac{7}{4\left(x^2+4\right)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \int \frac{1}{x^2} \text {dx}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{7}{4} \int \frac{1}{x^2+4} \text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{4 \text x}+\frac{7}{8}{/tex} tan -1 {tex}\left(\frac{\text x}{2}\right)+\text C{/tex}
solution_html:
I = {tex}\int \frac{2\text x^2+1}{\text x^2\left(\text x^2+4\right)}\text {dx}{/tex}
Let {tex}\frac{2 \text x^2+1}{\text x^2\left(\text x^2+4\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2\text y+1}{\text y(\text y+4)}{/tex}, where x2 = y
Put {tex}\frac{2\text y+1}{\text y(\text y+4)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text A}{\text y}+\frac{\text B}{\text y+4}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2y + 1 = A(y + 4) + By
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A = {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex}, B = {tex}\frac{7}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{2 \text y+1}{\text y(\text y+4)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4 y}+\frac{7}{4(y+4)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4 x^2}+\frac{7}{4\left(x^2+4\right)}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \int \frac{1}{x^2} \text {dx}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{7}{4} \int \frac{1}{x^2+4} \text {dx}{/tex}
= {tex}-\frac{1}{4 \text x}+\frac{7}{8}{/tex}tan-1 {tex}\left(\frac{\text x}{2}\right)+\text C{/tex}

solution: I = {tex}\int \frac{\text x}{\left(\text x^2+1\right)(\text x-1)}\text {dx}{/tex} Let {tex}\frac{\text x}{\left(\text x^2+1\right)(\text x-1)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text {Ax}+\text B}{\text x^2+1}+\frac{\text C}{\text x-1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = (x - 1)(Ax + B) + C(x 2 + 1) This gives A = {tex}-\frac{1}{2}{/tex} , B = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} , C = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}-\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{\text x-1}{\text x^2+1}\text {dx}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{\text x-1}\text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{2}\left\{\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2\text x}{\text x^2+1}\text {dx}-\int \frac{1}{\text x^2+1}\text {dx}\right\}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{1}{\text x-1}\text {dx}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{4}{/tex} log (x 2 + 1) + {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} tan -1 x + {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} log |x - 1| + C
solution_html:
I = {tex}\int \frac{\text x}{\left(\text x^2+1\right)(\text x-1)}\text {dx}{/tex}
Let {tex}\frac{\text x}{\left(\text x^2+1\right)(\text x-1)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text {Ax}+\text B}{\text x^2+1}+\frac{\text C}{\text x-1}{/tex}
