= {tex}-\frac{1}{4}{/tex}log (x2 + 1) + {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}tan-1x + {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}log |x - 1| + C

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Given {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} ....(i) {tex} \therefore \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \pi - x } { 1 + \sin ( \pi - x ) } d x{/tex} {tex} \left[ \because \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( x ) d x = \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( a - x ) d x \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \pi - x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} .....(ii) {tex} [ \because \sin ( \pi - x ) = \sin x ]{/tex} Adding Equations (i) and (ii) we get , {tex} 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x + \pi - x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 } { 1 + \sin x } \times \frac { 1 - \sin x } { 1 - \sin x } d x{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 - \sin x } { 1 - \sin ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 - \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}[cos^2x+sin^2x=1 \implies cos^2x=1-sin^2x ]{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \left( \sec ^ { 2 } x - \tan x \sec x \right) d x{/tex} {tex} = \pi [ \tan x - \sec x ] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi }{/tex} {tex} = \pi [ ( \tan \pi - \sec \pi ) - ( \tan 0 - \sec 0 ) ]{/tex} {tex} = \pi{/tex} [ (0 - (-1) - (0 - 1)] {tex} = \pi ( 1 + 1 ){/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 2 I = 2 \pi \quad \Rightarrow I = \pi{/tex}
solution: Given {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} ....(i) {tex} \therefore \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \pi - x } { 1 + \sin ( \pi - x ) } d x{/tex} {tex} \left[ \because \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( x ) d x = \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( a - x ) d x \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \pi - x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} .....(ii) {tex} [ \because \sin ( \pi - x ) = \sin x ]{/tex} Adding Equations (i) and (ii) we get , {tex} 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x + \pi - x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 } { 1 + \sin x } \times \frac { 1 - \sin x } { 1 - \sin x } d x{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 - \sin x } { 1 - \sin ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 - \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}[cos^2x+sin^2x=1 \implies cos^2x=1-sin^2x ]{/tex} {tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \left( \sec ^ { 2 } x - \tan x \sec x \right) d x{/tex} {tex} = \pi [ \tan x - \sec x ] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi }{/tex} {tex} = \pi [ ( \tan \pi - \sec \pi ) - ( \tan 0 - \sec 0 ) ]{/tex} {tex} = \pi{/tex} [ (0 - (-1) - (0 - 1)] {tex} = \pi ( 1 + 1 ){/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 2 I = 2 \pi \quad \Rightarrow I = \pi{/tex}
solution_html:
Given {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} ....(i)
{tex} \therefore \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \pi - x } { 1 + \sin ( \pi - x ) } d x{/tex}{tex} \left[ \because \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( x ) d x = \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( a - x ) d x \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \pi - x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} .....(ii){tex} [ \because \sin ( \pi - x ) = \sin x ]{/tex}
Adding Equations (i) and (ii) we get ,
{tex} 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x + \pi - x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 } { 1 + \sin x } \times \frac { 1 - \sin x } { 1 - \sin x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 - \sin x } { 1 - \sin ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 - \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}[cos^2x+sin^2x=1 \implies cos^2x=1-sin^2x ]{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \left( \sec ^ { 2 } x - \tan x \sec x \right) d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi [ \tan x - \sec x ] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi }{/tex}
{tex} = \pi [ ( \tan \pi - \sec \pi ) - ( \tan 0 - \sec 0 ) ]{/tex}
{tex} = \pi{/tex} [ (0 - (-1) -  (0 - 1)]
{tex} = \pi ( 1 + 1 ){/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 2 I = 2 \pi \quad \Rightarrow I = \pi{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}\text { Let } I=\int_{0}^{1} x(1-x)^{n} d x \text { . Then, }{/tex} we have {tex}I=\int_{0}^{1}(1-x)\left\{1-\left.(1-x)\right|^{n} d x\right.{/tex} {tex}\left[\because \int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\int_{0}^{1}(1-x) x^{n} d x=\int_{0}^{1}\left(x^{n}-x^{n+1}\right) d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\left[\frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1}-\frac{x^{n+2}}{n+2}\right]_{0}^{1}=\left\{\frac{1}{n+1}-\frac{1}{n+2}\right\}-(0-0)=\frac{1}{(n+1)(n+2)}{/tex}
solution: {tex}\text { Let } I=\int_{0}^{1} x(1-x)^{n} d x \text { . Then, }{/tex} we have {tex}I=\int_{0}^{1}(1-x)\left\{1-\left.(1-x)\right|^{n} d x\right.{/tex} {tex}\left[\because \int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\int_{0}^{1}(1-x) x^{n} d x=\int_{0}^{1}\left(x^{n}-x^{n+1}\right) d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\left[\frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1}-\frac{x^{n+2}}{n+2}\right]_{0}^{1}=\left\{\frac{1}{n+1}-\frac{1}{n+2}\right\}-(0-0)=\frac{1}{(n+1)(n+2)}{/tex}

{tex}\text { Let } I=\int_{0}^{1} x(1-x)^{n} d x \text { . Then, }{/tex}we have
{tex}I=\int_{0}^{1}(1-x)\left\{1-\left.(1-x)\right|^{n} d x\right.{/tex}{tex}\left[\because \int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: We have I = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}+x}{x^{4}-9} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}}{x^{4}-9} d x+\frac{x d x}{x^{4}-9}{/tex} = I 1 + I 2 Now I 1 = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}}{x^{4}-9}{/tex} Put t = x 4 - 9 so that 4x 3 dx = dt. Therefore I 1 = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \int \frac{d t}{t}=\frac{1}{4} \log |t|+\mathrm{C}_{1}=\frac{1}{4} \log \left|x^{4}-9\right|+\mathrm{C}_{1}{/tex} Again, I 2 = {tex}\int \frac{x d x}{x^{4}-9}{/tex} Put x 2 = u so that 2x dx = du. Then I 2 = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{d u}{u^{2}-(3)^{2}}=\frac{1}{2 \times 6} \log \left|\frac{u-3}{u+3}\right|+\mathrm{C_2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{12} \log \left|\frac{x^{2}-3}{x^{2}+3}\right|+C_{2}{/tex} Thus I = I 1 + I 2 = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log \left|x^{4}-9\right|+\frac{1}{12} \log \left|\frac{x^{2}-3}{x^{2}+3}\right|+C{/tex}
solution: We have I = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}+x}{x^{4}-9} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}}{x^{4}-9} d x+\frac{x d x}{x^{4}-9}{/tex} = I 1 + I 2 Now I 1 = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}}{x^{4}-9}{/tex} Put t = x 4 - 9 so that 4x 3 dx = dt. Therefore I 1 = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \int \frac{d t}{t}=\frac{1}{4} \log |t|+\mathrm{C}_{1}=\frac{1}{4} \log \left|x^{4}-9\right|+\mathrm{C}_{1}{/tex} Again, I 2 = {tex}\int \frac{x d x}{x^{4}-9}{/tex} Put x 2 = u so that 2x dx = du. Then I 2 = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{d u}{u^{2}-(3)^{2}}=\frac{1}{2 \times 6} \log \left|\frac{u-3}{u+3}\right|+\mathrm{C_2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{12} \log \left|\frac{x^{2}-3}{x^{2}+3}\right|+C_{2}{/tex} Thus I = I 1 + I 2 = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log \left|x^{4}-9\right|+\frac{1}{12} \log \left|\frac{x^{2}-3}{x^{2}+3}\right|+C{/tex}
solution_html:
We have
I = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}+x}{x^{4}-9} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}}{x^{4}-9} d x+\frac{x d x}{x^{4}-9}{/tex} = I1 + I2 
Now I1 = {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}}{x^{4}-9}{/tex} 
Put t = x4 - 9 so that 4x3 dx = dt. Therefore
I1 = {tex}\frac{1}{4} \int \frac{d t}{t}=\frac{1}{4} \log |t|+\mathrm{C}_{1}=\frac{1}{4} \log \left|x^{4}-9\right|+\mathrm{C}_{1}{/tex} 
Again, I2 = {tex}\int \frac{x d x}{x^{4}-9}{/tex} 
Put x2 = u so that 2x dx = du. Then
I2 = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{d u}{u^{2}-(3)^{2}}=\frac{1}{2 \times 6} \log \left|\frac{u-3}{u+3}\right|+\mathrm{C_2}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{12} \log \left|\frac{x^{2}-3}{x^{2}+3}\right|+C_{2}{/tex} 
Thus I = I1 + I2
= {tex}\frac{1}{4} \log \left|x^{4}-9\right|+\frac{1}{12} \log \left|\frac{x^{2}-3}{x^{2}+3}\right|+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: We have x 2 - 6x + 13 = x 2 - 6x + 3 2 - 3 2 + 13 = (x - 3) 2 + 4 So, {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}-6 x+13}=\int \frac{1}{(x-3)^{2}+2^{2}} d x{/tex} Let x – 3 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = dt Therefore, {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}-6 x+13}=\int \frac{d t}{t^{2}+2^{2}}=\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{t}{2}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x-3}{2}+C{/tex}
solution: We have x 2 - 6x + 13 = x 2 - 6x + 3 2 - 3 2 + 13 = (x - 3) 2 + 4 So, {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}-6 x+13}=\int \frac{1}{(x-3)^{2}+2^{2}} d x{/tex} Let x – 3 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = dt Therefore, {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}-6 x+13}=\int \frac{d t}{t^{2}+2^{2}}=\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{t}{2}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x-3}{2}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
We have x2 - 6x + 13 = x2 - 6x + 32 - 32 + 13 = (x - 3)2 + 4
So, {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}-6 x+13}=\int \frac{1}{(x-3)^{2}+2^{2}} d x{/tex} 
Let x – 3 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = dt
Therefore, 
{tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{2}-6 x+13}=\int \frac{d t}{t^{2}+2^{2}}=\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{t}{2}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} 
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x-3}{2}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: We will solve this integral by partial fraction Let {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(3 x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{A x+B}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\frac{C x+D}{\left(3 x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 = (Ax + B) (3x 2 + 4) + (Cx + D) (x 2 + 1) = (3A + C)x 3 + (3B + D) x 2 + (4A + C)x + 4B + D Equating similar terms, we get, 3A + C = 0, 3B + D = 1, 4A + C = 0, 4B + D = 0 Solving, we get, A = 0, B = -1, C = 0, D = 4 Thus, I = {tex}\int \frac{-d x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\int \frac{4 d x}{\left(3 x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} = - tan -1 x + {tex}\frac{4}{3} \cdot \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3} x}{2}\right)+c{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3} x}{2}\right){/tex} - tan -1 x + c
solution: We will solve this integral by partial fraction Let {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(3 x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{A x+B}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\frac{C x+D}{\left(3 x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 = (Ax + B) (3x 2 + 4) + (Cx + D) (x 2 + 1) = (3A + C)x 3 + (3B + D) x 2 + (4A + C)x + 4B + D Equating similar terms, we get, 3A + C = 0, 3B + D = 1, 4A + C = 0, 4B + D = 0 Solving, we get, A = 0, B = -1, C = 0, D = 4 Thus, I = {tex}\int \frac{-d x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\int \frac{4 d x}{\left(3 x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} = - tan -1 x + {tex}\frac{4}{3} \cdot \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3} x}{2}\right)+c{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3} x}{2}\right){/tex} - tan -1 x + c
solution_html:
We will solve this integral by partial fraction
Let{tex}\frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(3 x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{A x+B}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\frac{C x+D}{\left(3 x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 = (Ax + B) (3x2 + 4) + (Cx + D) (x2 + 1)
= (3A + C)x3 + (3B + D) x2 + (4A + C)x + 4B + D
Equating similar terms, we get,
3A + C = 0, 3B + D = 1, 4A + C = 0, 4B + D = 0
Solving, we get, A = 0, B = -1, C = 0, D = 4
Thus,
I = {tex}\int \frac{-d x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\int \frac{4 d x}{\left(3 x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex}
= - tan-1x + {tex}\frac{4}{3} \cdot \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3} x}{2}\right)+c{/tex}
I = {tex}\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\sqrt{3} x}{2}\right){/tex}- tan-1x + c

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let {tex}I = \int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } + 1 } d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } \left[ 1 + \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } } \right] } d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I = \int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } \left[ 1 + e ^ { - x } \right] } d x{/tex} ...(i) Let 1 + e -x = t then, d(1 + e -x ) = dt {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - e -x dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = {tex}\frac { - d t } { e ^ { - x } }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = - e x dt Putting 1 + e -x = t and dx = - e x dt in equation (i), we get, {tex}I = \int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } \times t } \times - e ^ { x } d t{/tex} {tex}= - \int \frac { d t } { t }{/tex} = - log |t| + c = - log |1 + e -x | + c = - log |1 + e -x | + c
solution: Let {tex}I = \int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } + 1 } d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } \left[ 1 + \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } } \right] } d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I = \int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } \left[ 1 + e ^ { - x } \right] } d x{/tex} ...(i) Let 1 + e -x = t then, d(1 + e -x ) = dt {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - e -x dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = {tex}\frac { - d t } { e ^ { - x } }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = - e x dt Putting 1 + e -x = t and dx = - e x dt in equation (i), we get, {tex}I = \int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } \times t } \times - e ^ { x } d t{/tex} {tex}= - \int \frac { d t } { t }{/tex} = - log |t| + c = - log |1 + e -x | + c = - log |1 + e -x | + c
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } + 1 } d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } \left[ 1 + \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } } \right] } d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I = \int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } \left[ 1 + e ^ { - x } \right] } d x{/tex} ...(i)
Let 1 + e-x = t then,
d(1 + e-x) = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - e-x dx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = {tex}\frac { - d t } { e ^ { - x } }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}dx = - ex dt
Putting 1 + e-x = t and dx = - ex dt in equation (i), we get,
{tex}I = \int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } \times t } \times - e ^ { x } d t{/tex}
{tex}= - \int \frac { d t } { t }{/tex}
= - log |t| + c
= - log |1 + e-x| + c
= - log |1 + e-x| + c

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let I = {tex}\int \frac{2 x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+2\right)} d x{/tex} . Put, x 2 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x dx = dt, we obtain I = {tex}\int \frac{d t}{(t+1)(t+2)}{/tex} Using partial fractions, {tex}\frac{1}{(t+1)(t+2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{A}{t+1}+\frac{B}{t+2}{/tex} ........(i) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 = A(t + 2) + B(t + 1) ........(ii) Putting t = - 2 in (ii), we obtain: B = - 1 Putting t = - 1 in (ii), we obtain: A = 1. Substituting the values of A and B in (i), we obtain {tex}\frac{1}{(t+1)(t+2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{t+1}-\frac{1}{t+2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\int \frac{1}{(t+1)(t+2)} d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int\left(\frac{1}{t+1}-\frac{1}{t+2}\right) d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = log |t + 1| - log |t + 2| + C = log |x 2 + 1| - log |x 2 + 2| + C
solution: Let I = {tex}\int \frac{2 x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+2\right)} d x{/tex} . Put, x 2 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x dx = dt, we obtain I = {tex}\int \frac{d t}{(t+1)(t+2)}{/tex} Using partial fractions, {tex}\frac{1}{(t+1)(t+2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{A}{t+1}+\frac{B}{t+2}{/tex} ........(i) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 = A(t + 2) + B(t + 1) ........(ii) Putting t = - 2 in (ii), we obtain: B = - 1 Putting t = - 1 in (ii), we obtain: A = 1. Substituting the values of A and B in (i), we obtain {tex}\frac{1}{(t+1)(t+2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{t+1}-\frac{1}{t+2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\int \frac{1}{(t+1)(t+2)} d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int\left(\frac{1}{t+1}-\frac{1}{t+2}\right) d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = log |t + 1| - log |t + 2| + C = log |x 2 + 1| - log |x 2 + 2| + C
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int \frac{2 x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+2\right)} d x{/tex}.
Put, x2 = t  {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x dx = dt, we obtain
I = {tex}\int \frac{d t}{(t+1)(t+2)}{/tex} 
Using partial fractions,
 {tex}\frac{1}{(t+1)(t+2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{A}{t+1}+\frac{B}{t+2}{/tex} ........(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 = A(t + 2) + B(t + 1) ........(ii)
Putting t = - 2 in (ii), we obtain: B = - 1
Putting t = - 1 in (ii), we obtain: A = 1.
Substituting the values of A and B in (i), we obtain
{tex}\frac{1}{(t+1)(t+2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{t+1}-\frac{1}{t+2}{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex}   I = {tex}\int \frac{1}{(t+1)(t+2)} d t{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  I = {tex}\int\left(\frac{1}{t+1}-\frac{1}{t+2}\right) d t{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  I = log |t + 1| - log |t + 2| + C = log |x2 + 1| - log |x2 + 2| + C

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}I=\int \limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{4}} \frac{d x}{1+\tan x}{/tex} {tex}I=\int\limits_0^\frac\pi4\frac{dx}{1+\frac{\sin x}{\cos x}}{/tex} {tex}=\int_\limits0^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{\cos x d x}{\cos x+\sin x}{/tex} ...(i) Using property, {tex}\int_\limits a^bf\left(x\right)dx=\int_\limits0^af\left(a+b-x\right)dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits0^{\frac\pi4}\frac{\cos\left(\frac\pi2-x\right)}{\sin\left(\frac\pi2-x\right)+\cos\left(\frac\pi2-x\right)}dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits0^{\frac\pi4}\frac{\sin x}{\cos x+\sin x}dx{/tex} ...(ii) Adding eqn (i) and (ii) {tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{\frac\pi4}\frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\cos x+\sin x}dx{/tex} {tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{\frac\pi4}dx=\left[x\right]_0^{\frac\pi4}{/tex} {tex}2I=\left[\frac\pi4-0\right]=\frac\pi4{/tex} {tex}I=\frac\pi8{/tex}
solution: {tex}I=\int \limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{4}} \frac{d x}{1+\tan x}{/tex} {tex}I=\int\limits_0^\frac\pi4\frac{dx}{1+\frac{\sin x}{\cos x}}{/tex} {tex}=\int_\limits0^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{\cos x d x}{\cos x+\sin x}{/tex} ...(i) Using property, {tex}\int_\limits a^bf\left(x\right)dx=\int_\limits0^af\left(a+b-x\right)dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits0^{\frac\pi4}\frac{\cos\left(\frac\pi2-x\right)}{\sin\left(\frac\pi2-x\right)+\cos\left(\frac\pi2-x\right)}dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits0^{\frac\pi4}\frac{\sin x}{\cos x+\sin x}dx{/tex} ...(ii) Adding eqn (i) and (ii) {tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{\frac\pi4}\frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\cos x+\sin x}dx{/tex} {tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{\frac\pi4}dx=\left[x\right]_0^{\frac\pi4}{/tex} {tex}2I=\left[\frac\pi4-0\right]=\frac\pi4{/tex} {tex}I=\frac\pi8{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int \limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{4}} \frac{d x}{1+\tan x}{/tex}
{tex}I=\int\limits_0^\frac\pi4\frac{dx}{1+\frac{\sin x}{\cos x}}{/tex} {tex}=\int_\limits0^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{\cos x d x}{\cos x+\sin x}{/tex} ...(i)
Using property,
{tex}\int_\limits a^bf\left(x\right)dx=\int_\limits0^af\left(a+b-x\right)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_\limits0^{\frac\pi4}\frac{\cos\left(\frac\pi2-x\right)}{\sin\left(\frac\pi2-x\right)+\cos\left(\frac\pi2-x\right)}dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_\limits0^{\frac\pi4}\frac{\sin x}{\cos x+\sin x}dx{/tex} ...(ii)
Adding eqn (i) and (ii)
{tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{\frac\pi4}\frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\cos x+\sin x}dx{/tex}
{tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{\frac\pi4}dx=\left[x\right]_0^{\frac\pi4}{/tex}
{tex}2I=\left[\frac\pi4-0\right]=\frac\pi4{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac\pi8{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}I=\int \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}} \cdot \sin 2 \mathrm{x} \ d \mathrm{x}{/tex} Using integration by parts Taking sin2x as first function {tex}I=\sin2x\int e^xdx-2\int\cos2x.e^xdx{/tex} {tex}I=\sin2x\int e^xdx-2(\cos2x.e^x+\int2\sin2x.e^x)dx{/tex} {tex}I=e^x.\sin2xdx-2\cos2x.e^x-\int4\sin2x.e^xdx{/tex} {tex}I=e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)-4\int\sin2x.e^xdx{/tex} Now, from eqn (i) {tex}I=e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)-4I\space+\ C{/tex} {tex}5I=e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)+\ C{/tex} {tex}I=\frac15e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)+C{/tex}
solution: {tex}I=\int \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}} \cdot \sin 2 \mathrm{x} \ d \mathrm{x}{/tex} Using integration by parts Taking sin2x as first function {tex}I=\sin2x\int e^xdx-2\int\cos2x.e^xdx{/tex} {tex}I=\sin2x\int e^xdx-2(\cos2x.e^x+\int2\sin2x.e^x)dx{/tex} {tex}I=e^x.\sin2xdx-2\cos2x.e^x-\int4\sin2x.e^xdx{/tex} {tex}I=e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)-4\int\sin2x.e^xdx{/tex} Now, from eqn (i) {tex}I=e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)-4I\space+\ C{/tex} {tex}5I=e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)+\ C{/tex} {tex}I=\frac15e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)+C{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}} \cdot \sin 2 \mathrm{x} \ d \mathrm{x}{/tex}
Using integration by parts
Taking sin2x as first function
{tex}I=\sin2x\int e^xdx-2\int\cos2x.e^xdx{/tex}
{tex}I=\sin2x\int e^xdx-2(\cos2x.e^x+\int2\sin2x.e^x)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=e^x.\sin2xdx-2\cos2x.e^x-\int4\sin2x.e^xdx{/tex}
{tex}I=e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)-4\int\sin2x.e^xdx{/tex}
Now, from eqn (i)
{tex}I=e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)-4I\space+\ C{/tex}
{tex}5I=e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)+\ C{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac15e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)+C{/tex}

(i) and eq. (ii) {tex}2I=\int_0^\frac\pi2\frac{cos^{\frac52}\;x+\sin^{\frac52}x}{\cos^{\frac52}x+\sin^{\frac52}x}dx{/tex} {tex}2I=\int_0^\frac\pi2dx{/tex} {tex}2I=\left[x\right]_0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}{/tex} {tex}2I=\frac\pi2-0=\frac\pi2{/tex} {tex}I=\frac\pi4{/tex}
solution: {tex}let\;I=\int_0^\frac\pi2\frac1{1+cot^{\frac52}x}dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_0^\frac\pi2\frac1{1+{\frac{\cos^{\frac52}x}{\sin^{\frac52}x}}}dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_0^\frac\pi2\frac{\sin^{\frac52}\;x}{\sin^{\frac52}\;x+\cos^{\frac52}x}dx{/tex} ...(i) ∵ {tex}\int_0^af(x)\;dx=\int_0^af(a-x)\;dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_0^\frac\pi2\frac{\sin^{\frac52}\;\left({\frac\pi2}-x\right)}{\sin^{\frac52}\left({\frac\pi2}-x\right)+\cos^{\frac52}\left({\frac\pi2}-x\right)}dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_0^\frac\pi2\frac{cos^{\frac52}\;x}{\cos^{\frac52}x+\sin^{\frac52}x}dx{/tex} ...(ii) Adding eq. (i) and eq. (ii) {tex}2I=\int_0^\frac\pi2\frac{cos^{\frac52}\;x+\sin^{\frac52}x}{\cos^{\frac52}x+\sin^{\frac52}x}dx{/tex} {tex}2I=\int_0^\frac\pi2dx{/tex} {tex}2I=\left[x\right]_0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}{/tex} {tex}2I=\frac\pi2-0=\frac\pi2{/tex} {tex}I=\frac\pi4{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}let\;I=\int_0^\frac\pi2\frac1{1+cot^{\frac52}x}dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_0^\frac\pi2\frac1{1+{\frac{\cos^{\frac52}x}{\sin^{\frac52}x}}}dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_0^\frac\pi2\frac{\sin^{\frac52}\;x}{\sin^{\frac52}\;x+\cos^{\frac52}x}dx{/tex} ...(i)
∵ {tex}\int_0^af(x)\;dx=\int_0^af(a-x)\;dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_0^\frac\pi2\frac{\sin^{\frac52}\;\left({\frac\pi2}-x\right)}{\sin^{\frac52}\left({\frac\pi2}-x\right)+\cos^{\frac52}\left({\frac\pi2}-x\right)}dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_0^\frac\pi2\frac{cos^{\frac52}\;x}{\cos^{\frac52}x+\sin^{\frac52}x}dx{/tex} ...(ii)
Adding eq. (i) and eq. (ii)
{tex}2I=\int_0^\frac\pi2\frac{cos^{\frac52}\;x+\sin^{\frac52}x}{\cos^{\frac52}x+\sin^{\frac52}x}dx{/tex}
{tex}2I=\int_0^\frac\pi2dx{/tex}
{tex}2I=\left[x\right]_0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}{/tex}
{tex}2I=\frac\pi2-0=\frac\pi2{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac\pi4{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}I=\int\frac{\sin x}{\sin\left(x-2a\right)}dx{/tex} Put x - 2a = t, dx = dt {tex}I=\int\frac{\sin(t+2a)}{\sin\;t}dt{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} sin (A+B) = sinA cosB + cosA sinB {tex}I=\int\frac{\sin\;t.\cos2a+\cos\;t.\sin2a}{\sin\;t}dt{/tex} {tex}I=\int\frac{\sin\;t.\cos2a}{\sin t}dt+\int\frac{\cos\;t.\sin2a}{\sin t}dt{/tex} {tex}I=\int\cos2a\;dt+\sin2a\int cot\;tdt{/tex} {tex}I=\cos2a.t+\sin2a\;\log\left|\sin\;t\right|+C{/tex} Putting value of t {tex}I=\cos2a.\left(x-2a\right)+\sin2a\;\log\left|\sin\;\left(x-2a\right)\right|+C{/tex} {tex}I=\sin2a\;\log\left|\sin\;\left(x-2a\right)\right|+x\cos\;2a-2a\;\cos2a+C{/tex} {tex}I=\sin2a\;\log\left|\sin\;\left(x-2a\right)\right|+x\cos\;2a+C{/tex} As 2a cos 2 a and C are constant.
solution: {tex}I=\int\frac{\sin x}{\sin\left(x-2a\right)}dx{/tex} Put x - 2a = t, dx = dt {tex}I=\int\frac{\sin(t+2a)}{\sin\;t}dt{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} sin (A+B) = sinA cosB + cosA sinB {tex}I=\int\frac{\sin\;t.\cos2a+\cos\;t.\sin2a}{\sin\;t}dt{/tex} {tex}I=\int\frac{\sin\;t.\cos2a}{\sin t}dt+\int\frac{\cos\;t.\sin2a}{\sin t}dt{/tex} {tex}I=\int\cos2a\;dt+\sin2a\int cot\;tdt{/tex} {tex}I=\cos2a.t+\sin2a\;\log\left|\sin\;t\right|+C{/tex} Putting value of t {tex}I=\cos2a.\left(x-2a\right)+\sin2a\;\log\left|\sin\;\left(x-2a\right)\right|+C{/tex} {tex}I=\sin2a\;\log\left|\sin\;\left(x-2a\right)\right|+x\cos\;2a-2a\;\cos2a+C{/tex} {tex}I=\sin2a\;\log\left|\sin\;\left(x-2a\right)\right|+x\cos\;2a+C{/tex} As 2a cos 2 a and C are constant.
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int\frac{\sin x}{\sin\left(x-2a\right)}dx{/tex}
Put x - 2a = t, dx = dt
{tex}I=\int\frac{\sin(t+2a)}{\sin\;t}dt{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} sin (A+B) = sinA cosB + cosA sinB
{tex}I=\int\frac{\sin\;t.\cos2a+\cos\;t.\sin2a}{\sin\;t}dt{/tex}
{tex}I=\int\frac{\sin\;t.\cos2a}{\sin t}dt+\int\frac{\cos\;t.\sin2a}{\sin t}dt{/tex}
{tex}I=\int\cos2a\;dt+\sin2a\int cot\;tdt{/tex}
{tex}I=\cos2a.t+\sin2a\;\log\left|\sin\;t\right|+C{/tex}
Putting value of t
{tex}I=\cos2a.\left(x-2a\right)+\sin2a\;\log\left|\sin\;\left(x-2a\right)\right|+C{/tex}
{tex}I=\sin2a\;\log\left|\sin\;\left(x-2a\right)\right|+x\cos\;2a-2a\;\cos2a+C{/tex}
{tex}I=\sin2a\;\log\left|\sin\;\left(x-2a\right)\right|+x\cos\;2a+C{/tex}
As 2a cos 2 a and C are constant.

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}I=\int \limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{1}{1+(\tan x)^{\frac{2}{3}}} d x{/tex} ...(i) Using property, {tex}\int_\limits0^af(x)dx=\int_\limits0^af(a-x)dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}\frac{dx}{1+\left[\tan\left({\displaystyle\frac\pi2}-x\right)\right]^\frac23}{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}\frac{dx}{1+\left[cotx\right]^\frac23}{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}\frac{\left(\tan x\right)^\frac23dx}{1+\left(\tan x\right)^\frac23}{/tex} ...(ii) Adding eqn (i) and (ii) {tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}\frac{1+\left(\tan x\right)^\frac23dx}{1+\left(\tan x\right)^\frac23}{/tex} {tex}2I=\left[x\right]_0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}{/tex} {tex}2I=\left[\frac\pi2-0\right]{/tex} {tex}I=\frac\pi4{/tex}
solution: {tex}I=\int \limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{1}{1+(\tan x)^{\frac{2}{3}}} d x{/tex} ...(i) Using property, {tex}\int_\limits0^af(x)dx=\int_\limits0^af(a-x)dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}\frac{dx}{1+\left[\tan\left({\displaystyle\frac\pi2}-x\right)\right]^\frac23}{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}\frac{dx}{1+\left[cotx\right]^\frac23}{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}\frac{\left(\tan x\right)^\frac23dx}{1+\left(\tan x\right)^\frac23}{/tex} ...(ii) Adding eqn (i) and (ii) {tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}\frac{1+\left(\tan x\right)^\frac23dx}{1+\left(\tan x\right)^\frac23}{/tex} {tex}2I=\left[x\right]_0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}{/tex} {tex}2I=\left[\frac\pi2-0\right]{/tex} {tex}I=\frac\pi4{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int \limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{1}{1+(\tan x)^{\frac{2}{3}}} d x{/tex} ...(i)
Using property,
{tex}\int_\limits0^af(x)dx=\int_\limits0^af(a-x)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_\limits0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}\frac{dx}{1+\left[\tan\left({\displaystyle\frac\pi2}-x\right)\right]^\frac23}{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_\limits0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}\frac{dx}{1+\left[cotx\right]^\frac23}{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_\limits0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}\frac{\left(\tan x\right)^\frac23dx}{1+\left(\tan x\right)^\frac23}{/tex} ...(ii)
Adding eqn (i) and (ii)
{tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}\frac{1+\left(\tan x\right)^\frac23dx}{1+\left(\tan x\right)^\frac23}{/tex}
{tex}2I=\left[x\right]_0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}{/tex}
{tex}2I=\left[\frac\pi2-0\right]{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac\pi4{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}I=\int\cos x\;\tan^{-1}\left(\sin x\right)dx{/tex} Let sin x = t, cos x dx = dt {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}I=\int\tan^{-1}t.1dt{/tex} Using integration by parts Let {tex}\tan^{-1}t=u{/tex} , dv = dt {tex}\frac1{1+t^2}dt=du{/tex} , v = t So {tex}I=\left(\tan^{-1}t\right)t-\int t.\left(\frac1{1+t^2}\right)dt{/tex} {tex}I=t.\left(\tan^{-1}t\right)-\int\frac t{1+t^2}dt{/tex} let, s = 1 + t², ds = 2 t dt {tex}\frac12{/tex} ds = t dt {tex}I=t.\left(\tan^{-1}t\right)-\frac12\int\frac1sds{/tex} {tex}I=t.\left(\tan^{-1}t\right)-\frac12\log\;s\;+\;C{/tex} {tex}I=t.\left(\tan^{-1}t\right)-\frac12\log\;\left(1+t^2\right)\;+\;C{/tex} {tex}I=\sin x.\tan^{-1}\left(\sin x\right)-\frac12\log\left(1+\sin^2x\right)+C{/tex}
solution: {tex}I=\int\cos x\;\tan^{-1}\left(\sin x\right)dx{/tex} Let sin x = t, cos x dx = dt {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}I=\int\tan^{-1}t.1dt{/tex} Using integration by parts Let {tex}\tan^{-1}t=u{/tex} , dv = dt {tex}\frac1{1+t^2}dt=du{/tex} , v = t So {tex}I=\left(\tan^{-1}t\right)t-\int t.\left(\frac1{1+t^2}\right)dt{/tex} {tex}I=t.\left(\tan^{-1}t\right)-\int\frac t{1+t^2}dt{/tex} let, s = 1 + t², ds = 2 t dt {tex}\frac12{/tex} ds = t dt {tex}I=t.\left(\tan^{-1}t\right)-\frac12\int\frac1sds{/tex} {tex}I=t.\left(\tan^{-1}t\right)-\frac12\log\;s\;+\;C{/tex} {tex}I=t.\left(\tan^{-1}t\right)-\frac12\log\;\left(1+t^2\right)\;+\;C{/tex} {tex}I=\sin x.\tan^{-1}\left(\sin x\right)-\frac12\log\left(1+\sin^2x\right)+C{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int\cos x\;\tan^{-1}\left(\sin x\right)dx{/tex}
Let sin x = t, cos x dx = dt
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}I=\int\tan^{-1}t.1dt{/tex}
Using integration by parts
Let {tex}\tan^{-1}t=u{/tex}, dv = dt
{tex}\frac1{1+t^2}dt=du{/tex}, v = t
So {tex}I=\left(\tan^{-1}t\right)t-\int t.\left(\frac1{1+t^2}\right)dt{/tex}
{tex}I=t.\left(\tan^{-1}t\right)-\int\frac t{1+t^2}dt{/tex}
let, s = 1 + t², ds = 2 t dt
{tex}\frac12{/tex} ds = t dt
{tex}I=t.\left(\tan^{-1}t\right)-\frac12\int\frac1sds{/tex}
{tex}I=t.\left(\tan^{-1}t\right)-\frac12\log\;s\;+\;C{/tex}
{tex}I=t.\left(\tan^{-1}t\right)-\frac12\log\;\left(1+t^2\right)\;+\;C{/tex}
{tex}I=\sin x.\tan^{-1}\left(\sin x\right)-\frac12\log\left(1+\sin^2x\right)+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}I=\int \limits_1^3 \frac{\sqrt{x}}{\sqrt{x}+\sqrt{4-x}} d x{/tex} ...(i) Using property, {tex}\int_\limits a^bf\left(x\right)dx=\int_\limits a^bf\left(a+b-x\right)dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits1^3\frac{\sqrt{4-x}}{\sqrt x+\sqrt{4-x}}dx{/tex} ...(ii) Adding eqn (i) and (ii) {tex}2I=\int_\limits1^3\frac{\sqrt x+\sqrt{4-x}}{\sqrt x+\sqrt{4-x}}dx{/tex} {tex}2I=\int_\limits1^3dx=\left[x\right]_1^3{/tex} {tex}2I=\left[3-1\right]=2{/tex} I = 1
solution: {tex}I=\int \limits_1^3 \frac{\sqrt{x}}{\sqrt{x}+\sqrt{4-x}} d x{/tex} ...(i) Using property, {tex}\int_\limits a^bf\left(x\right)dx=\int_\limits a^bf\left(a+b-x\right)dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits1^3\frac{\sqrt{4-x}}{\sqrt x+\sqrt{4-x}}dx{/tex} ...(ii) Adding eqn (i) and (ii) {tex}2I=\int_\limits1^3\frac{\sqrt x+\sqrt{4-x}}{\sqrt x+\sqrt{4-x}}dx{/tex} {tex}2I=\int_\limits1^3dx=\left[x\right]_1^3{/tex} {tex}2I=\left[3-1\right]=2{/tex} I = 1
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int \limits_1^3 \frac{\sqrt{x}}{\sqrt{x}+\sqrt{4-x}} d x{/tex} ...(i)
Using property,
{tex}\int_\limits a^bf\left(x\right)dx=\int_\limits a^bf\left(a+b-x\right)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_\limits1^3\frac{\sqrt{4-x}}{\sqrt x+\sqrt{4-x}}dx{/tex} ...(ii)
Adding eqn (i) and (ii)
{tex}2I=\int_\limits1^3\frac{\sqrt x+\sqrt{4-x}}{\sqrt x+\sqrt{4-x}}dx{/tex}
{tex}2I=\int_\limits1^3dx=\left[x\right]_1^3{/tex}
{tex}2I=\left[3-1\right]=2{/tex}
I = 1

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}I=\int\frac{e^x}{\left(e^x+1\right)\left(e^x+3\right)}{/tex} Using integration by parts Let {tex}\frac{e^x}{\left(e^x+1\right)\left(e^x+3\right)}=\frac A{\left(e^x+1\right)}+\frac B{\left(e^x+3\right)}{/tex} e x = Ae x + 3A + Be x + B e x = (A + B)e x + 3A + B On comparing A + B = 1, 3A + B = 0 {tex}A=-\frac12{/tex} , {tex}B=\frac32{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}I=-\frac12\int\frac{dx}{e^x+1}+\frac32\int\frac{dx}{e^x+3}{/tex} {tex}I=-\frac12\int\frac{dx}{\frac1{e^{-x}}+1}+\frac32\int\frac{dx}{\frac1{e^{-x}}+3}{/tex} {tex}I=-\frac12\int\frac{e^{-x}\;dx}{1+e^{-x}}+\frac32\int\frac{e^{-x}\;dx}{1+3e^{-x}}{/tex} Put 1 + e -x = u and 1 + 3e -x = v -e -x dx = du and -3e -x dx = dv {tex}I=\frac12\int\frac{du}u-\frac12\int\frac{dv}v{/tex} {tex}I=\frac12\log\left|u\right|-\frac12\log\left|v\right|+C{/tex} {tex}I=\frac12\log\left|\frac uv\right|+C{/tex} {tex}I=\frac12\log\left|\frac{1+e^{-x}}{1+3e^{-x}}\right|+C{/tex}
solution: {tex}I=\int\frac{e^x}{\left(e^x+1\right)\left(e^x+3\right)}{/tex} Using integration by parts Let {tex}\frac{e^x}{\left(e^x+1\right)\left(e^x+3\right)}=\frac A{\left(e^x+1\right)}+\frac B{\left(e^x+3\right)}{/tex} e x = Ae x + 3A + Be x + B e x = (A + B)e x + 3A + B On comparing A + B = 1, 3A + B = 0 {tex}A=-\frac12{/tex} , {tex}B=\frac32{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}I=-\frac12\int\frac{dx}{e^x+1}+\frac32\int\frac{dx}{e^x+3}{/tex} {tex}I=-\frac12\int\frac{dx}{\frac1{e^{-x}}+1}+\frac32\int\frac{dx}{\frac1{e^{-x}}+3}{/tex} {tex}I=-\frac12\int\frac{e^{-x}\;dx}{1+e^{-x}}+\frac32\int\frac{e^{-x}\;dx}{1+3e^{-x}}{/tex} Put 1 + e -x = u and 1 + 3e -x = v -e -x dx = du and -3e -x dx = dv {tex}I=\frac12\int\frac{du}u-\frac12\int\frac{dv}v{/tex} {tex}I=\frac12\log\left|u\right|-\frac12\log\left|v\right|+C{/tex} {tex}I=\frac12\log\left|\frac uv\right|+C{/tex} {tex}I=\frac12\log\left|\frac{1+e^{-x}}{1+3e^{-x}}\right|+C{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int\frac{e^x}{\left(e^x+1\right)\left(e^x+3\right)}{/tex}
Using integration by parts
Let {tex}\frac{e^x}{\left(e^x+1\right)\left(e^x+3\right)}=\frac A{\left(e^x+1\right)}+\frac B{\left(e^x+3\right)}{/tex}
ex = Aex + 3A + Bex + B
ex = (A + B)ex + 3A + B
On comparing A + B = 1, 3A + B = 0
{tex}A=-\frac12{/tex}, {tex}B=\frac32{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}I=-\frac12\int\frac{dx}{e^x+1}+\frac32\int\frac{dx}{e^x+3}{/tex}
{tex}I=-\frac12\int\frac{dx}{\frac1{e^{-x}}+1}+\frac32\int\frac{dx}{\frac1{e^{-x}}+3}{/tex}
{tex}I=-\frac12\int\frac{e^{-x}\;dx}{1+e^{-x}}+\frac32\int\frac{e^{-x}\;dx}{1+3e^{-x}}{/tex}
Put 1 + e-x = u and 1 + 3e-x = v
-e-xdx = du and -3e-xdx = dv
{tex}I=\frac12\int\frac{du}u-\frac12\int\frac{dv}v{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac12\log\left|u\right|-\frac12\log\left|v\right|+C{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac12\log\left|\frac uv\right|+C{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac12\log\left|\frac{1+e^{-x}}{1+3e^{-x}}\right|+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let {tex}I=\int_0^5x\sqrt{5-x}\;dx{/tex} Using property {tex}\int_0^af(x)\;dx=\int_0^af\left(a-x\right)dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_0^5\left(5-x\right)\sqrt xdx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_0^55\sqrt xdx-\int_0^5\left(x\right)^\frac32dx{/tex} {tex}I=5\left[\frac{x^\frac32}{\frac32}\right]_0^5-\left[\frac{x^\frac52}{\frac52}\right]_0^5{/tex} {tex}I=\frac{5\times2}3\left[5^{\textstyle\frac32}\right]-\frac25\left(5^{\textstyle\frac52}\right){/tex} {tex}I=\frac{250\sqrt5-150\sqrt5}{15}=\frac{20\sqrt5}3{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}I=\int_0^5x\sqrt{5-x}\;dx{/tex} Using property {tex}\int_0^af(x)\;dx=\int_0^af\left(a-x\right)dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_0^5\left(5-x\right)\sqrt xdx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_0^55\sqrt xdx-\int_0^5\left(x\right)^\frac32dx{/tex} {tex}I=5\left[\frac{x^\frac32}{\frac32}\right]_0^5-\left[\frac{x^\frac52}{\frac52}\right]_0^5{/tex} {tex}I=\frac{5\times2}3\left[5^{\textstyle\frac32}\right]-\frac25\left(5^{\textstyle\frac52}\right){/tex} {tex}I=\frac{250\sqrt5-150\sqrt5}{15}=\frac{20\sqrt5}3{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I=\int_0^5x\sqrt{5-x}\;dx{/tex}
Using property {tex}\int_0^af(x)\;dx=\int_0^af\left(a-x\right)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_0^5\left(5-x\right)\sqrt xdx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_0^55\sqrt xdx-\int_0^5\left(x\right)^\frac32dx{/tex}
{tex}I=5\left[\frac{x^\frac32}{\frac32}\right]_0^5-\left[\frac{x^\frac52}{\frac52}\right]_0^5{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac{5\times2}3\left[5^{\textstyle\frac32}\right]-\frac25\left(5^{\textstyle\frac52}\right){/tex}
{tex}I=\frac{250\sqrt5-150\sqrt5}{15}=\frac{20\sqrt5}3{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: I = {tex}\int \sin 2 x \cdot \sin 3 x d x{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} Sin A {tex}\cdot{/tex} Sin B = {tex}\frac{1}{2}[\cos (A-B)-\cos (A+B)]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\int \frac{1}{2}[\cos (2 x-3 x){/tex} - {tex}\cos (2 x+3 x)] d x{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int[\cos (-x){/tex} - {tex}\cos 5 x] d x{/tex} I = {tex}\left.\frac{1}{2} \int[\cos x-\cos 5 x)\right] d x{/tex} { {tex}\because{/tex} cos (-x) = cosx} I = {tex}\frac{1}{2}[\sin x{/tex} - {tex}\left.\frac{\sin 5 x}{5}\right]+c{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \sin x-\frac{1}{10} \sin 5 x+c{/tex}
solution: I = {tex}\int \sin 2 x \cdot \sin 3 x d x{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} Sin A {tex}\cdot{/tex} Sin B = {tex}\frac{1}{2}[\cos (A-B)-\cos (A+B)]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\int \frac{1}{2}[\cos (2 x-3 x){/tex} - {tex}\cos (2 x+3 x)] d x{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int[\cos (-x){/tex} - {tex}\cos 5 x] d x{/tex} I = {tex}\left.\frac{1}{2} \int[\cos x-\cos 5 x)\right] d x{/tex} { {tex}\because{/tex} cos (-x) = cosx} I = {tex}\frac{1}{2}[\sin x{/tex} - {tex}\left.\frac{\sin 5 x}{5}\right]+c{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \sin x-\frac{1}{10} \sin 5 x+c{/tex}
solution_html:
I = {tex}\int \sin 2 x \cdot \sin 3 x d x{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} Sin A {tex}\cdot{/tex} Sin B = {tex}\frac{1}{2}[\cos (A-B)-\cos (A+B)]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\int \frac{1}{2}[\cos (2 x-3 x){/tex} - {tex}\cos (2 x+3 x)] d x{/tex}
I = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \int[\cos (-x){/tex} - {tex}\cos 5 x] d x{/tex}
I = {tex}\left.\frac{1}{2} \int[\cos x-\cos 5 x)\right] d x{/tex} {{tex}\because{/tex} cos (-x) = cosx}
I = {tex}\frac{1}{2}[\sin x{/tex} - {tex}\left.\frac{\sin 5 x}{5}\right]+c{/tex}
I = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \sin x-\frac{1}{10} \sin 5 x+c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: I = {tex}=\int \underbrace{\sin ^{-1} x}_I \cdot \underbrace{1}_{{II}} d x{/tex} using integration by parts I = {tex}\sin ^{-1} x \int 1 \cdot d x{/tex} - {tex}\int\left[\frac{d}{d x}\left(\sin ^{-1} x\right) \cdot \int 1 \cdot d x\right] d x{/tex} = {tex}\sin ^{-1} x \cdot x{/tex} - {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}} \cdot x d x{/tex} = {tex}x \cdot \sin ^{-1} x{/tex} - {tex}\int \frac{x}{\sqrt{1-x^2}} d x{/tex} = {tex}x \sin ^{-1} x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{t}}{/tex} [put 1 - x 2 = t, -2xdx = dt, xdx = - {tex}\frac{dt}{2}{/tex} ] = {tex}x \sin ^{-1} x+\frac{1}{2} \int t^{\frac{-1}{2}} d t{/tex} = {tex}x \sin ^{-1} x{/tex} + {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{t^{\frac{1}{2}}}{\frac{1}{2}}\right]+c{/tex} {using {tex}\int x^n d x=\frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1}{/tex} } = {tex}x \sin ^{-1} x+\sqrt{t}+c{/tex} = {tex}x \sin ^{-1} x+\sqrt{1-x^2}+c{/tex}
solution: I = {tex}=\int \underbrace{\sin ^{-1} x}_I \cdot \underbrace{1}_{{II}} d x{/tex} using integration by parts I = {tex}\sin ^{-1} x \int 1 \cdot d x{/tex} - {tex}\int\left[\frac{d}{d x}\left(\sin ^{-1} x\right) \cdot \int 1 \cdot d x\right] d x{/tex} = {tex}\sin ^{-1} x \cdot x{/tex} - {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}} \cdot x d x{/tex} = {tex}x \cdot \sin ^{-1} x{/tex} - {tex}\int \frac{x}{\sqrt{1-x^2}} d x{/tex} = {tex}x \sin ^{-1} x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{t}}{/tex} [put 1 - x 2 = t, -2xdx = dt, xdx = - {tex}\frac{dt}{2}{/tex} ] = {tex}x \sin ^{-1} x+\frac{1}{2} \int t^{\frac{-1}{2}} d t{/tex} = {tex}x \sin ^{-1} x{/tex} + {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{t^{\frac{1}{2}}}{\frac{1}{2}}\right]+c{/tex} {using {tex}\int x^n d x=\frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1}{/tex} } = {tex}x \sin ^{-1} x+\sqrt{t}+c{/tex} = {tex}x \sin ^{-1} x+\sqrt{1-x^2}+c{/tex}
solution_html:
I = {tex}=\int \underbrace{\sin ^{-1} x}_I \cdot \underbrace{1}_{{II}} d x{/tex}
using integration by parts
I = {tex}\sin ^{-1} x \int 1 \cdot d x{/tex} - {tex}\int\left[\frac{d}{d x}\left(\sin ^{-1} x\right) \cdot \int 1 \cdot d x\right] d x{/tex}
= {tex}\sin ^{-1} x \cdot x{/tex} - {tex}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}} \cdot x d x{/tex}
= {tex}x \cdot \sin ^{-1} x{/tex} - {tex}\int \frac{x}{\sqrt{1-x^2}} d x{/tex}
= {tex}x \sin ^{-1} x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{t}}{/tex} [put 1 - x2 = t, -2xdx = dt, xdx = - {tex}\frac{dt}{2}{/tex}]
= {tex}x \sin ^{-1} x+\frac{1}{2} \int t^{\frac{-1}{2}} d t{/tex}
= {tex}x \sin ^{-1} x{/tex} + {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{t^{\frac{1}{2}}}{\frac{1}{2}}\right]+c{/tex} {using {tex}\int x^n d x=\frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1}{/tex}}
= {tex}x \sin ^{-1} x+\sqrt{t}+c{/tex}
= {tex}x \sin ^{-1} x+\sqrt{1-x^2}+c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}I=\int \frac{\log x-3}{(\log x)^4} \mathrm{~d} x{/tex} {tex}I=\int\frac{\log x}{(\log x)^4}dx-\int\frac3{\left(\log x\right)^4}dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int\frac1{(\log x)^3}dx-\int\frac3{\left(\log x\right)^4}dx{/tex} ...(i) Taking {tex}I_1=\int\frac1{(\log x)^3}.1dx{/tex} Using integration by parts {tex}I_1=\frac1{\left(\log x\right)^3}.x-\int\frac{\left(-3\right)}{x(\log x)^4}.xdx{/tex} {tex}I_1=\frac x{\left(\log x\right)^3}+\int\frac3{(\log x)^4}dx{/tex} Putting in eqn (i) {tex}I=\frac x{\left(\log x\right)^3}+\int\frac3{(\log x)^4}dx-\int\frac3{\left(\log x\right)^4}dx{/tex} {tex}I=\frac x{\left(\log x\right)^3}{/tex}
solution: {tex}I=\int \frac{\log x-3}{(\log x)^4} \mathrm{~d} x{/tex} {tex}I=\int\frac{\log x}{(\log x)^4}dx-\int\frac3{\left(\log x\right)^4}dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int\frac1{(\log x)^3}dx-\int\frac3{\left(\log x\right)^4}dx{/tex} ...(i) Taking {tex}I_1=\int\frac1{(\log x)^3}.1dx{/tex} Using integration by parts {tex}I_1=\frac1{\left(\log x\right)^3}.x-\int\frac{\left(-3\right)}{x(\log x)^4}.xdx{/tex} {tex}I_1=\frac x{\left(\log x\right)^3}+\int\frac3{(\log x)^4}dx{/tex} Putting in eqn (i) {tex}I=\frac x{\left(\log x\right)^3}+\int\frac3{(\log x)^4}dx-\int\frac3{\left(\log x\right)^4}dx{/tex} {tex}I=\frac x{\left(\log x\right)^3}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int \frac{\log x-3}{(\log x)^4} \mathrm{~d} x{/tex}
{tex}I=\int\frac{\log x}{(\log x)^4}dx-\int\frac3{\left(\log x\right)^4}dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int\frac1{(\log x)^3}dx-\int\frac3{\left(\log x\right)^4}dx{/tex} ...(i)
Taking {tex}I_1=\int\frac1{(\log x)^3}.1dx{/tex}
Using integration by parts
{tex}I_1=\frac1{\left(\log x\right)^3}.x-\int\frac{\left(-3\right)}{x(\log x)^4}.xdx{/tex}
{tex}I_1=\frac x{\left(\log x\right)^3}+\int\frac3{(\log x)^4}dx{/tex}
Putting in eqn (i)
{tex}I=\frac x{\left(\log x\right)^3}+\int\frac3{(\log x)^4}dx-\int\frac3{\left(\log x\right)^4}dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac x{\left(\log x\right)^3}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}I=\int\frac{dx}{\sqrt x+\sqrt[3]x}=\int\frac{dx}{x^\frac12+x^\frac32}{/tex} Put x = t 6 , dx = 6t 5 dt {tex}I=\int\frac{6t^5dt}{t^3+t^2}=6\int\frac{t^3dt}{t+1}{/tex} {tex}I=6\int\left[t^2-t+1-\frac1{t+1}\right]dt{/tex} {tex}I=6\left[\frac{t^3}3-\frac{t^2}2+t-\log\left(t+1\right)\right]+C{/tex} {tex}I=6\left[\frac{\sqrt x}3-\frac{\sqrt[3]x}2+x^{\textstyle\frac16}-\log\left(x^{\textstyle\frac16}+1\right)\right]+c{/tex}
solution: {tex}I=\int\frac{dx}{\sqrt x+\sqrt[3]x}=\int\frac{dx}{x^\frac12+x^\frac32}{/tex} Put x = t 6 , dx = 6t 5 dt {tex}I=\int\frac{6t^5dt}{t^3+t^2}=6\int\frac{t^3dt}{t+1}{/tex} {tex}I=6\int\left[t^2-t+1-\frac1{t+1}\right]dt{/tex} {tex}I=6\left[\frac{t^3}3-\frac{t^2}2+t-\log\left(t+1\right)\right]+C{/tex} {tex}I=6\left[\frac{\sqrt x}3-\frac{\sqrt[3]x}2+x^{\textstyle\frac16}-\log\left(x^{\textstyle\frac16}+1\right)\right]+c{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int\frac{dx}{\sqrt x+\sqrt[3]x}=\int\frac{dx}{x^\frac12+x^\frac32}{/tex}
Put x = t6 , dx = 6t5 dt
{tex}I=\int\frac{6t^5dt}{t^3+t^2}=6\int\frac{t^3dt}{t+1}{/tex}
{tex}I=6\int\left[t^2-t+1-\frac1{t+1}\right]dt{/tex}
{tex}I=6\left[\frac{t^3}3-\frac{t^2}2+t-\log\left(t+1\right)\right]+C{/tex}
{tex}I=6\left[\frac{\sqrt x}3-\frac{\sqrt[3]x}2+x^{\textstyle\frac16}-\log\left(x^{\textstyle\frac16}+1\right)\right]+c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}I=\int \limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\cos x}{(1+\sin x)(4+\sin x)} \mathrm{d} x{/tex} Put sinx = t cosx dx = dt At x = 0 t = 0, at x = {tex}\frac {tex}\pi{/tex} 2{/tex} t = 1 {tex}I=\int_\limits0^1\frac{dt}{\left(1+t\right)\left(9+t\right)}{/tex} Now using partial fraction {tex}\frac1{\left(1+t\right)\left(9+t\right)}=\frac A{\left(1+t\right)}+\frac B{\left(4+t\right)}{/tex} ...(1) 1 = 4A + A + B + Bt = 4A + B + (A + B)t A + B = 0, 4A + B = 1 A = {tex}\frac13{/tex} and B = {tex}\frac{-1}{3}{/tex} Putting in eqn(1) {tex}I=\frac13\int\limits_0^1\frac{dt}{\left(1+t\right)}-\frac13\int_\limits0^1\frac{dt}{\left(4+t\right)}{/tex} {tex}I=\frac13\left[\log\left(1+t\right)\right]_0^1-\frac13\left[\log\left(4+t\right)\right]_0^1{/tex} {tex}I=\frac13\left[\log2-\log1\right]-\frac13\left[\log5-\log4\right]{/tex} {tex}I=\frac13\log\left(\frac85\right){/tex}
solution: {tex}I=\int \limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\cos x}{(1+\sin x)(4+\sin x)} \mathrm{d} x{/tex} Put sinx = t cosx dx = dt At x = 0 t = 0, at x = {tex}\frac {tex}\pi{/tex} 2{/tex} t = 1 {tex}I=\int_\limits0^1\frac{dt}{\left(1+t\right)\left(9+t\right)}{/tex} Now using partial fraction {tex}\frac1{\left(1+t\right)\left(9+t\right)}=\frac A{\left(1+t\right)}+\frac B{\left(4+t\right)}{/tex} ...(1) 1 = 4A + A + B + Bt = 4A + B + (A + B)t A + B = 0, 4A + B = 1 A = {tex}\frac13{/tex} and B = {tex}\frac{-1}{3}{/tex} Putting in eqn(1) {tex}I=\frac13\int\limits_0^1\frac{dt}{\left(1+t\right)}-\frac13\int_\limits0^1\frac{dt}{\left(4+t\right)}{/tex} {tex}I=\frac13\left[\log\left(1+t\right)\right]_0^1-\frac13\left[\log\left(4+t\right)\right]_0^1{/tex} {tex}I=\frac13\left[\log2-\log1\right]-\frac13\left[\log5-\log4\right]{/tex} {tex}I=\frac13\log\left(\frac85\right){/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int \limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\cos x}{(1+\sin x)(4+\sin x)} \mathrm{d} x{/tex}
Put sinx = t
cosx dx = dt
At x = 0 t = 0, at x = {tex}\frac{tex}\pi{/tex}2{/tex} t = 1
{tex}I=\int_\limits0^1\frac{dt}{\left(1+t\right)\left(9+t\right)}{/tex}
Now using partial fraction
{tex}\frac1{\left(1+t\right)\left(9+t\right)}=\frac A{\left(1+t\right)}+\frac B{\left(4+t\right)}{/tex} ...(1)
1 = 4A + A + B + Bt = 4A + B + (A + B)t
A + B = 0, 4A + B = 1
A = {tex}\frac13{/tex} and B = {tex}\frac{-1}{3}{/tex}
Putting in eqn(1)
{tex}I=\frac13\int\limits_0^1\frac{dt}{\left(1+t\right)}-\frac13\int_\limits0^1\frac{dt}{\left(4+t\right)}{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac13\left[\log\left(1+t\right)\right]_0^1-\frac13\left[\log\left(4+t\right)\right]_0^1{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac13\left[\log2-\log1\right]-\frac13\left[\log5-\log4\right]{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac13\log\left(\frac85\right){/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}I=\int_\limits{-\pi}^\pi\left(3\sin x-2\right)^2dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits{-\pi}^\pi\left(9\sin^2x+4-12\sin x\right)dx{/tex} {tex}=\int_\limits{-\pi}^\pi9\sin^2xdx+\int_\limits{-\pi}^\pi4dx-12\int_\limits{-\pi}^\pi\sin xdx{/tex} Using Property {tex}\int_\limits{-a}^af\left(x\right)dx=2\int_\limits0^af\left(x\right)dx\;,\;f\left(-x\right)=f\left(x\right){/tex} {tex}=0\;,\;f\left(-x\right)=-f\left(x\right){/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} sin 2 x is even function, sin x is a odd function So, {tex}I=18\int_\limits0^\pi\sin xdx+4\int_\limits{-\pi}^\pi dx-12\times0{/tex} {tex}I=18\int_\limits0^\pi\left[\frac12-\frac{\cos2x}2\right]dx+4\left[x\right]_{-\pi}^\pi{/tex} {tex}I=18\left[\frac x2-\frac{\sin2x}4\right]_0^\pi+4\left[\pi+\pi\right]{/tex} {tex}I=18\left[\frac\pi2-\frac{\sin2\pi}4-0+0\right]+8\pi{/tex} {tex}I=18\left[\frac\pi2-0\right]+8\pi{/tex} I = 9 {tex}\pi{/tex} + 8 {tex}\pi{/tex} = 17 {tex}\pi{/tex}
solution: {tex}I=\int_\limits{-\pi}^\pi\left(3\sin x-2\right)^2dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits{-\pi}^\pi\left(9\sin^2x+4-12\sin x\right)dx{/tex} {tex}=\int_\limits{-\pi}^\pi9\sin^2xdx+\int_\limits{-\pi}^\pi4dx-12\int_\limits{-\pi}^\pi\sin xdx{/tex} Using Property {tex}\int_\limits{-a}^af\left(x\right)dx=2\int_\limits0^af\left(x\right)dx\;,\;f\left(-x\right)=f\left(x\right){/tex} {tex}=0\;,\;f\left(-x\right)=-f\left(x\right){/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} sin 2 x is even function, sin x is a odd function So, {tex}I=18\int_\limits0^\pi\sin xdx+4\int_\limits{-\pi}^\pi dx-12\times0{/tex} {tex}I=18\int_\limits0^\pi\left[\frac12-\frac{\cos2x}2\right]dx+4\left[x\right]_{-\pi}^\pi{/tex} {tex}I=18\left[\frac x2-\frac{\sin2x}4\right]_0^\pi+4\left[\pi+\pi\right]{/tex} {tex}I=18\left[\frac\pi2-\frac{\sin2\pi}4-0+0\right]+8\pi{/tex} {tex}I=18\left[\frac\pi2-0\right]+8\pi{/tex} I = 9 {tex}\pi{/tex} + 8 {tex}\pi{/tex} = 17 {tex}\pi{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int_\limits{-\pi}^\pi\left(3\sin x-2\right)^2dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_\limits{-\pi}^\pi\left(9\sin^2x+4-12\sin x\right)dx{/tex}
{tex}=\int_\limits{-\pi}^\pi9\sin^2xdx+\int_\limits{-\pi}^\pi4dx-12\int_\limits{-\pi}^\pi\sin xdx{/tex}
Using Property
{tex}\int_\limits{-a}^af\left(x\right)dx=2\int_\limits0^af\left(x\right)dx\;,\;f\left(-x\right)=f\left(x\right){/tex}
{tex}=0\;,\;f\left(-x\right)=-f\left(x\right){/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} sin2x is even function, sin x is a odd function
So, {tex}I=18\int_\limits0^\pi\sin xdx+4\int_\limits{-\pi}^\pi dx-12\times0{/tex}
{tex}I=18\int_\limits0^\pi\left[\frac12-\frac{\cos2x}2\right]dx+4\left[x\right]_{-\pi}^\pi{/tex}
{tex}I=18\left[\frac x2-\frac{\sin2x}4\right]_0^\pi+4\left[\pi+\pi\right]{/tex}
{tex}I=18\left[\frac\pi2-\frac{\sin2\pi}4-0+0\right]+8\pi{/tex}
{tex}I=18\left[\frac\pi2-0\right]+8\pi{/tex}
I = 9{tex}\pi{/tex} + 8{tex}\pi{/tex} = 17{tex}\pi{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: ∵ sin 3x = 3 sinx - 4sin 3 x {tex}\int{\textstyle\frac{\sin3x}{\sin x}}\;dx{/tex} {tex}=\int{\textstyle\frac{3\sin x-4\sin^3x}{\sin x}}\;dx{/tex} {tex}=3\int dx-4\int\;\sin^2xdx{/tex} Putting {tex}sin^2x=\frac{1-cos2x}2{/tex} {tex}=3\int dx-4\int\;\frac{1-\cos2x}2dx{/tex} {tex}=3\int dx-\frac42\int\;dx+\frac42\int\cos2x\;dx{/tex} = 3x - 2x + 2 {tex}\frac{sin2x}2{/tex} +C {tex}\left\{\int\cos\;ax=\frac{\sin\;ax}a\right\}{/tex} = x + sin2x +C
solution: ∵ sin 3x = 3 sinx - 4sin 3 x {tex}\int{\textstyle\frac{\sin3x}{\sin x}}\;dx{/tex} {tex}=\int{\textstyle\frac{3\sin x-4\sin^3x}{\sin x}}\;dx{/tex} {tex}=3\int dx-4\int\;\sin^2xdx{/tex} Putting {tex}sin^2x=\frac{1-cos2x}2{/tex} {tex}=3\int dx-4\int\;\frac{1-\cos2x}2dx{/tex} {tex}=3\int dx-\frac42\int\;dx+\frac42\int\cos2x\;dx{/tex} = 3x - 2x + 2 {tex}\frac{sin2x}2{/tex} +C {tex}\left\{\int\cos\;ax=\frac{\sin\;ax}a\right\}{/tex} = x + sin2x +C
solution_html:
∵ sin 3x = 3 sinx - 4sin3x
{tex}\int{\textstyle\frac{\sin3x}{\sin x}}\;dx{/tex}
{tex}=\int{\textstyle\frac{3\sin x-4\sin^3x}{\sin x}}\;dx{/tex}
{tex}=3\int dx-4\int\;\sin^2xdx{/tex}
Putting {tex}sin^2x=\frac{1-cos2x}2{/tex}
{tex}=3\int dx-4\int\;\frac{1-\cos2x}2dx{/tex}
{tex}=3\int dx-\frac42\int\;dx+\frac42\int\cos2x\;dx{/tex}
= 3x - 2x + 2 {tex}\frac{sin2x}2{/tex} +C {tex}\left\{\int\cos\;ax=\frac{\sin\;ax}a\right\}{/tex}
= x + sin2x +C

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}\int_0^{{\textstyle\frac12}\log3}\frac{e^x}{1+e^{2x}}\;dx{/tex} {tex}\int_0^{{\textstyle\frac12}\log3}\frac{e^x}{1+\left(e^x\right)^2}\;dx{/tex} Put e x = t, e x dx=dt Changing limit at x = 0, t = e 0 = 1 at x = {tex}\frac12\log3{/tex} , t = {tex}e^{{\textstyle\frac12}\log3}=e^{\log\sqrt3}=\sqrt3{/tex} So, {tex}\int_1^\sqrt3\frac{dt}{1+t^2}=\left[\tan^{-1}t\right]_1^\sqrt3{/tex} {tex}=\left[\tan^{-1}\sqrt3-\tan^{-1}1\right]=\frac\pi3-\frac\pi4{/tex} {tex}=\frac\pi{12}{/tex}
solution: {tex}\int_0^{{\textstyle\frac12}\log3}\frac{e^x}{1+e^{2x}}\;dx{/tex} {tex}\int_0^{{\textstyle\frac12}\log3}\frac{e^x}{1+\left(e^x\right)^2}\;dx{/tex} Put e x = t, e x dx=dt Changing limit at x = 0, t = e 0 = 1 at x = {tex}\frac12\log3{/tex} , t = {tex}e^{{\textstyle\frac12}\log3}=e^{\log\sqrt3}=\sqrt3{/tex} So, {tex}\int_1^\sqrt3\frac{dt}{1+t^2}=\left[\tan^{-1}t\right]_1^\sqrt3{/tex} {tex}=\left[\tan^{-1}\sqrt3-\tan^{-1}1\right]=\frac\pi3-\frac\pi4{/tex} {tex}=\frac\pi{12}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int_0^{{\textstyle\frac12}\log3}\frac{e^x}{1+e^{2x}}\;dx{/tex}
{tex}\int_0^{{\textstyle\frac12}\log3}\frac{e^x}{1+\left(e^x\right)^2}\;dx{/tex}
Put ex = t, exdx=dt
Changing limit
at x = 0, t = e0 = 1
at x = {tex}\frac12\log3{/tex}, t = {tex}e^{{\textstyle\frac12}\log3}=e^{\log\sqrt3}=\sqrt3{/tex}
So,
{tex}\int_1^\sqrt3\frac{dt}{1+t^2}=\left[\tan^{-1}t\right]_1^\sqrt3{/tex}
{tex}=\left[\tan^{-1}\sqrt3-\tan^{-1}1\right]=\frac\pi3-\frac\pi4{/tex}
{tex}=\frac\pi{12}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}I=\int \limits_0^1 \tan ^{-1} x \ d x{/tex} Using integration by parts Taking u = {tex}\tan^{-1}x{/tex} , v = 1 {tex}I=\tan^{-1}x\int1.dx-\int{\textstyle\frac1{1+x^2}}\left(\int1.dx\right)dx{/tex} {tex}I=x\tan^{-1}x-\int{\textstyle\frac x{1+x^2}}dx{/tex} {tex}I=x\tan^{-1}x-{\textstyle\frac12}\int{\textstyle\frac{2x}{1+x^2}}dx{/tex} {tex}I=\left[x\tan^{-1}x-{\textstyle\frac12}\log\left|1+x^2\right|\right]_0^1{/tex} {tex}I=1\tan^{-1}1-{\textstyle\frac12}\log\left|1+1\right|-0+{\textstyle\frac12}\log1{/tex} {tex}I=\frac\pi4-{\textstyle\frac12}\log\left|2\right|{/tex}
solution: {tex}I=\int \limits_0^1 \tan ^{-1} x \ d x{/tex} Using integration by parts Taking u = {tex}\tan^{-1}x{/tex} , v = 1 {tex}I=\tan^{-1}x\int1.dx-\int{\textstyle\frac1{1+x^2}}\left(\int1.dx\right)dx{/tex} {tex}I=x\tan^{-1}x-\int{\textstyle\frac x{1+x^2}}dx{/tex} {tex}I=x\tan^{-1}x-{\textstyle\frac12}\int{\textstyle\frac{2x}{1+x^2}}dx{/tex} {tex}I=\left[x\tan^{-1}x-{\textstyle\frac12}\log\left|1+x^2\right|\right]_0^1{/tex} {tex}I=1\tan^{-1}1-{\textstyle\frac12}\log\left|1+1\right|-0+{\textstyle\frac12}\log1{/tex} {tex}I=\frac\pi4-{\textstyle\frac12}\log\left|2\right|{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}I=\int \frac{2 x}{x^2+3 x+2} d x{/tex} {tex}I=\int\frac{2x}{\left(x+1\right)\left(x+2\right)}dx{/tex} Using partial fraction {tex}\frac{2x}{\left(x+1\right)\left(x+2\right)}=\frac A{\left(x+1\right)}+\frac B{\left(x+2\right)}{/tex} 2x = Ax + 2A + Bx + B 2x = (A + B)x + 2A + B On compairing A + B = 2, 2A + B = 0 A = -2 , B = 4 {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{2x}{\left(x+1\right)\left(x+2\right)}=\frac{-2}{\left(x+1\right)}+\frac4{\left(x+2\right)}{/tex} {tex}\int\frac{2xdx}{\left(x+1\right)\left(x+2\right)}=\int\frac{-2dx}{\left(x+1\right)}+\int\frac{4dx}{\left(x+2\right)}{/tex} {tex}I=-2\log\left|x+1\right|+4\log\left|x+2\right|+C{/tex}
solution: {tex}I=\int \frac{2 x}{x^2+3 x+2} d x{/tex} {tex}I=\int\frac{2x}{\left(x+1\right)\left(x+2\right)}dx{/tex} Using partial fraction {tex}\frac{2x}{\left(x+1\right)\left(x+2\right)}=\frac A{\left(x+1\right)}+\frac B{\left(x+2\right)}{/tex} 2x = Ax + 2A + Bx + B 2x = (A + B)x + 2A + B On compairing A + B = 2, 2A + B = 0 A = -2 , B = 4 {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{2x}{\left(x+1\right)\left(x+2\right)}=\frac{-2}{\left(x+1\right)}+\frac4{\left(x+2\right)}{/tex} {tex}\int\frac{2xdx}{\left(x+1\right)\left(x+2\right)}=\int\frac{-2dx}{\left(x+1\right)}+\int\frac{4dx}{\left(x+2\right)}{/tex} {tex}I=-2\log\left|x+1\right|+4\log\left|x+2\right|+C{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int \frac{2 x}{x^2+3 x+2} d x{/tex}
{tex}I=\int\frac{2x}{\left(x+1\right)\left(x+2\right)}dx{/tex}
Using partial fraction
{tex}\frac{2x}{\left(x+1\right)\left(x+2\right)}=\frac A{\left(x+1\right)}+\frac B{\left(x+2\right)}{/tex}
2x = Ax + 2A + Bx + B
2x = (A + B)x + 2A + B
On compairing
A + B = 2, 2A + B = 0
A = -2 , B = 4
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{2x}{\left(x+1\right)\left(x+2\right)}=\frac{-2}{\left(x+1\right)}+\frac4{\left(x+2\right)}{/tex}
{tex}\int\frac{2xdx}{\left(x+1\right)\left(x+2\right)}=\int\frac{-2dx}{\left(x+1\right)}+\int\frac{4dx}{\left(x+2\right)}{/tex}
{tex}I=-2\log\left|x+1\right|+4\log\left|x+2\right|+C{/tex}

explanation: Let I = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \frac{x}{\cos x+\sin x} d x{/tex} ...(i) {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \frac{\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right) d x}{\cos \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)+\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)}{/tex} { {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\int_0^a f(x)=\int_0^a f(a-x){/tex} } I = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \frac{\frac{\pi}{2}-x}{\sin x+\cos x}{/tex} ...(2) Add (1) & (2) 2I = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{x+\frac{\pi}{2}-x}{\sin x+\cos x} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \frac{\pi}{2(\sin x+\cos x)}{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4} \int_\limits0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{d x}{\sin x+\cos x}{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4} \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex} {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \frac{1 d x}{\sin x \cos \frac{\pi}{4}+\sin \frac{\pi}{4} \cos x}{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4 \sqrt{2}}{/tex} {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \frac{1 \cdot d x}{\sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4 \sqrt{2}}{/tex} {tex}\int_\limits0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \operatorname{cosec}\left(x+\frac{\pi}{14}\right) d x{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4 \sqrt{2}}{/tex} {tex}\left[\log \{ \operatorname{cosec}\left(x+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)\right.{/tex} - {tex}\left.\left.\omega t\left(x+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)\right\}\right]_0^{\pi / 2}{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4 \sqrt{2}}{/tex} {tex}[\log (\sqrt{2}+1)-\log (\sqrt{2}-1)]{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4 \sqrt{2}}{/tex} log {tex}\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}+1}{\sqrt{2}-1}\right){/tex}
solution: Let I = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \frac{x}{\cos x+\sin x} d x{/tex} ...(i) {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \frac{\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right) d x}{\cos \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)+\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)}{/tex} { {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\int_0^a f(x)=\int_0^a f(a-x){/tex} } I = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \frac{\frac{\pi}{2}-x}{\sin x+\cos x}{/tex} ...(2) Add (1) & (2) 2I = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{x+\frac{\pi}{2}-x}{\sin x+\cos x} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \frac{\pi}{2(\sin x+\cos x)}{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4} \int_\limits0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{d x}{\sin x+\cos x}{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4} \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex} {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \frac{1 d x}{\sin x \cos \frac{\pi}{4}+\sin \frac{\pi}{4} \cos x}{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4 \sqrt{2}}{/tex} {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \frac{1 \cdot d x}{\sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4 \sqrt{2}}{/tex} {tex}\int_\limits0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \operatorname{cosec}\left(x+\frac{\pi}{14}\right) d x{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4 \sqrt{2}}{/tex} {tex}\left[\log \{ \operatorname{cosec}\left(x+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)\right.{/tex} - {tex}\left.\left.\omega t\left(x+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)\right\}\right]_0^{\pi / 2}{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4 \sqrt{2}}{/tex} {tex}[\log (\sqrt{2}+1)-\log (\sqrt{2}-1)]{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4 \sqrt{2}}{/tex} log {tex}\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}+1}{\sqrt{2}-1}\right){/tex}
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \frac{x}{\cos x+\sin x} d x{/tex} ...(i)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \frac{\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right) d x}{\cos \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)+\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}-x\right)}{/tex} {{tex}\because{/tex}{tex}\int_0^a f(x)=\int_0^a f(a-x){/tex}}
I = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \frac{\frac{\pi}{2}-x}{\sin x+\cos x}{/tex} ...(2)
Add (1) & (2)
2I = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{x+\frac{\pi}{2}-x}{\sin x+\cos x} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \frac{\pi}{2(\sin x+\cos x)}{/tex}
I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4} \int_\limits0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{d x}{\sin x+\cos x}{/tex}
I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4} \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}{/tex} {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \frac{1 d x}{\sin x \cos \frac{\pi}{4}+\sin \frac{\pi}{4} \cos x}{/tex}
I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4 \sqrt{2}}{/tex} {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \frac{1 \cdot d x}{\sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)}{/tex}= {tex}\frac{\pi}{4 \sqrt{2}}{/tex}{tex}\int_\limits0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \operatorname{cosec}\left(x+\frac{\pi}{14}\right) d x{/tex}
I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4 \sqrt{2}}{/tex} {tex}\left[\log \{ \operatorname{cosec}\left(x+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)\right.{/tex} - {tex}\left.\left.\omega t\left(x+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)\right\}\right]_0^{\pi / 2}{/tex}
I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4 \sqrt{2}}{/tex}{tex}[\log (\sqrt{2}+1)-\log (\sqrt{2}-1)]{/tex}
I = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4 \sqrt{2}}{/tex} log{tex}\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}+1}{\sqrt{2}-1}\right){/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find: {tex}\int \frac{\sqrt{\cot x}}{\sin x \cos x} d x{/tex}
question_text: Find: {tex}\int \frac{\sqrt{\cot x}}{\sin x \cos x} d x{/tex}
explanation: {tex}\int\frac{\sqrt{cotx}}{\sin x.\cos x}dx{/tex} {tex}\int\frac{\sqrt{cotx}}{\sin x.\cos x}\frac{\sin x}{\sin x}dx{/tex} {tex}\int\frac{\sqrt{cotx}.\ cosec^2x}{cotx}dx{/tex} {tex}\int\frac{\ cosec^2x}{\sqrt{cotx}}dx{/tex} Put cos x = t -cosec 2 x dx = dt {tex}\int\frac{-dt}{\sqrt t}=-\int t^{\textstyle\frac{-1}2}dt{/tex} {tex}=\;\left[\frac{t\left(\frac{-1}2+1\right)}{\frac{-1}2+1}\right]+C=\frac{-\sqrt t}{\frac12}+C{/tex} Putting value of t {tex}=-2\sqrt{cot\;x}+C{/tex}
solution: {tex}\int\frac{\sqrt{cotx}}{\sin x.\cos x}dx{/tex} {tex}\int\frac{\sqrt{cotx}}{\sin x.\cos x}\frac{\sin x}{\sin x}dx{/tex} {tex}\int\frac{\sqrt{cotx}.\ cosec^2x}{cotx}dx{/tex} {tex}\int\frac{\ cosec^2x}{\sqrt{cotx}}dx{/tex} Put cos x = t -cosec 2 x dx = dt {tex}\int\frac{-dt}{\sqrt t}=-\int t^{\textstyle\frac{-1}2}dt{/tex} {tex}=\;\left[\frac{t\left(\frac{-1}2+1\right)}{\frac{-1}2+1}\right]+C=\frac{-\sqrt t}{\frac12}+C{/tex} Putting value of t {tex}=-2\sqrt{cot\;x}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int\frac{\sqrt{cotx}}{\sin x.\cos x}dx{/tex}
{tex}\int\frac{\sqrt{cotx}}{\sin x.\cos x}\frac{\sin x}{\sin x}dx{/tex}
{tex}\int\frac{\sqrt{cotx}.\ cosec^2x}{cotx}dx{/tex}
{tex}\int\frac{\ cosec^2x}{\sqrt{cotx}}dx{/tex}
Put cos x = t
-cosec2x dx = dt
{tex}\int\frac{-dt}{\sqrt t}=-\int t^{\textstyle\frac{-1}2}dt{/tex}
{tex}=\;\left[\frac{t\left(\frac{-1}2+1\right)}{\frac{-1}2+1}\right]+C=\frac{-\sqrt t}{\frac12}+C{/tex}
Putting value of t
{tex}=-2\sqrt{cot\;x}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Using the partial fraction method Let, {tex}\frac1{x\left(x^2+4\right)}=\frac Ax+\frac{Bx+C}{x^2+4}{/tex} {tex}\frac1{x\left(x^2+4\right)}=\frac{Ax^2+4A+Bx^2+Cx}{x\left(x^2+4\right)}{/tex} On comparing 1 = Ax² +4A + Bx² + Cx 4A = 1 , A = {tex}\frac14{/tex} A + B = 0, B = {tex}\frac{-1}4{/tex} , C = 0 {tex}\therefore\int\frac1{x\left(x^2+4\right)}=\int\frac1{4x}dx\;+\int\frac{\frac{-x}4}{x^2+4}dx{/tex} {tex}\int\frac1{x\left(x^2+4\right)}=\frac14\int\frac1xdx\;-\frac1{4\times2}\int\frac{2x}{x^2+4}dx{/tex} {tex}\int\frac1{x\left(x^2+4\right)}=\frac14\log\;x\;-\frac18\log\left|x^2+4\right|+C{/tex}
solution: Using the partial fraction method Let, {tex}\frac1{x\left(x^2+4\right)}=\frac Ax+\frac{Bx+C}{x^2+4}{/tex} {tex}\frac1{x\left(x^2+4\right)}=\frac{Ax^2+4A+Bx^2+Cx}{x\left(x^2+4\right)}{/tex} On comparing 1 = Ax² +4A + Bx² + Cx 4A = 1 , A = {tex}\frac14{/tex} A + B = 0, B = {tex}\frac{-1}4{/tex} , C = 0 {tex}\therefore\int\frac1{x\left(x^2+4\right)}=\int\frac1{4x}dx\;+\int\frac{\frac{-x}4}{x^2+4}dx{/tex} {tex}\int\frac1{x\left(x^2+4\right)}=\frac14\int\frac1xdx\;-\frac1{4\times2}\int\frac{2x}{x^2+4}dx{/tex} {tex}\int\frac1{x\left(x^2+4\right)}=\frac14\log\;x\;-\frac18\log\left|x^2+4\right|+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Using the partial fraction method
Let, {tex}\frac1{x\left(x^2+4\right)}=\frac Ax+\frac{Bx+C}{x^2+4}{/tex}
{tex}\frac1{x\left(x^2+4\right)}=\frac{Ax^2+4A+Bx^2+Cx}{x\left(x^2+4\right)}{/tex}
On comparing
1 = Ax² +4A + Bx² + Cx
4A = 1 , A = {tex}\frac14{/tex}
A + B = 0, B = {tex}\frac{-1}4{/tex}, C = 0
{tex}\therefore\int\frac1{x\left(x^2+4\right)}=\int\frac1{4x}dx\;+\int\frac{\frac{-x}4}{x^2+4}dx{/tex}
{tex}\int\frac1{x\left(x^2+4\right)}=\frac14\int\frac1xdx\;-\frac1{4\times2}\int\frac{2x}{x^2+4}dx{/tex}
{tex}\int\frac1{x\left(x^2+4\right)}=\frac14\log\;x\;-\frac18\log\left|x^2+4\right|+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}I=\int \limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{d x}{1+e^{\sin x}}{/tex} ...(i) Using property, {tex}\int_\limits0^af\left(x\right)dx=\int_\limits0^af\left(a-x\right)dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}\frac{dx}{1+e^{\sin\left(2\pi-x\right)}}=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}{\textstyle\frac{dx}{1+e^{-\sin x}}}{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}\frac{dx}{1+\frac1{e^{\sin x}}}=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}{\textstyle\frac{e^{\sin x}dx}{e^{\sin x}+1}}{/tex} ...(ii) Adding eqn (i) and (ii) {tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}\frac{dx}{1+e^{\sin x}}+\int_\limits0^{2\pi}{\textstyle\frac{e^{\sin x}dx}{e^{\sin x}+1}}{/tex} {tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}\frac{\left(1+e^{\sin x}\right)dx}{1+e^{\sin x}}{/tex} {tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}dx=\left[x\right]_0^{2\pi}{/tex} {tex}2I=\left[2\pi-0\right]{/tex} {tex}I=\pi{/tex}
solution: {tex}I=\int \limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{d x}{1+e^{\sin x}}{/tex} ...(i) Using property, {tex}\int_\limits0^af\left(x\right)dx=\int_\limits0^af\left(a-x\right)dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}\frac{dx}{1+e^{\sin\left(2\pi-x\right)}}=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}{\textstyle\frac{dx}{1+e^{-\sin x}}}{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}\frac{dx}{1+\frac1{e^{\sin x}}}=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}{\textstyle\frac{e^{\sin x}dx}{e^{\sin x}+1}}{/tex} ...(ii) Adding eqn (i) and (ii) {tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}\frac{dx}{1+e^{\sin x}}+\int_\limits0^{2\pi}{\textstyle\frac{e^{\sin x}dx}{e^{\sin x}+1}}{/tex} {tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}\frac{\left(1+e^{\sin x}\right)dx}{1+e^{\sin x}}{/tex} {tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}dx=\left[x\right]_0^{2\pi}{/tex} {tex}2I=\left[2\pi-0\right]{/tex} {tex}I=\pi{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int \limits_0^{2 \pi} \frac{d x}{1+e^{\sin x}}{/tex} ...(i)
Using property, {tex}\int_\limits0^af\left(x\right)dx=\int_\limits0^af\left(a-x\right)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}\frac{dx}{1+e^{\sin\left(2\pi-x\right)}}=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}{\textstyle\frac{dx}{1+e^{-\sin x}}}{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}\frac{dx}{1+\frac1{e^{\sin x}}}=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}{\textstyle\frac{e^{\sin x}dx}{e^{\sin x}+1}}{/tex} ...(ii)
Adding eqn (i) and (ii)
{tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}\frac{dx}{1+e^{\sin x}}+\int_\limits0^{2\pi}{\textstyle\frac{e^{\sin x}dx}{e^{\sin x}+1}}{/tex}
{tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}\frac{\left(1+e^{\sin x}\right)dx}{1+e^{\sin x}}{/tex}
{tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{2\pi}dx=\left[x\right]_0^{2\pi}{/tex}
{tex}2I=\left[2\pi-0\right]{/tex}
{tex}I=\pi{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}I=\int\sqrt{5-4x-x^2}\;dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int\sqrt{-\left(x^2+4x+4-4-5\right)}\;dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int\sqrt{-\left(x+2\right)^2+9}\;dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int\sqrt{\left(3\right)^2-\left(x+2\right)^2}\;dx{/tex} As, {tex}\int\sqrt{\left(a\right)^2-\left(x\right)^2}\;dx{/tex} {tex}=\frac x2\sqrt{a^2-x^2}+\frac{a^2}2\sin^{-1}{\textstyle\frac xa}+C{/tex} So, {tex}\int\sqrt{\left(3\right)^2-\left(x+2\right)^2}\;dx{/tex} {tex}=\frac{x+2}2\sqrt{5-4x-x^2}+\frac92\sin^{-1}{\textstyle\frac{x+2}3}+C{/tex}
solution: {tex}I=\int\sqrt{5-4x-x^2}\;dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int\sqrt{-\left(x^2+4x+4-4-5\right)}\;dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int\sqrt{-\left(x+2\right)^2+9}\;dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int\sqrt{\left(3\right)^2-\left(x+2\right)^2}\;dx{/tex} As, {tex}\int\sqrt{\left(a\right)^2-\left(x\right)^2}\;dx{/tex} {tex}=\frac x2\sqrt{a^2-x^2}+\frac{a^2}2\sin^{-1}{\textstyle\frac xa}+C{/tex} So, {tex}\int\sqrt{\left(3\right)^2-\left(x+2\right)^2}\;dx{/tex} {tex}=\frac{x+2}2\sqrt{5-4x-x^2}+\frac92\sin^{-1}{\textstyle\frac{x+2}3}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int\sqrt{5-4x-x^2}\;dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int\sqrt{-\left(x^2+4x+4-4-5\right)}\;dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int\sqrt{-\left(x+2\right)^2+9}\;dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int\sqrt{\left(3\right)^2-\left(x+2\right)^2}\;dx{/tex}
As, {tex}\int\sqrt{\left(a\right)^2-\left(x\right)^2}\;dx{/tex} {tex}=\frac x2\sqrt{a^2-x^2}+\frac{a^2}2\sin^{-1}{\textstyle\frac xa}+C{/tex}
So, {tex}\int\sqrt{\left(3\right)^2-\left(x+2\right)^2}\;dx{/tex} {tex}=\frac{x+2}2\sqrt{5-4x-x^2}+\frac92\sin^{-1}{\textstyle\frac{x+2}3}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}I=\int \limits_{\frac{-\pi}{2}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(\sin |x|+\cos |x|) d x{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} I is a even function i.e. f(x) = f(-x) Using property, {tex}\int_\limits{-a}^af\left(x\right)dx=2\int_\limits0^af\left(x\right)dx{/tex} {tex}I=2\int_\limits0^{\frac\pi2}\left(\sin\left|x\right|+\cos\left|x\right|\right)dx{/tex} {tex}I=2\left[-\cos x+\sin x\right]_0^{\frac\pi2}{/tex} {tex}I=2\left[-\cos\frac\pi2+\sin\frac\pi2+\cos0-\sin0\right]{/tex} {tex}I=2\left[0+1+1-0\right]=\;2\times2=4{/tex} I = 4
solution: {tex}I=\int \limits_{\frac{-\pi}{2}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(\sin |x|+\cos |x|) d x{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} I is a even function i.e. f(x) = f(-x) Using property, {tex}\int_\limits{-a}^af\left(x\right)dx=2\int_\limits0^af\left(x\right)dx{/tex} {tex}I=2\int_\limits0^{\frac\pi2}\left(\sin\left|x\right|+\cos\left|x\right|\right)dx{/tex} {tex}I=2\left[-\cos x+\sin x\right]_0^{\frac\pi2}{/tex} {tex}I=2\left[-\cos\frac\pi2+\sin\frac\pi2+\cos0-\sin0\right]{/tex} {tex}I=2\left[0+1+1-0\right]=\;2\times2=4{/tex} I = 4
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int \limits_{\frac{-\pi}{2}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(\sin |x|+\cos |x|) d x{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} I is a even function i.e. f(x) = f(-x)
Using property, {tex}\int_\limits{-a}^af\left(x\right)dx=2\int_\limits0^af\left(x\right)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=2\int_\limits0^{\frac\pi2}\left(\sin\left|x\right|+\cos\left|x\right|\right)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=2\left[-\cos x+\sin x\right]_0^{\frac\pi2}{/tex}
{tex}I=2\left[-\cos\frac\pi2+\sin\frac\pi2+\cos0-\sin0\right]{/tex}
{tex}I=2\left[0+1+1-0\right]=\;2\times2=4{/tex}
I = 4

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}I=\int \limits_1^4\{|x|+|3-x|\} d x{/tex} ​​​​​ |3 - x| = -(3 - x) for 1 and 2 |3 - x| = (3 - x) for 3 and 4 So, {tex}I=\int\limits_1^4xdx+\int_1^2-(3-x)dx+\int_3^4(3-x)dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int\limits_1^4xdx-\int_1^2(3-x)dx+\int_3^4(3-x)dx{/tex} {tex}I=\left[\frac{x^2}2\right]_1^4-\left[3x-\frac{x^2}2\right]_1^2+\left[3x-\frac{x^2}2\right]_3^4{/tex} {tex}I=\left[{\textstyle\frac{16}2}-{\textstyle\frac12}\right]-\left[6-{\textstyle\frac42}-3+{\textstyle\frac12}\right]+{/tex} {tex}\left[12-{\textstyle\frac{16}2}-9+{\textstyle\frac92}\right]{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{11}2{/tex} ​​​​​​​
solution: {tex}I=\int \limits_1^4\{|x|+|3-x|\} d x{/tex} ​​​​​ |3 - x| = -(3 - x) for 1 and 2 |3 - x| = (3 - x) for 3 and 4 So, {tex}I=\int\limits_1^4xdx+\int_1^2-(3-x)dx+\int_3^4(3-x)dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int\limits_1^4xdx-\int_1^2(3-x)dx+\int_3^4(3-x)dx{/tex} {tex}I=\left[\frac{x^2}2\right]_1^4-\left[3x-\frac{x^2}2\right]_1^2+\left[3x-\frac{x^2}2\right]_3^4{/tex} {tex}I=\left[{\textstyle\frac{16}2}-{\textstyle\frac12}\right]-\left[6-{\textstyle\frac42}-3+{\textstyle\frac12}\right]+{/tex} {tex}\left[12-{\textstyle\frac{16}2}-9+{\textstyle\frac92}\right]{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{11}2{/tex} ​​​​​​​
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int \limits_1^4\{|x|+|3-x|\} d x{/tex} ​​​​​
|3 - x| = -(3 - x) for 1 and 2
|3 - x| = (3 - x) for 3 and 4
So, {tex}I=\int\limits_1^4xdx+\int_1^2-(3-x)dx+\int_3^4(3-x)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int\limits_1^4xdx-\int_1^2(3-x)dx+\int_3^4(3-x)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\left[\frac{x^2}2\right]_1^4-\left[3x-\frac{x^2}2\right]_1^2+\left[3x-\frac{x^2}2\right]_3^4{/tex}
{tex}I=\left[{\textstyle\frac{16}2}-{\textstyle\frac12}\right]-\left[6-{\textstyle\frac42}-3+{\textstyle\frac12}\right]+{/tex} {tex}\left[12-{\textstyle\frac{16}2}-9+{\textstyle\frac92}\right]{/tex}
I = {tex}\frac{11}2{/tex}​​​​​​​

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2}(2 \log \cos x{/tex} - {tex}\log \sin 2 x) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2}(2 \log \cos x{/tex} - {tex}\log 2 \sin x \cos x) d x{/tex} { {tex}\because sin2x=2sin x\ cos x{/tex} } = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} 2 \log \cos x d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log 2 d x {/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \sin x d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \cos x d x{/tex} { {tex}\because{/tex} log(ab) = loga - logb} = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \cos x d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log 2 d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log [\sin (\pi / 2-x)] d x{/tex} { {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\int_\limits0^a f(x){/tex} = {tex}\int_\limits0^a f(a-x) d x{/tex} } = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \cos x d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log 2 d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \cos x d x{/tex} = - {tex}\log2\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} d x{/tex} = - {tex}\log 2[x]_0^{\pi / 2}{/tex} = - {tex}\log 2\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-0\right){/tex} = - {tex}\frac{\pi}{2} \log 2{/tex}
solution: {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2}(2 \log \cos x{/tex} - {tex}\log \sin 2 x) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2}(2 \log \cos x{/tex} - {tex}\log 2 \sin x \cos x) d x{/tex} { {tex}\because sin2x=2sin x\ cos x{/tex} } = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} 2 \log \cos x d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log 2 d x {/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \sin x d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \cos x d x{/tex} { {tex}\because{/tex} log(ab) = loga - logb} = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \cos x d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log 2 d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log [\sin (\pi / 2-x)] d x{/tex} { {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\int_\limits0^a f(x){/tex} = {tex}\int_\limits0^a f(a-x) d x{/tex} } = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \cos x d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log 2 d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \cos x d x{/tex} = - {tex}\log2\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} d x{/tex} = - {tex}\log 2[x]_0^{\pi / 2}{/tex} = - {tex}\log 2\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-0\right){/tex} = - {tex}\frac{\pi}{2} \log 2{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2}(2 \log \cos x{/tex} - {tex}\log \sin 2 x) d x{/tex}
= {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2}(2 \log \cos x{/tex} - {tex}\log 2 \sin x \cos x) d x{/tex} {{tex}\because sin2x=2sin x\ cos x{/tex}}
= {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} 2 \log \cos x d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log 2 d x {/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \sin x d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \cos x d x{/tex} {{tex}\because{/tex} log(ab) = loga - logb}
= {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \cos x d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log 2 d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log [\sin (\pi / 2-x)] d x{/tex} {{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\int_\limits0^a f(x){/tex} = {tex}\int_\limits0^a f(a-x) d x{/tex}}
= {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \cos x d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log 2 d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \cos x d x{/tex}
= - {tex}\log2\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} d x{/tex} = - {tex}\log 2[x]_0^{\pi / 2}{/tex}
= - {tex}\log 2\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-0\right){/tex} = - {tex}\frac{\pi}{2} \log 2{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}I=\int_0^\pi\frac{xdx}{16\cos^2x+9\sin^2x}{/tex} ...(i) {tex}\int_a^bf\left(x\right)dx=\int_a^bf\left(a+b-x\right)dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_0^\pi\frac{\left(\pi-x\right)dx}{16\cos^2\left(\pi-x\right)+9\sin^2\left(\pi-x\right)}{/tex} {tex}I=\int_0^\pi\frac{\left(\pi-x\right)dx}{16\cos^2x+9\sin^2x}{/tex} ...(ii) Add eqn (i) and (ii) {tex}2I=\int_0^\pi\frac{\pi-x+x}{16\cos^2x+9\sin^2x}dx{/tex} {tex}I=\frac\pi{2\times9}\int_0^\pi\frac1{\frac{16}9\cos^2x+\sin^2x}dx{/tex} Dividing by cos 2 x both sides {tex}I=\frac\pi{18}\int_0^\pi\frac{sec^2x}{\left(\frac43\right)^2+\tan^2x}dx{/tex} Put tan x = t, sec 2 x dx = dt {tex}I=\frac\pi{18}\int_0^\infty\frac{dt}{\left(\frac43\right)^2+t^2}{/tex} {tex}I=\frac\pi{18}\left[\tan^{-1}\left(\frac t{\frac43}\right)\right]_0^\infty{/tex} {tex}I=\frac\pi{18}\left[\tan^{-1}\infty-\tan^{-1}0\right]{/tex} {tex}I=\frac\pi{18}\left[\frac\pi2-0\right]{/tex} {tex}I=\frac{\pi^2}{36}{/tex}
solution: {tex}I=\int_0^\pi\frac{xdx}{16\cos^2x+9\sin^2x}{/tex} ...(i) {tex}\int_a^bf\left(x\right)dx=\int_a^bf\left(a+b-x\right)dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_0^\pi\frac{\left(\pi-x\right)dx}{16\cos^2\left(\pi-x\right)+9\sin^2\left(\pi-x\right)}{/tex} {tex}I=\int_0^\pi\frac{\left(\pi-x\right)dx}{16\cos^2x+9\sin^2x}{/tex} ...(ii) Add eqn (i) and (ii) {tex}2I=\int_0^\pi\frac{\pi-x+x}{16\cos^2x+9\sin^2x}dx{/tex} {tex}I=\frac\pi{2\times9}\int_0^\pi\frac1{\frac{16}9\cos^2x+\sin^2x}dx{/tex} Dividing by cos 2 x both sides {tex}I=\frac\pi{18}\int_0^\pi\frac{sec^2x}{\left(\frac43\right)^2+\tan^2x}dx{/tex} Put tan x = t, sec 2 x dx = dt {tex}I=\frac\pi{18}\int_0^\infty\frac{dt}{\left(\frac43\right)^2+t^2}{/tex} {tex}I=\frac\pi{18}\left[\tan^{-1}\left(\frac t{\frac43}\right)\right]_0^\infty{/tex} {tex}I=\frac\pi{18}\left[\tan^{-1}\infty-\tan^{-1}0\right]{/tex} {tex}I=\frac\pi{18}\left[\frac\pi2-0\right]{/tex} {tex}I=\frac{\pi^2}{36}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int_0^\pi\frac{xdx}{16\cos^2x+9\sin^2x}{/tex} ...(i)
{tex}\int_a^bf\left(x\right)dx=\int_a^bf\left(a+b-x\right)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_0^\pi\frac{\left(\pi-x\right)dx}{16\cos^2\left(\pi-x\right)+9\sin^2\left(\pi-x\right)}{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_0^\pi\frac{\left(\pi-x\right)dx}{16\cos^2x+9\sin^2x}{/tex} ...(ii)
Add eqn (i) and (ii)
{tex}2I=\int_0^\pi\frac{\pi-x+x}{16\cos^2x+9\sin^2x}dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac\pi{2\times9}\int_0^\pi\frac1{\frac{16}9\cos^2x+\sin^2x}dx{/tex}
Dividing by cos2x both sides
{tex}I=\frac\pi{18}\int_0^\pi\frac{sec^2x}{\left(\frac43\right)^2+\tan^2x}dx{/tex}
Put tan x = t, sec2x dx = dt
{tex}I=\frac\pi{18}\int_0^\infty\frac{dt}{\left(\frac43\right)^2+t^2}{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac\pi{18}\left[\tan^{-1}\left(\frac t{\frac43}\right)\right]_0^\infty{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac\pi{18}\left[\tan^{-1}\infty-\tan^{-1}0\right]{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac\pi{18}\left[\frac\pi2-0\right]{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac{\pi^2}{36}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}I=\int \limits_{-3}^3 \frac{x^4}{1+e^x} d x{/tex} Using {tex}\int_\limits a^bf(x)dx=\int_a^bf(a+b-x)dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits{-3}^3\frac{\left(-x\right)^4}{\left(1+e^{-x}\right)}dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits{-3}^3\frac{e^x.\left(x\right)^4}{\left(1+e^x\right)}dx=\int_\limits{-3}^3\frac{\left(e^x+1-1\right)x⁴}{e^x+1}dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits{-3}^3\frac{\left(e^x+1\right).\left(x\right)^4}{\left(1+e^x\right)}dx-\int_\limits{{}^{-3}}^3\frac{x^4}{e^x+1}dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits{-3}^3x^4dx-I{/tex} {tex}2I=\left[\frac{x^5}5\right]_{-3}^3{/tex} {tex}2I=\frac15\left[\left(3\right)^5-\left(-3\right)^5\right]{/tex} {tex}I=\frac{243}5{/tex}
solution: {tex}I=\int \limits_{-3}^3 \frac{x^4}{1+e^x} d x{/tex} Using {tex}\int_\limits a^bf(x)dx=\int_a^bf(a+b-x)dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits{-3}^3\frac{\left(-x\right)^4}{\left(1+e^{-x}\right)}dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits{-3}^3\frac{e^x.\left(x\right)^4}{\left(1+e^x\right)}dx=\int_\limits{-3}^3\frac{\left(e^x+1-1\right)x⁴}{e^x+1}dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits{-3}^3\frac{\left(e^x+1\right).\left(x\right)^4}{\left(1+e^x\right)}dx-\int_\limits{{}^{-3}}^3\frac{x^4}{e^x+1}dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int_\limits{-3}^3x^4dx-I{/tex} {tex}2I=\left[\frac{x^5}5\right]_{-3}^3{/tex} {tex}2I=\frac15\left[\left(3\right)^5-\left(-3\right)^5\right]{/tex} {tex}I=\frac{243}5{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}I=\int \limits_{-3}^3 \frac{x^4}{1+e^x} d x{/tex}
Using {tex}\int_\limits a^bf(x)dx=\int_a^bf(a+b-x)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_\limits{-3}^3\frac{\left(-x\right)^4}{\left(1+e^{-x}\right)}dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_\limits{-3}^3\frac{e^x.\left(x\right)^4}{\left(1+e^x\right)}dx=\int_\limits{-3}^3\frac{\left(e^x+1-1\right)x⁴}{e^x+1}dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_\limits{-3}^3\frac{\left(e^x+1\right).\left(x\right)^4}{\left(1+e^x\right)}dx-\int_\limits{{}^{-3}}^3\frac{x^4}{e^x+1}dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_\limits{-3}^3x^4dx-I{/tex}
{tex}2I=\left[\frac{x^5}5\right]_{-3}^3{/tex}
{tex}2I=\frac15\left[\left(3\right)^5-\left(-3\right)^5\right]{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac{243}5{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: {tex}\int\frac1{\sqrt{4x-x^2}}dx=\int\frac1{\sqrt{-\left(x^2-4x\right)}}dx{/tex} {tex}=\int\frac1{\sqrt{-\left(x^2-4x+4-4\right)}}dx=\int\frac1{-\left(x-2\right)^2+4}dx{/tex} {tex}=\int\frac1{\left(2\right)^2-\left(x-2\right)^2}dx{/tex} Put x - 2 = t, dx = dt {tex}=\int\frac{dt}{\left(2\right)^2-\left(t\right)^2}{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\int\frac{dx}{\left(a\right)^2-\left(x\right)^2}=\sin^{-1}\left(\frac xa\right)+C{/tex} So, {tex}\int\frac{dt}{\left(2\right)^2-\left(t\right)^2}=\sin^{-1}\left(\frac t2\right)+C{/tex} Putting value of t {tex}=\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{x-2}2\right)+C{/tex}
solution: {tex}\int\frac1{\sqrt{4x-x^2}}dx=\int\frac1{\sqrt{-\left(x^2-4x\right)}}dx{/tex} {tex}=\int\frac1{\sqrt{-\left(x^2-4x+4-4\right)}}dx=\int\frac1{-\left(x-2\right)^2+4}dx{/tex} {tex}=\int\frac1{\left(2\right)^2-\left(x-2\right)^2}dx{/tex} Put x - 2 = t, dx = dt {tex}=\int\frac{dt}{\left(2\right)^2-\left(t\right)^2}{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\int\frac{dx}{\left(a\right)^2-\left(x\right)^2}=\sin^{-1}\left(\frac xa\right)+C{/tex} So, {tex}\int\frac{dt}{\left(2\right)^2-\left(t\right)^2}=\sin^{-1}\left(\frac t2\right)+C{/tex} Putting value of t {tex}=\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{x-2}2\right)+C{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\int\frac1{\sqrt{4x-x^2}}dx=\int\frac1{\sqrt{-\left(x^2-4x\right)}}dx{/tex}
{tex}=\int\frac1{\sqrt{-\left(x^2-4x+4-4\right)}}dx=\int\frac1{-\left(x-2\right)^2+4}dx{/tex}
{tex}=\int\frac1{\left(2\right)^2-\left(x-2\right)^2}dx{/tex}
Put x - 2 = t, dx = dt
{tex}=\int\frac{dt}{\left(2\right)^2-\left(t\right)^2}{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\int\frac{dx}{\left(a\right)^2-\left(x\right)^2}=\sin^{-1}\left(\frac xa\right)+C{/tex}
So, {tex}\int\frac{dt}{\left(2\right)^2-\left(t\right)^2}=\sin^{-1}\left(\frac t2\right)+C{/tex}
Putting value of t
{tex}=\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{x-2}2\right)+C{/tex}
