subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let I = {tex}\int_{-\pi}^{\pi}{/tex} (cos ax - sin bx) 2 dx, then we have I = {tex}\int_{-\pi}^{\pi}{/tex} (cos 2 ax + sin 2 bx - 2 cos ax sin bx) dx = {tex}\int_{-\pi}^{\pi}{/tex} cos 2 ax dx + {tex}\int_{-\pi}^{\pi}{/tex} sin 2 bx dx - {tex}\int_{-\pi}^{\pi}{/tex} 2 cos ax sin bx dx = 2 {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} cos 2 ax dx + 2 {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} sin 2 bx dx - 0 [since cos 2 ax and sin 2 bx are even functions and cos ax sin bx is an odd function.] {tex}\therefore ~I=2 \int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{1+\cos 2 a x}{2} d x+2 \int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{1-\cos 2 b x}{2} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} (1 + cos 2 ax) dx + {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} (1 - cos 2 bx) dx = {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} (1 + cos 2 ax + 1 - cos 2 bx) dx = {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} (2 + cos 2 ax - cos 2 bx) dx {tex}=2[x]_{0}^{\pi}+\left[\frac{\sin 2 a x}{2 a}\right]_{0}^{\pi}-\left[\frac{\sin 2 b x}{2 b}\right]_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} {tex}=2 \pi+\frac{\sin 2 a \pi}{2 a}-\frac{\sin 2 b \pi}{2 b}{/tex}
solution: Let I = {tex}\int_{-\pi}^{\pi}{/tex} (cos ax - sin bx) 2 dx, then we have I = {tex}\int_{-\pi}^{\pi}{/tex} (cos 2 ax + sin 2 bx - 2 cos ax sin bx) dx = {tex}\int_{-\pi}^{\pi}{/tex} cos 2 ax dx + {tex}\int_{-\pi}^{\pi}{/tex} sin 2 bx dx - {tex}\int_{-\pi}^{\pi}{/tex} 2 cos ax sin bx dx = 2 {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} cos 2 ax dx + 2 {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} sin 2 bx dx - 0 [since cos 2 ax and sin 2 bx are even functions and cos ax sin bx is an odd function.] {tex}\therefore ~I=2 \int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{1+\cos 2 a x}{2} d x+2 \int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{1-\cos 2 b x}{2} d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} (1 + cos 2 ax) dx + {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} (1 - cos 2 bx) dx = {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} (1 + cos 2 ax + 1 - cos 2 bx) dx = {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} (2 + cos 2 ax - cos 2 bx) dx {tex}=2[x]_{0}^{\pi}+\left[\frac{\sin 2 a x}{2 a}\right]_{0}^{\pi}-\left[\frac{\sin 2 b x}{2 b}\right]_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} {tex}=2 \pi+\frac{\sin 2 a \pi}{2 a}-\frac{\sin 2 b \pi}{2 b}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int_{-\pi}^{\pi}{/tex} (cos ax - sin bx)2 dx, then we have
I = {tex}\int_{-\pi}^{\pi}{/tex} (cos2ax + sin2bx - 2 cos ax sin bx) dx
= {tex}\int_{-\pi}^{\pi}{/tex} cos2 ax dx + {tex}\int_{-\pi}^{\pi}{/tex} sin2 bx dx - {tex}\int_{-\pi}^{\pi}{/tex} 2 cos ax sin bx dx
= 2 {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} cos2 ax dx + 2 {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} sin2 bx dx - 0     [since cos2 ax and sin2 bx are even functions and cos ax sin bx is an odd function.]
{tex}\therefore ~I=2 \int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{1+\cos 2 a x}{2} d x+2 \int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{1-\cos 2 b x}{2} d x{/tex}
= {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} (1 + cos 2 ax) dx + {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} (1 - cos 2 bx) dx
= {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} (1 + cos 2 ax + 1 - cos 2 bx) dx
= {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} (2 + cos 2 ax - cos 2 bx) dx
{tex}=2[x]_{0}^{\pi}+\left[\frac{\sin 2 a x}{2 a}\right]_{0}^{\pi}-\left[\frac{\sin 2 b x}{2 b}\right]_{0}^{\pi}{/tex}
{tex}=2 \pi+\frac{\sin 2 a \pi}{2 a}-\frac{\sin 2 b \pi}{2 b}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Given, {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{3}\left(x^{5}+1\right)^{\frac{3}{5}}}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{3} \cdot x^{3}\left(1+\frac{1}{x^{5}}\right)^{\frac{3}{5}}}{/tex} (take x 5 common from denominator) = {tex}\int \frac{x^{-6} d x}{\left(1+\frac{1}{x^{5}}\right)^{\frac{3}{5}}}{/tex} Let {tex}1+\frac{1}{x^{5}}{/tex} = t -5x -6 dx = dt or x -6 dx = {tex}\frac{d t}{-5}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{d t}{-5(t)^{\frac{3}{5}}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1}{5} \int t^{\frac{-3}{5}} d t{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1}{5} \times \frac{t^{\frac{-3}{5}+1}}{\frac{-3}{5}+1}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1}{5} \frac{t^{\frac{2}{5}}}{\frac{2}{5}}=\frac{-t^{\frac{2}{5}}}{2}{/tex} + C Replacing valuye of t = {tex}1+\frac{1}{x^{5}}{/tex} , we get: I = {tex}\frac{-\left(1+\frac{1}{x^{5}}\right)^{\frac{2}{5}}}{2}{/tex} + C
solution: Given, {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{3}\left(x^{5}+1\right)^{\frac{3}{5}}}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{3} \cdot x^{3}\left(1+\frac{1}{x^{5}}\right)^{\frac{3}{5}}}{/tex} (take x 5 common from denominator) = {tex}\int \frac{x^{-6} d x}{\left(1+\frac{1}{x^{5}}\right)^{\frac{3}{5}}}{/tex} Let {tex}1+\frac{1}{x^{5}}{/tex} = t -5x -6 dx = dt or x -6 dx = {tex}\frac{d t}{-5}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{d t}{-5(t)^{\frac{3}{5}}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1}{5} \int t^{\frac{-3}{5}} d t{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1}{5} \times \frac{t^{\frac{-3}{5}+1}}{\frac{-3}{5}+1}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1}{5} \frac{t^{\frac{2}{5}}}{\frac{2}{5}}=\frac{-t^{\frac{2}{5}}}{2}{/tex} + C Replacing valuye of t = {tex}1+\frac{1}{x^{5}}{/tex} , we get: I = {tex}\frac{-\left(1+\frac{1}{x^{5}}\right)^{\frac{2}{5}}}{2}{/tex} + C
solution_html:
Given,
{tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{3}\left(x^{5}+1\right)^{\frac{3}{5}}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\int \frac{d x}{x^{3} \cdot x^{3}\left(1+\frac{1}{x^{5}}\right)^{\frac{3}{5}}}{/tex} (take x5 common from denominator)
= {tex}\int \frac{x^{-6} d x}{\left(1+\frac{1}{x^{5}}\right)^{\frac{3}{5}}}{/tex} 
Let {tex}1+\frac{1}{x^{5}}{/tex} = t
-5x-6 dx = dt
or x-6 dx = {tex}\frac{d t}{-5}{/tex} 
= {tex}\int \frac{d t}{-5(t)^{\frac{3}{5}}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{-1}{5} \int t^{\frac{-3}{5}} d t{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{-1}{5} \times \frac{t^{\frac{-3}{5}+1}}{\frac{-3}{5}+1}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1}{5} \frac{t^{\frac{2}{5}}}{\frac{2}{5}}=\frac{-t^{\frac{2}{5}}}{2}{/tex} + C
Replacing valuye of t =  {tex}1+\frac{1}{x^{5}}{/tex} , we get:
I = {tex}\frac{-\left(1+\frac{1}{x^{5}}\right)^{\frac{2}{5}}}{2}{/tex} + C

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let the given integral be, l = {tex}\int \frac{(x+3) e^{x}}{(x+5)^{3}} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int\left[\frac{(x+5)-2}{(x+5)^{3}}\right] e^{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int\left[\frac{1}{(x+5)^{2}}+\frac{-2}{(x+5)^{3}}\right] e^{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1}{(x+5)^{2}} e^{x} d x{/tex} - {tex}\int \frac{2}{(x+5)^{3}} e^{x} d x{/tex} using parts method for 1st part and keep 2nd part as it is we get. {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{(x+5)^{2}} \int e^{x} d x{/tex} - {tex}\int\left[\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{1}{(x+5)^{2}}\right) \int e^{x} d x\right] d x{/tex} - {tex}\int \frac{2}{(x+5)^{3}} e^{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{(x+5)^{2}} e^{x}{/tex} + {tex}\int \frac{2}{(x+5)^{3}} e^{x} dx{/tex} - {tex}\int \frac{2}{(x+5)^{3}} e^{x} dx{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{(x+5)^{2}} e^{x}{/tex} + C
solution: Let the given integral be, l = {tex}\int \frac{(x+3) e^{x}}{(x+5)^{3}} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int\left[\frac{(x+5)-2}{(x+5)^{3}}\right] e^{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int\left[\frac{1}{(x+5)^{2}}+\frac{-2}{(x+5)^{3}}\right] e^{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1}{(x+5)^{2}} e^{x} d x{/tex} - {tex}\int \frac{2}{(x+5)^{3}} e^{x} d x{/tex} using parts method for 1st part and keep 2nd part as it is we get. {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{(x+5)^{2}} \int e^{x} d x{/tex} - {tex}\int\left[\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{1}{(x+5)^{2}}\right) \int e^{x} d x\right] d x{/tex} - {tex}\int \frac{2}{(x+5)^{3}} e^{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{(x+5)^{2}} e^{x}{/tex} + {tex}\int \frac{2}{(x+5)^{3}} e^{x} dx{/tex} - {tex}\int \frac{2}{(x+5)^{3}} e^{x} dx{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{(x+5)^{2}} e^{x}{/tex} + C
solution_html:
Let the given integral be, l = {tex}\int \frac{(x+3) e^{x}}{(x+5)^{3}} d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int\left[\frac{(x+5)-2}{(x+5)^{3}}\right] e^{x} d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int\left[\frac{1}{(x+5)^{2}}+\frac{-2}{(x+5)^{3}}\right] e^{x} d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1}{(x+5)^{2}} e^{x} d x{/tex} - {tex}\int \frac{2}{(x+5)^{3}} e^{x} d x{/tex}

using parts method for 1st part and keep 2nd part as it is we get.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{(x+5)^{2}} \int e^{x} d x{/tex} - {tex}\int\left[\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{1}{(x+5)^{2}}\right) \int e^{x} d x\right] d x{/tex} - {tex}\int \frac{2}{(x+5)^{3}} e^{x} d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{1}{(x+5)^{2}} e^{x}{/tex} + {tex}\int \frac{2}{(x+5)^{3}} e^{x} dx{/tex} - {tex}\int \frac{2}{(x+5)^{3}} e^{x} dx{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{(x+5)^{2}} e^{x}{/tex} + C

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let the given integral be, l = {tex}\int(3-2 x) \sqrt{2+x-x^{2}} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int(3-2 x) \sqrt{\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{2}-\left(x-\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}} d x{/tex} Substituting {tex}\frac{2 x-1}{3}{/tex} = cost {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow d x=-\frac{3}{2} \sin \theta d \theta{/tex} , we get {tex}=\frac{3}{2} \int(3-2 x) \sqrt{1-\left(\frac{2 x-1}{3}\right)^{2}} d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{3}{2} \int(2-3 \cos \theta) \sin \theta\left(-\frac{3}{2} \sin \theta\right) d \theta{/tex} {tex}=\frac{9}{4} \int(3 \cos \theta-2) \sin ^{2} \theta d \theta\\=\frac{27}{4} \int \cos \theta \sin ^{2} \theta d \theta-\frac{9}{2} \int \sin ^{2} \theta d \theta{/tex} {tex}=\frac{9}{4} \sin ^{3} \theta-\frac{9}{4} \int(1-\cos 2 \theta) d \theta{/tex} {tex}=\frac{9}{4} \sin ^{3} \theta-\frac{9}{4} \theta+\frac{9}{8} \sin 2 \theta+C{/tex} Substituiting back we get {tex}\frac{9}{4} \sin ^{3} \theta-\frac{9}{4} \theta+\frac{9}{8} \sin 2 \theta+C{/tex} {tex}=\frac{9}{4}\left(1-\left(\frac{2 x-1}{3}\right)^{2}\right)^{\frac{3}{2}}-\frac{9}{4} \cos ^{-1} \frac{2 x-1}{3}+\frac{9}{4}\left(\frac{2 x-1}{3}\right)\left(1-\left(\frac{2 x-1}{3}\right)^{2}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}+C{/tex}
solution: Let the given integral be, l = {tex}\int(3-2 x) \sqrt{2+x-x^{2}} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int(3-2 x) \sqrt{\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{2}-\left(x-\frac{1}{2}\right)^{2}} d x{/tex} Substituting {tex}\frac{2 x-1}{3}{/tex} = cost {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow d x=-\frac{3}{2} \sin \theta d \theta{/tex} , we get {tex}=\frac{3}{2} \int(3-2 x) \sqrt{1-\left(\frac{2 x-1}{3}\right)^{2}} d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{3}{2} \int(2-3 \cos \theta) \sin \theta\left(-\frac{3}{2} \sin \theta\right) d \theta{/tex} {tex}=\frac{9}{4} \int(3 \cos \theta-2) \sin ^{2} \theta d \theta\\=\frac{27}{4} \int \cos \theta \sin ^{2} \theta d \theta-\frac{9}{2} \int \sin ^{2} \theta d \theta{/tex} {tex}=\frac{9}{4} \sin ^{3} \theta-\frac{9}{4} \int(1-\cos 2 \theta) d \theta{/tex} {tex}=\frac{9}{4} \sin ^{3} \theta-\frac{9}{4} \theta+\frac{9}{8} \sin 2 \theta+C{/tex} Substituiting back we get {tex}\frac{9}{4} \sin ^{3} \theta-\frac{9}{4} \theta+\frac{9}{8} \sin 2 \theta+C{/tex} {tex}=\frac{9}{4}\left(1-\left(\frac{2 x-1}{3}\right)^{2}\right)^{\frac{3}{2}}-\frac{9}{4} \cos ^{-1} \frac{2 x-1}{3}+\frac{9}{4}\left(\frac{2 x-1}{3}\right)\left(1-\left(\frac{2 x-1}{3}\right)^{2}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let the given integral be,
l = {tex}\int(3-2 x) \sqrt{2+x-x^{2}} d x{/tex}

solution: Let {tex}I=\int \frac{x^{3}}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{\left(x^{3}-1\right)+1}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{x^{3}-1}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)} d x+\int \frac{1}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+x+1\right)}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)} d x{/tex} {tex}+\int \frac{d x}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)}{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{x^{2}+x+1}{x^{2}+1} d x{/tex} {tex}+\int \frac{d x}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)}{/tex} {tex}=\int d x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 x}{x^{2}+1} d x+\int \frac{d x}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)}{/tex} {tex}=x+\frac{1}{2}{/tex} ln (x 2 + 1) + I, Where {tex}I=\int \frac{d x}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)}{/tex} Using partial fractions {tex}\frac{1}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)}=\frac{A}{(x-1)}+\frac{B x+C}{x^{2}+1}{/tex} ..... (i) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A(x 2 + 1) + (Bx + C) (x - 1) = 1 At x = 1, {tex}A=\frac{1}{2}{/tex} At x = 1, {tex}C=-\frac{1}{2}{/tex} and {tex}B=-\frac{1}{2}{/tex} using these value in (Eqn. (i), we get {tex}I_{1}=\int\left[\frac{1 / 2}{(x-1)}+\frac{\frac {-1}{2} x -\frac {1}{2}}{x^{2}+1}\right] d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \ln (x-1)-\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{x}{x^{2}+1} d x-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{d x}{x^{2}+1}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \ln (x-1)-\frac{1}{4}{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{2 x}{x^{2}+1} d x-\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \ln (x-1)-\frac{1}{4}{/tex} {tex}\ln \left(x^{2}+1\right)-\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}I=x+\frac{1}{2} \ln \left(x^{2}+1\right){/tex} {tex}+\frac{1}{2} \ln (x-1)-\frac{1}{4} \ln \left(x^{2}+1\right){/tex} {tex}\frac {-1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x+c{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I=\int \frac{x^{3}}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)} d x{/tex}
{tex}=\int \frac{\left(x^{3}-1\right)+1}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)} d x{/tex}
{tex}=\int \frac{x^{3}-1}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)} d x+\int \frac{1}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)} d x{/tex}
{tex}=\int \frac{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+x+1\right)}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)} d x{/tex} {tex}+\int \frac{d x}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)}{/tex}
{tex}=\int \frac{x^{2}+x+1}{x^{2}+1} d x{/tex} {tex}+\int \frac{d x}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)}{/tex}
{tex}=\int d x+\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 x}{x^{2}+1} d x+\int \frac{d x}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)}{/tex}
{tex}=x+\frac{1}{2}{/tex} ln (x2 + 1) + I,
Where {tex}I=\int \frac{d x}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)}{/tex}
Using partial fractions
{tex}\frac{1}{(x-1)\left(x^{2}+1\right)}=\frac{A}{(x-1)}+\frac{B x+C}{x^{2}+1}{/tex} ..... (i)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A(x2 + 1) + (Bx + C) (x - 1) = 1
At x = 1, {tex}A=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
At x = 1, {tex}C=-\frac{1}{2}{/tex} and {tex}B=-\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
using these value in (Eqn. (i), we get
{tex}I_{1}=\int\left[\frac{1 / 2}{(x-1)}+\frac{\frac {-1}{2} x -\frac {1}{2}}{x^{2}+1}\right] d x{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \ln (x-1)-\frac{1}{2}{/tex}{tex}\int \frac{x}{x^{2}+1} d x-\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{d x}{x^{2}+1}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \ln (x-1)-\frac{1}{4}{/tex}{tex}\int \frac{2 x}{x^{2}+1} d x-\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \ln (x-1)-\frac{1}{4}{/tex}{tex}\ln \left(x^{2}+1\right)-\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}I=x+\frac{1}{2} \ln \left(x^{2}+1\right){/tex}{tex}+\frac{1}{2} \ln (x-1)-\frac{1}{4} \ln \left(x^{2}+1\right){/tex}{tex}\frac {-1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x+c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Evaluate: {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi / 2}\left(\frac{5 \sin x+3 \cos x}{\sin x+\cos x}\right) d x{/tex}
question_text: Evaluate: {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi / 2}\left(\frac{5 \sin x+3 \cos x}{\sin x+\cos x}\right) d x{/tex}
explanation: Let I = {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{5 \sin x+3 \cos x}{\sin x+\cos x} d x{/tex} , then we have {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{3+5 \tan x}{1+\tan x} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{5(1+\tan x)-2}{1+\tan x} d x{/tex} {tex}= \int^ {\pi / 2}_0 5 \, d x-2 \int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{1}{1+\tan x} d x{/tex} {tex}5[x]_{0}^{\pi / 2}{/tex} - {tex} \int_0 ^{\pi\over 2} \frac{2 \cos x}{\cos x+\sin x} d x{/tex} = {tex}5{\pi \over 2}{/tex} - {tex}\left[\int_{0}^{\pi / 2}\left(\frac{\cos x+\sin x)+(\cos x-\sin x)}{(\cos x+\sin x)} d x\right]\right.{/tex} = {tex}5{\pi \over 2}{/tex} - {tex}[x]_{0}^{\pi / 2}-[\log |\cos x+\sin x|]_{0}^{\pi / 2}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{5 \pi}{2}-\pi / 2-\log |1|{/tex} {tex}=\frac{5 \pi-\pi}{2}{/tex} = {tex}2 \pi{/tex}
solution: Let I = {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{5 \sin x+3 \cos x}{\sin x+\cos x} d x{/tex} , then we have {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{3+5 \tan x}{1+\tan x} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{5(1+\tan x)-2}{1+\tan x} d x{/tex} {tex}= \int^ {\pi / 2}_0 5 \, d x-2 \int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{1}{1+\tan x} d x{/tex} {tex}5[x]_{0}^{\pi / 2}{/tex} - {tex} \int_0 ^{\pi\over 2} \frac{2 \cos x}{\cos x+\sin x} d x{/tex} = {tex}5{\pi \over 2}{/tex} - {tex}\left[\int_{0}^{\pi / 2}\left(\frac{\cos x+\sin x)+(\cos x-\sin x)}{(\cos x+\sin x)} d x\right]\right.{/tex} = {tex}5{\pi \over 2}{/tex} - {tex}[x]_{0}^{\pi / 2}-[\log |\cos x+\sin x|]_{0}^{\pi / 2}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{5 \pi}{2}-\pi / 2-\log |1|{/tex} {tex}=\frac{5 \pi-\pi}{2}{/tex} = {tex}2 \pi{/tex}
solution_html:
Let I  =  {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{5 \sin x+3 \cos x}{\sin x+\cos x} d x{/tex}, then we have
{tex}I=\int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{3+5 \tan x}{1+\tan x} d x{/tex}
{tex}=\int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{5(1+\tan x)-2}{1+\tan x} d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int^ {\pi / 2}_0 5 \, d x-2 \int_{0}^{\pi / 2} \frac{1}{1+\tan x} d x{/tex}
{tex}5[x]_{0}^{\pi / 2}{/tex} -{tex} \int_0 ^{\pi\over 2} \frac{2 \cos x}{\cos x+\sin x} d x{/tex}
= {tex}5{\pi \over 2}{/tex} -{tex}\left[\int_{0}^{\pi / 2}\left(\frac{\cos x+\sin x)+(\cos x-\sin x)}{(\cos x+\sin x)} d x\right]\right.{/tex}
= {tex}5{\pi \over 2}{/tex} -{tex}[x]_{0}^{\pi / 2}-[\log |\cos x+\sin x|]_{0}^{\pi / 2}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{5 \pi}{2}-\pi / 2-\log |1|{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{5 \pi-\pi}{2}{/tex}
= {tex}2 \pi{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let {tex}I=\int \frac{x}{(1+x \tan x)} d x{/tex} , then we have {tex}I=\int \frac{x}{\left(1+x\left(\frac{\sin x}{\cos x}\right)\right)} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{x \cos x}{(\cos x+x \sin x)} d x{/tex} Let (cos x + x sin x) = t, then x cos x dx = dt So, {tex}\mathrm{I}=\int \frac{1}{\mathrm{t}} \mathrm{dt}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\log \mathrm{t}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \mathrm{I}=\log (\cos \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{x} \sin \mathrm{x})+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}I=\int \frac{x}{(1+x \tan x)} d x{/tex} , then we have {tex}I=\int \frac{x}{\left(1+x\left(\frac{\sin x}{\cos x}\right)\right)} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{x \cos x}{(\cos x+x \sin x)} d x{/tex} Let (cos x + x sin x) = t, then x cos x dx = dt So, {tex}\mathrm{I}=\int \frac{1}{\mathrm{t}} \mathrm{dt}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\log \mathrm{t}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \mathrm{I}=\log (\cos \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{x} \sin \mathrm{x})+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I=\int \frac{x}{(1+x \tan x)} d x{/tex}, then we have
{tex}I=\int \frac{x}{\left(1+x\left(\frac{\sin x}{\cos x}\right)\right)} d x{/tex}
{tex}=\int \frac{x \cos x}{(\cos x+x \sin x)} d x{/tex}
Let (cos x + x sin x) = t, then x cos x dx = dt
So, {tex}\mathrm{I}=\int \frac{1}{\mathrm{t}} \mathrm{dt}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\log \mathrm{t}+\mathrm{C}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \mathrm{I}=\log (\cos \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{x} \sin \mathrm{x})+\mathrm{C}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let I = {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi / 4} \frac{\sec x}{1+2 \sin ^{2} x} d x{/tex} , then I = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{\cos x d x}{\cos ^{2}\left(1+2 \sin ^{2} x\right)}{/tex} Let sin x =t cos x dx = dt {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{\cos x d x}{\left(1-\sin ^{2} x\right)\left(1+2 \sin ^{2} x\right)}{/tex} {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{d t}{\left(1-t^{2}\right)\left(1+2 t^{2}\right)}{/tex} {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left(\frac{A}{1-t}+\frac{B}{1+t}+\frac{c t+D}{1+2 t^{2}}\right) d t{/tex} {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{1}{6}\left(\frac{1}{4 t}\right) d t+\int \frac{1}{6}\left(\frac{1}{t+1}\right) d t+\frac{2}{3} \int \frac{d t}{1+2 t^{2}}{/tex} {tex}y-\frac{1}{6} \ln (1-t)+\frac{1}{6} \ln (t+1)+\frac{2}{3} \times \frac{1}{2} \times \frac{1}{1 / 2} \tan ^{-1}(\sqrt{2} t)+c{/tex} {tex}\left[-\frac{1}{6} \ln (1-\sin x)+\frac{1}{6} \ln (\sin x+1)+\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3} \tan ^{4}(\sqrt{2} \sin x)\right]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left[\frac{1}{6} \ln \left(\frac{1+\sin x}{1-\sin x}\right)+\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3} \tan ^{-1}(\sqrt{2} \sin x)\right]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{6} \ln \left(\frac{\sqrt{2}+1}{\sqrt{2}-1}\right)+\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3} \times \frac{\pi}{4}-0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{6} \ln \left(\frac{\sqrt{2}+1}{\sqrt{2}-1}\right)+\frac{\pi}{6 \sqrt{2}}{/tex} .
solution: Let I = {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi / 4} \frac{\sec x}{1+2 \sin ^{2} x} d x{/tex} , then I = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{\cos x d x}{\cos ^{2}\left(1+2 \sin ^{2} x\right)}{/tex} Let sin x =t cos x dx = dt {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{\cos x d x}{\left(1-\sin ^{2} x\right)\left(1+2 \sin ^{2} x\right)}{/tex} {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{d t}{\left(1-t^{2}\right)\left(1+2 t^{2}\right)}{/tex} {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left(\frac{A}{1-t}+\frac{B}{1+t}+\frac{c t+D}{1+2 t^{2}}\right) d t{/tex} {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{1}{6}\left(\frac{1}{4 t}\right) d t+\int \frac{1}{6}\left(\frac{1}{t+1}\right) d t+\frac{2}{3} \int \frac{d t}{1+2 t^{2}}{/tex} {tex}y-\frac{1}{6} \ln (1-t)+\frac{1}{6} \ln (t+1)+\frac{2}{3} \times \frac{1}{2} \times \frac{1}{1 / 2} \tan ^{-1}(\sqrt{2} t)+c{/tex} {tex}\left[-\frac{1}{6} \ln (1-\sin x)+\frac{1}{6} \ln (\sin x+1)+\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3} \tan ^{4}(\sqrt{2} \sin x)\right]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left[\frac{1}{6} \ln \left(\frac{1+\sin x}{1-\sin x}\right)+\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3} \tan ^{-1}(\sqrt{2} \sin x)\right]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{6} \ln \left(\frac{\sqrt{2}+1}{\sqrt{2}-1}\right)+\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3} \times \frac{\pi}{4}-0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{6} \ln \left(\frac{\sqrt{2}+1}{\sqrt{2}-1}\right)+\frac{\pi}{6 \sqrt{2}}{/tex} .
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi / 4} \frac{\sec x}{1+2 \sin ^{2} x} d x{/tex}, then
I = {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{\cos x d x}{\cos ^{2}\left(1+2 \sin ^{2} x\right)}{/tex}
Let sin x =t 
cos x dx = dt
{tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{\cos x d x}{\left(1-\sin ^{2} x\right)\left(1+2 \sin ^{2} x\right)}{/tex}
{tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{d t}{\left(1-t^{2}\right)\left(1+2 t^{2}\right)}{/tex}
{tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}\left(\frac{A}{1-t}+\frac{B}{1+t}+\frac{c t+D}{1+2 t^{2}}\right) d t{/tex}
{tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \frac{1}{6}\left(\frac{1}{4 t}\right) d t+\int \frac{1}{6}\left(\frac{1}{t+1}\right) d t+\frac{2}{3} \int \frac{d t}{1+2 t^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}y-\frac{1}{6} \ln (1-t)+\frac{1}{6} \ln (t+1)+\frac{2}{3} \times \frac{1}{2} \times \frac{1}{1 / 2} \tan ^{-1}(\sqrt{2} t)+c{/tex}
{tex}\left[-\frac{1}{6} \ln (1-\sin x)+\frac{1}{6} \ln (\sin x+1)+\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3} \tan ^{4}(\sqrt{2} \sin x)\right]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left[\frac{1}{6} \ln \left(\frac{1+\sin x}{1-\sin x}\right)+\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3} \tan ^{-1}(\sqrt{2} \sin x)\right]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{1}{6} \ln \left(\frac{\sqrt{2}+1}{\sqrt{2}-1}\right)+\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3} \times \frac{\pi}{4}-0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\frac{1}{6} \ln \left(\frac{\sqrt{2}+1}{\sqrt{2}-1}\right)+\frac{\pi}{6 \sqrt{2}}{/tex}.

subject: Mathematics
explanation: We have to evaluate {tex}\int x \cdot \sin ^{-1} x d x{/tex} Let {tex} x=\sin \theta{/tex} {tex}d x=\cos \theta d \theta{/tex} Substituting values, we get {tex}\int x \sin ^{-1} x d x=\int \sin \theta \sin ^{-1}(\sin \theta) \cos \theta d \theta{/tex} {tex}=\int \sin \theta \theta \cos \theta d \theta{/tex} {tex}\left(\because \sin ^{-1}(\sin \theta)=\theta\right){/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \int \theta(2 \sin \theta \cos \theta) d \theta{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \int \theta \sin 2 \theta d \theta{/tex} {tex}(\because 2 \sin x \cos x=\sin 2 x){/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\theta \int \sin 2 \theta d \theta-\int\left(\frac{d \theta}{d \theta} \int \sin 2 \theta d \theta\right) d \theta\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\theta \frac{-(\cos 2 \theta)}{2}-\int 1 . \frac{-(\cos 2 \theta)}{2} d \theta\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{-\theta}{2} \cos 2 \theta+\frac{1}{2} \int \cos 2 \theta d \theta\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{-\theta}{2} \cos 2 \theta+\frac{1}{2} \frac{\sin 2 \theta}{2}\right]+C{/tex} {tex}=\frac{-\theta}{4} \cos 2 \theta+\frac{1}{8} \sin 2 \theta+c{/tex} {tex}=\frac{-\theta}{4}\left(1-2 \sin ^{2} \theta\right)+\frac{1}{8} \times 2 \sin \theta \cos \theta+C{/tex} {tex}=\frac{-\theta}{4}\left(1-2 \sin ^{2} \theta\right)+\frac{1}{4} \sin \theta \cos \theta+c{/tex} {tex}=\frac{-\theta}{4}\left(1-2 \sin ^{2} \theta\right)+\frac{1}{4} \sin \theta \sqrt{1-\sin ^{2} \theta}+C{/tex} Usingx = sin {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\theta{/tex} = sin -1 x {tex}=\frac{-\sin ^{-1} x}{4}\left(1-2 x^{2}\right)+\frac{x}{4} \sqrt{1-x^{2}}+C{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\left(2 x^{2}-1\right)}{4} \sin ^{-1} x+\frac{x}{4} \sqrt{1-x^{2}}+C{/tex}
solution: We have to evaluate {tex}\int x \cdot \sin ^{-1} x d x{/tex} Let {tex} x=\sin \theta{/tex} {tex}d x=\cos \theta d \theta{/tex} Substituting values, we get {tex}\int x \sin ^{-1} x d x=\int \sin \theta \sin ^{-1}(\sin \theta) \cos \theta d \theta{/tex} {tex}=\int \sin \theta \theta \cos \theta d \theta{/tex} {tex}\left(\because \sin ^{-1}(\sin \theta)=\theta\right){/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \int \theta(2 \sin \theta \cos \theta) d \theta{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \int \theta \sin 2 \theta d \theta{/tex} {tex}(\because 2 \sin x \cos x=\sin 2 x){/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\theta \int \sin 2 \theta d \theta-\int\left(\frac{d \theta}{d \theta} \int \sin 2 \theta d \theta\right) d \theta\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\theta \frac{-(\cos 2 \theta)}{2}-\int 1 . \frac{-(\cos 2 \theta)}{2} d \theta\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{-\theta}{2} \cos 2 \theta+\frac{1}{2} \int \cos 2 \theta d \theta\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{-\theta}{2} \cos 2 \theta+\frac{1}{2} \frac{\sin 2 \theta}{2}\right]+C{/tex} {tex}=\frac{-\theta}{4} \cos 2 \theta+\frac{1}{8} \sin 2 \theta+c{/tex} {tex}=\frac{-\theta}{4}\left(1-2 \sin ^{2} \theta\right)+\frac{1}{8} \times 2 \sin \theta \cos \theta+C{/tex} {tex}=\frac{-\theta}{4}\left(1-2 \sin ^{2} \theta\right)+\frac{1}{4} \sin \theta \cos \theta+c{/tex} {tex}=\frac{-\theta}{4}\left(1-2 \sin ^{2} \theta\right)+\frac{1}{4} \sin \theta \sqrt{1-\sin ^{2} \theta}+C{/tex} Usingx = sin {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\theta{/tex} = sin -1 x {tex}=\frac{-\sin ^{-1} x}{4}\left(1-2 x^{2}\right)+\frac{x}{4} \sqrt{1-x^{2}}+C{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\left(2 x^{2}-1\right)}{4} \sin ^{-1} x+\frac{x}{4} \sqrt{1-x^{2}}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
We have to evaluate {tex}\int x \cdot \sin ^{-1} x d x{/tex}  
Let {tex} x=\sin \theta{/tex} 
{tex}d x=\cos \theta d \theta{/tex}
Substituting values, we get
{tex}\int x \sin ^{-1} x d x=\int \sin \theta \sin ^{-1}(\sin \theta) \cos \theta d \theta{/tex}
{tex}=\int \sin \theta \theta \cos \theta d \theta{/tex} {tex}\left(\because \sin ^{-1}(\sin \theta)=\theta\right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \int \theta(2 \sin \theta \cos \theta) d \theta{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \int \theta \sin 2 \theta d \theta{/tex}  {tex}(\because 2 \sin x \cos x=\sin 2 x){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\theta \int \sin 2 \theta d \theta-\int\left(\frac{d \theta}{d \theta} \int \sin 2 \theta d \theta\right) d \theta\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\theta \frac{-(\cos 2 \theta)}{2}-\int 1 . \frac{-(\cos 2 \theta)}{2} d \theta\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{-\theta}{2} \cos 2 \theta+\frac{1}{2} \int \cos 2 \theta d \theta\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{-\theta}{2} \cos 2 \theta+\frac{1}{2} \frac{\sin 2 \theta}{2}\right]+C{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{-\theta}{4} \cos 2 \theta+\frac{1}{8} \sin 2 \theta+c{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{-\theta}{4}\left(1-2 \sin ^{2} \theta\right)+\frac{1}{8} \times 2 \sin \theta \cos \theta+C{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{-\theta}{4}\left(1-2 \sin ^{2} \theta\right)+\frac{1}{4} \sin \theta \cos \theta+c{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{-\theta}{4}\left(1-2 \sin ^{2} \theta\right)+\frac{1}{4} \sin \theta \sqrt{1-\sin ^{2} \theta}+C{/tex}
Usingx = sin{tex}\theta{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\theta{/tex} = sin-1x
{tex}=\frac{-\sin ^{-1} x}{4}\left(1-2 x^{2}\right)+\frac{x}{4} \sqrt{1-x^{2}}+C{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\left(2 x^{2}-1\right)}{4} \sin ^{-1} x+\frac{x}{4} \sqrt{1-x^{2}}+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let {tex}I=\int_{\pi / 4}^{\pi / 2} \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}\left(\frac{1+\sin 2 \mathrm{x}}{1+\cos 2 \mathrm{x}}\right) \mathrm{dx}{/tex} Now, {tex}\int \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}\left(\frac{1+\sin 2 \mathrm{x}}{1+\cos 2 \mathrm{x}}\right) \mathrm{dx}{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{e^{2 x}(1+\sin 2 x)}{1+\cos 2 x} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{e^{2 x}(1+2 \sin x \cos x)}{1+2 \cos ^{2} x-1} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{e^{2 x}(1+2 \sin x \cos x)}{2 \cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \int e^{2 x} \sec ^{2} x d x+\int e^{2 x} \tan x d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left(e^{2 x} \int \sec ^{2} x d x-2 \int e^{2 x} \int \sec ^{2} x d x d x\right)+\int e^{2 x} \tan x d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left(e^{2 x} \tan x-2 \int e^{2 x} \tan x d x\right)+\int e^{2 x} \tan x d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{e^{2 x} \tan x}{2}-\int e^{2 x} \tan x d x+\int e^{2 x} \tan x d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{e^{2 x} \tan x}{2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore ~ I =[\frac{e^{2 x} \tan x}{2}]_{\frac{\pi}4}^{\frac{\pi}2}\\=\infty{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}I=\int_{\pi / 4}^{\pi / 2} \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}\left(\frac{1+\sin 2 \mathrm{x}}{1+\cos 2 \mathrm{x}}\right) \mathrm{dx}{/tex} Now, {tex}\int \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}\left(\frac{1+\sin 2 \mathrm{x}}{1+\cos 2 \mathrm{x}}\right) \mathrm{dx}{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{e^{2 x}(1+\sin 2 x)}{1+\cos 2 x} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{e^{2 x}(1+2 \sin x \cos x)}{1+2 \cos ^{2} x-1} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{e^{2 x}(1+2 \sin x \cos x)}{2 \cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2} \int e^{2 x} \sec ^{2} x d x+\int e^{2 x} \tan x d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left(e^{2 x} \int \sec ^{2} x d x-2 \int e^{2 x} \int \sec ^{2} x d x d x\right)+\int e^{2 x} \tan x d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left(e^{2 x} \tan x-2 \int e^{2 x} \tan x d x\right)+\int e^{2 x} \tan x d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{e^{2 x} \tan x}{2}-\int e^{2 x} \tan x d x+\int e^{2 x} \tan x d x{/tex} {tex}=\frac{e^{2 x} \tan x}{2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore ~ I =[\frac{e^{2 x} \tan x}{2}]_{\frac{\pi}4}^{\frac{\pi}2}\\=\infty{/tex}
solution_html:
Let  {tex}I=\int_{\pi / 4}^{\pi / 2} \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}\left(\frac{1+\sin 2 \mathrm{x}}{1+\cos 2 \mathrm{x}}\right) \mathrm{dx}{/tex}
Now,  {tex}\int \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}\left(\frac{1+\sin 2 \mathrm{x}}{1+\cos 2 \mathrm{x}}\right) \mathrm{dx}{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{e^{2 x}(1+\sin 2 x)}{1+\cos 2 x} d x{/tex}
{tex}=\int \frac{e^{2 x}(1+2 \sin x \cos x)}{1+2 \cos ^{2} x-1} d x{/tex}
{tex}=\int \frac{e^{2 x}(1+2 \sin x \cos x)}{2 \cos ^{2} x} d x{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \int e^{2 x} \sec ^{2} x d x+\int e^{2 x} \tan x d x{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left(e^{2 x} \int \sec ^{2} x d x-2 \int e^{2 x} \int \sec ^{2} x d x d x\right)+\int e^{2 x} \tan x d x{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{2}\left(e^{2 x} \tan x-2 \int e^{2 x} \tan x d x\right)+\int e^{2 x} \tan x d x{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{e^{2 x} \tan x}{2}-\int e^{2 x} \tan x d x+\int e^{2 x} \tan x d x{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{e^{2 x} \tan x}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore ~ I =[\frac{e^{2 x} \tan x}{2}]_{\frac{\pi}4}^{\frac{\pi}2}\\=\infty{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log (1+\tan x) d x{/tex}
question_text: By using the properties of definite integrals, evaluate the integral {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log (1+\tan x) d x{/tex}
explanation: Given {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log (1+\tan x) d x{/tex} Let {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log (1+\tan x) d x{/tex} .....(i) as, {tex}\left\{\int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left[1+\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}-\mathrm{x}\right)\right] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} as {tex}\left\{\tan (A-B)=\frac{\tan (A)-\tan (B)}{1+\tan (A) \tan (B)}\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left[1+\frac{\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)-\tan (\mathrm{x})}{1+\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) \tan (\mathrm{x})}\right] \mathrm{dx}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left[1+\frac{1-\tan (\mathrm{x})}{1+\tan (\mathrm{x})}\right] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left[\frac{2}{1+\tan (x)}\right] d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log [2] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}-\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log [1+\tan (\mathrm{x})] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log [2] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}-\mathrm{I}{/tex} (from (i)) {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=[\mathrm{x} \log 2]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{4} \log 2-0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{8} \log 2{/tex}
solution: Given {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log (1+\tan x) d x{/tex} Let {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log (1+\tan x) d x{/tex} .....(i) as, {tex}\left\{\int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left[1+\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}-\mathrm{x}\right)\right] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} as {tex}\left\{\tan (A-B)=\frac{\tan (A)-\tan (B)}{1+\tan (A) \tan (B)}\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left[1+\frac{\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)-\tan (\mathrm{x})}{1+\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) \tan (\mathrm{x})}\right] \mathrm{dx}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left[1+\frac{1-\tan (\mathrm{x})}{1+\tan (\mathrm{x})}\right] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left[\frac{2}{1+\tan (x)}\right] d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log [2] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}-\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log [1+\tan (\mathrm{x})] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log [2] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}-\mathrm{I}{/tex} (from (i)) {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=[\mathrm{x} \log 2]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{4} \log 2-0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{8} \log 2{/tex}
solution_html:
Given {tex}\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log (1+\tan x) d x{/tex} 
Let {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log (1+\tan x) d x{/tex} .....(i)
as, {tex}\left\{\int_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right\}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left[1+\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}-\mathrm{x}\right)\right] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
as {tex}\left\{\tan (A-B)=\frac{\tan (A)-\tan (B)}{1+\tan (A) \tan (B)}\right\}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left[1+\frac{\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)-\tan (\mathrm{x})}{1+\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) \tan (\mathrm{x})}\right] \mathrm{dx}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left[1+\frac{1-\tan (\mathrm{x})}{1+\tan (\mathrm{x})}\right] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log \left[\frac{2}{1+\tan (x)}\right] d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log [2] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}-\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log [1+\tan (\mathrm{x})] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} \log [2] \mathrm{d} \mathrm{x}-\mathrm{I}{/tex} (from (i))
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=[\mathrm{x} \log 2]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{4} \log 2-0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{8} \log 2{/tex}

{tex}= \pi \left[ [ x ] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } - 2 \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \left( \frac { 1 } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } - \frac { \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } \right) d x \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \pi \left[ ( \pi - 0 ) - 2 \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \left( \sec ^ { 2 } x - \sec x \tan x \right) d x \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \pi \left\{ \pi - 2 [ \tan x - \sec x ] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \right\}{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \left\{ \pi - \lim _ { x \rightarrow \frac { \pi } { 2 } } 2 ( \tan x - \sec x ) + 2 ( \tan 0 - \sec 0 ) \right\}{/tex}
{tex}2 I = \pi \{ \pi - 0 + 2 ( 0 - 1 ) \} = \pi ( \pi - 2 ){/tex}
{tex} \therefore \quad I = \frac { \pi } { 2 } ( \pi - 2 ){/tex}

(ii) Adding (i) and (ii), we get {tex}2 I=\pi \int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\sin x}{\left(1+\cos ^{2} x\right)} d x=-\pi \int_{1}^{-1} \frac{d t}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)}{/tex} , where cos x = t [x = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t = 1 and x = n {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t = -1] {tex}=\pi \int_{-1}^{1} \frac{d t}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)}=\pi\left[\tan ^{-1} t\right]_{-1}^{1}{/tex} {tex}=\pi\left[\tan ^{-1} 1-\tan ^{-1}(-1)\right]{/tex} {tex}=\pi\left[\frac{\pi}{4}-\left(-\frac{\pi}{4}\right)\right]=\frac{\pi^{2}}{2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad I=\frac{\pi^{2}}{4}{/tex}

(ii) Adding (i) and (ii), we get {tex}2 I=\pi \int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\sin x}{\left(1+\cos ^{2} x\right)} d x=-\pi \int_{1}^{-1} \frac{d t}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)}{/tex} , where cos x = t [x = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t = 1 and x = n {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t = -1] {tex}=\pi \int_{-1}^{1} \frac{d t}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)}=\pi\left[\tan ^{-1} t\right]_{-1}^{1}{/tex} {tex}=\pi\left[\tan ^{-1} 1-\tan ^{-1}(-1)\right]{/tex} {tex}=\pi\left[\frac{\pi}{4}-\left(-\frac{\pi}{4}\right)\right]=\frac{\pi^{2}}{2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad I=\frac{\pi^{2}}{4}{/tex}

Then, {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{(\pi-x) \sin (\pi-x)}{1+\cos ^{2}(\pi-x)} d x{/tex}
or {tex}I=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{(\pi-x) \sin x}{\left(1+\cos ^{2} x\right)} d x{/tex} .......... (ii)
Adding (i) and (ii), we get
{tex}2 I=\pi \int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\sin x}{\left(1+\cos ^{2} x\right)} d x=-\pi \int_{1}^{-1} \frac{d t}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)}{/tex}, where cos x = t
[x = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t = 1 and x = n {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} t = -1]
{tex}=\pi \int_{-1}^{1} \frac{d t}{\left(1+t^{2}\right)}=\pi\left[\tan ^{-1} t\right]_{-1}^{1}{/tex}
{tex}=\pi\left[\tan ^{-1} 1-\tan ^{-1}(-1)\right]{/tex} {tex}=\pi\left[\frac{\pi}{4}-\left(-\frac{\pi}{4}\right)\right]=\frac{\pi^{2}}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad I=\frac{\pi^{2}}{4}{/tex}

from equation (i) and (ii) we get ,

question_html: Evaluate {tex} \int _ { \pi / 6 } ^ { \pi / 3 } \frac { \sin x + \cos x } { \sqrt { \sin 2 x } } d x.{/tex}
question_text: Evaluate {tex} \int _ { \pi / 6 } ^ { \pi / 3 } \frac { \sin x + \cos x } { \sqrt { \sin 2 x } } d x.{/tex}
explanation: Let, {tex}I = \int _ { \pi / 6 } ^ { \pi / 3 } \frac { \sin x + \cos x } { \sqrt { \sin 2 x } } d x{/tex} Put {tex}sin x - cos x = t{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow (cos x + sin x) dx = dt{/tex} Lower limit , when {tex} x = \frac { \pi } { 6 },{/tex} {tex} t = \sin \frac { \pi } { 6 } - \cos \frac { \pi } { 6 } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } - \frac { \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 } = \frac { 1 - \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 }{/tex} Upper limit , when {tex} x = \frac { \pi } { 3 },{/tex} {tex} t = \sin \frac { \pi } { 3 } - \cos \frac { \pi } { 3 } = \frac { \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 } - \frac { 1 } { 2 } = \frac { \sqrt { 3 } - 1 } { 2 }{/tex} Also, (sin x - cos x) 2 = t 2 {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} sin 2 x + cos 2 x - 2sinx cos x = t 2 {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} 1 - sin 2x = t 2 {tex} \Rightarrow sin 2x = 1 - t^2{/tex} {tex} \therefore I = \int _ { \frac { 1 - \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 } } ^ { \frac { \sqrt { 3 } - 1 } { 2 } } \frac { d t } { \sqrt { 1 - t ^ { 2 } } } = \left[ \sin ^ { - 1 } t \right] _ { \frac { 1 - \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 } } ^ { \frac { \sqrt { 3 } - 1 } { 2 } }{/tex} {tex} = \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { \sqrt { 3 } - 1 } { 2 } \right) - \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { 1 - \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 } \right){/tex} {tex} = \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { \sqrt { 3 } - 1 } { 2 } \right) + \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { \sqrt { 3 } - 1 } { 2 } \right){/tex} [ {tex} \because{/tex} sin -1 (-x) = -sin -1 x] {tex} = 2 \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { \sqrt { 3 } - 1 } { 2 } \right){/tex}
solution: Let, {tex}I = \int _ { \pi / 6 } ^ { \pi / 3 } \frac { \sin x + \cos x } { \sqrt { \sin 2 x } } d x{/tex} Put {tex}sin x - cos x = t{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow (cos x + sin x) dx = dt{/tex} Lower limit , when {tex} x = \frac { \pi } { 6 },{/tex} {tex} t = \sin \frac { \pi } { 6 } - \cos \frac { \pi } { 6 } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } - \frac { \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 } = \frac { 1 - \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 }{/tex} Upper limit , when {tex} x = \frac { \pi } { 3 },{/tex} {tex} t = \sin \frac { \pi } { 3 } - \cos \frac { \pi } { 3 } = \frac { \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 } - \frac { 1 } { 2 } = \frac { \sqrt { 3 } - 1 } { 2 }{/tex} Also, (sin x - cos x) 2 = t 2 {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} sin 2 x + cos 2 x - 2sinx cos x = t 2 {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} 1 - sin 2x = t 2 {tex} \Rightarrow sin 2x = 1 - t^2{/tex} {tex} \therefore I = \int _ { \frac { 1 - \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 } } ^ { \frac { \sqrt { 3 } - 1 } { 2 } } \frac { d t } { \sqrt { 1 - t ^ { 2 } } } = \left[ \sin ^ { - 1 } t \right] _ { \frac { 1 - \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 } } ^ { \frac { \sqrt { 3 } - 1 } { 2 } }{/tex} {tex} = \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { \sqrt { 3 } - 1 } { 2 } \right) - \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { 1 - \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 } \right){/tex} {tex} = \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { \sqrt { 3 } - 1 } { 2 } \right) + \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { \sqrt { 3 } - 1 } { 2 } \right){/tex} [ {tex} \because{/tex} sin -1 (-x) = -sin -1 x] {tex} = 2 \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { \sqrt { 3 } - 1 } { 2 } \right){/tex}
solution_html:
Let, {tex}I = \int _ { \pi / 6 } ^ { \pi / 3 } \frac { \sin x + \cos x } { \sqrt { \sin 2 x } } d x{/tex}
Put {tex}sin x - cos x = t{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow (cos x + sin x) dx = dt{/tex} 
Lower limit , when {tex} x = \frac { \pi } { 6 },{/tex} {tex} t = \sin \frac { \pi } { 6 } - \cos \frac { \pi } { 6 } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } - \frac { \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 } = \frac { 1 - \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 }{/tex}
Upper limit , when {tex} x = \frac { \pi } { 3 },{/tex}  {tex} t = \sin \frac { \pi } { 3 } - \cos \frac { \pi } { 3 } = \frac { \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 } - \frac { 1 } { 2 } = \frac { \sqrt { 3 } - 1 } { 2 }{/tex}
Also, (sin x - cos x)2 = t2
{tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} sin2 x + cos2 x - 2sinx cos x = t2
{tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} 1 - sin 2x = t2
{tex} \Rightarrow sin 2x = 1 - t^2{/tex}
{tex} \therefore I = \int _ { \frac { 1 - \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 } } ^ { \frac { \sqrt { 3 } - 1 } { 2 } } \frac { d t } { \sqrt { 1 - t ^ { 2 } } } = \left[ \sin ^ { - 1 } t \right] _ { \frac { 1 - \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 } } ^ { \frac { \sqrt { 3 } - 1 } { 2 } }{/tex}
{tex} = \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { \sqrt { 3 } - 1 } { 2 } \right) - \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { 1 - \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 } \right){/tex}
{tex} = \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { \sqrt { 3 } - 1 } { 2 } \right) + \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { \sqrt { 3 } - 1 } { 2 } \right){/tex} [{tex} \because{/tex} sin-1 (-x) = -sin-1 x]
{tex} = 2 \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { \sqrt { 3 } - 1 } { 2 } \right){/tex}

According to the question, {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 4 } \frac { \sin x + \cos x } { 9 + 16 \sin 2 x } d x{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 4 } \frac { \sin x + \cos x } { 9 + 16 \left[ \begin{array} { r } { 1 - \left( \cos ^ { 2 } x + \sin ^ { 2 } x \right. } { - 2 \sin x \cos x ) } \end{array} \right] } d x{/tex}{tex}[\because 1 = \cos ^ { 2 } x + \sin ^ { 2 } x ]{/tex} and {tex}[\because \sin 2 x = 2 \sin x \cos x ]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 4 } \frac { \sin x + \cos x } { 9 + 16 \left[ 1 - ( \cos x - \sin x ) ^ { 2 } \right] } d x{/tex}
put , {tex}cos x - sin x = t{/tex}

Lower limit , when x = 0, then t = cos 0 - sin 0 = 1 
Upper limit , when x = {tex}\frac { \pi } { 4 } , {/tex} then {tex}t = \cos \frac { \pi } { 4 } - \sin \frac { \pi } { 4 } = \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } } - \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } } = 0.{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad I = \int _ { 1 } ^ { 0 } \frac { - d t } { 9 + 16 \left( 1 - t ^ { 2 } \right) }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { d t } { 9 + 16 \left( 1 - t ^ { 2 } \right) }{/tex}
{tex}= \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { d t } { 25 - 16 t ^ { 2 } }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 16 } \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { d t } { \left( \frac { 5 } { 4 } \right) ^ { 2 } - t ^ { 2 } }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 \times \frac { 5 } { 4 } \times 16 } \left[ \log \left| \frac { 5 + 4 t } { 5 - 4 t } \right| \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 1 }{/tex}{tex}\left[ \because \int \frac { 1 } { a ^ { 2 } - x ^ { 2 } } d x = \frac { 1 } { 2 a } \log \left| \frac { a + x } { a - x } \right| + C \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 40 } \left[ \log \left| \frac { 5 + 4 } { 5 - 4 } \right| - \log \left| \frac { 5 } { 5 } \right| \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 40 } \left[ \log \left( \frac { 9 } { 1 } \right) - \log \left( \frac { 5 } { 5 } \right) \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 40 } ( \log 9 - \log 1 ) {/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 40 } ( \log 9 ){/tex} [{tex} \because{/tex} log 1 = 0]
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { 1 } { 40 } \log ( 3 ) ^ { 2 }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 2 } { 40 } \log 3{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex}log an = nlog a]
{tex}\therefore \quad I = \frac { 1 } { 20 } \log 3{/tex}

According to the question, {tex}I=\int \frac { 5 x - 2 } { 1 + 2 x + 3 x ^ { 2 } } d x{/tex}
{tex}(5x - 2){/tex} can be written as ,
{tex}5 x - 2 = A \frac { d } { d x } \left( 1 + 2 x + 3 x ^ { 2 } \right) + B{/tex}
{tex}I=\int \frac { 5 x - 2 } { 1 + 2 x + 3 x ^ { 2 } } d x =\int \frac { A \frac { d } { d x } \left( 1 + 2 x + 3 x ^ { 2 } \right) + B } { 1 + 2 x + 3 x ^ { 2 } } d x{/tex}...(i)

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}5x - 2 = A(2 + 6x) + B{/tex}
Comparing the coefficients of x and constant terms, 
{tex}5 = 6 A \Rightarrow A = \frac { 5 } { 6 }{/tex}
and {tex}- 2 = 2 A + B \Rightarrow B = - 2 A - 2{/tex}
{tex}= - \frac { 5 } { 3 } - 2 = - \frac { 11 } { 3 }{/tex} {tex}\left[ \because A = \frac { 5 } { 6 } \right]{/tex}
From Eq. (i), we get
{tex}I = \int \frac { \frac { 5 } { 6 } ( 2 + 6 x ) - \frac { 11 } { 3 } } { 1 + 2 x + 3 x ^ { 2 } } d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I = \int \frac { \frac { 5 } { 6 } ( 2 + 6 x ) } { 1 + 2 x + 3 x ^ { 2 } } d x - \int \frac { \left( \frac { 11 } { 3 } \right) } { 1 + 2 x + 3 x ^ { 2 } } d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I=I_1-I_2{/tex} ...(ii)
where, {tex}I _ { 1 } = \frac { 5 } { 6 } \int \frac { 2 + 6 x } { 1 + 2 x + 3 x ^ { 2 } } d x{/tex}
Put {tex}1 + 2x + 3x^2 = t {/tex}{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}(2+6x) dx = dt{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad I _ { 1 } = \frac { 5 } { 6 } \int \frac { d t } { t } = \frac { 5 } { 6 } \log | t | + C _ { 1 }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 5 } { 6 } \log \left| 1 + 2 x + 3 x ^ { 2 } \right| + C _ { 1 }{/tex} [{tex}t = 1 + 2x + 3x^2{/tex}]
where, {tex}I _ { 2 } = \frac { 11 } { 3 } \int \frac { d x } { 3 x ^ { 2 } + 2 x + 1 }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 11 } { 9 } \int \frac { d x } { \left[ x ^ { 2 } + \frac { 2 x } { 3 } + \frac { 1 } { 3 } \right] }{/tex}

{tex}= \frac { 11 } { 9 } \int \frac { d x } { \left[ x ^ { 2 } + \frac { 2 x } { 3 } + \frac { 1 } { 3 } + \frac {1} {9}- \frac {1} {9}\right] }{/tex}

{tex}= \frac { 11 } { 9 } \int \frac { d x } { \left[ x ^ { 2 } + \frac { 2 x } { 3 } + \frac { 1 } { 9} + \frac {1} {3}- \frac {1} {9}\right] }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 11 } { 9 } \int \frac { d x } { \left( x + \frac { 1 } { 3 } \right) ^ { 2 } + \frac { 2 } { 9 } } [ \because (a+b)^2 = a^2+b^2+2ab]{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 11 } { 9 } \cdot \frac { 1 } { \frac { \sqrt { 2 } } { 3 } } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { x + \frac { 1 } { 3 } } { \frac { \sqrt { 2 } } { 3 } } \right) + C _ { 2 }{/tex}{tex}\left[ \because \int \frac { 1 } { x ^ { 2 } + a ^ { 2 } } d x = \frac { 1 } { a } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { x } { a } \right) + c \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 11 } { 3 \sqrt { 2 } } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { 3 x + 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } } \right) + C _ { 2 }{/tex}
Putting the values of {tex}I_1\ and\ I_2{/tex} in Equation (ii), 

{tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { 5 } { 6 } \log \left| 1 + 2 x + 3 x ^ { 2 } \right|+C_1 - \frac { 11 } { 3 \sqrt { 2 } } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { 3 x + 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } } \right) - C_2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { 5 } { 6 } \log \left| 1 + 2 x + 3 x ^ { 2 } \right| - \frac { 11 } { 3 \sqrt { 2 } } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { 3 x + 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } } \right) + C [\because C=C_1-C_2]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Consider {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} and put x 2 = y Then, {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}=\frac{y}{(y+1)(y+4)}{/tex} Write, {tex}\frac{y}{(y+1)(y+4)}=\frac{A}{y+1}+\frac{B}{y+4}{/tex} So that, y = A (y + 4) + B (y + 1) Comparing coefficients of y and constant terms on both sides, we get A + B = 1 and 4A + B = 0, which give {tex}A=-\frac{1}{3}{/tex} and {tex}B=\frac{4}{3}{/tex} Thus, {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}=-\frac{1}{3\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\frac{4}{3\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{3} \tan ^{-1} x+\frac{4}{3} \times \frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}+C{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{3} \tan ^{-1} x+\frac{2}{3} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}+C{/tex}
solution: Consider {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} and put x 2 = y Then, {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}=\frac{y}{(y+1)(y+4)}{/tex} Write, {tex}\frac{y}{(y+1)(y+4)}=\frac{A}{y+1}+\frac{B}{y+4}{/tex} So that, y = A (y + 4) + B (y + 1) Comparing coefficients of y and constant terms on both sides, we get A + B = 1 and 4A + B = 0, which give {tex}A=-\frac{1}{3}{/tex} and {tex}B=\frac{4}{3}{/tex} Thus, {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}=-\frac{1}{3\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\frac{4}{3\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{3} \tan ^{-1} x+\frac{4}{3} \times \frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}+C{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{3} \tan ^{-1} x+\frac{2}{3} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Consider {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} and put x2 = y
Then, {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}=\frac{y}{(y+1)(y+4)}{/tex} 
Write, {tex}\frac{y}{(y+1)(y+4)}=\frac{A}{y+1}+\frac{B}{y+4}{/tex} 
So that, y = A (y + 4) + B (y + 1)
Comparing coefficients of y and constant terms on both sides,
we get A + B = 1 and 4A + B = 0, which give
{tex}A=-\frac{1}{3}{/tex} and {tex}B=\frac{4}{3}{/tex} 
Thus, {tex}\frac{x^{2}}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+4\right)}=-\frac{1}{3\left(x^{2}+1\right)}+\frac{4}{3\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{/tex} 
= {tex}-\frac{1}{3} \tan ^{-1} x+\frac{4}{3} \times \frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}+C{/tex} 
= {tex}-\frac{1}{3} \tan ^{-1} x+\frac{2}{3} \tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}+C{/tex}

question_html: Evaluate {tex}\int \frac { \sin ^ { 6 } x + \cos ^ { 6 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x.{/tex}
question_text: Evaluate {tex}\int \frac { \sin ^ { 6 } x + \cos ^ { 6 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x.{/tex}
explanation: Let {tex}I = \int \frac { \sin ^ { 6 } x + \cos ^ { 6 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \int \frac { \left( \sin ^ { 2 } x \right) ^ { 3 } + \left( \cos ^ { 2 } x \right) ^ { 3 } } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { \left[ \begin{array} { l } { \left( \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x \right) ^ { 3 } } \\ { - 3 \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x \left( \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x \right) } \end{array} \right] } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}[\because a^3 + b^3 = (a + b)^3 - 3ab(a + b)]{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { ( 1 ) ^ { 3 } - 3 \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} [ {tex} \because{/tex} sin 2 x + cos 2 x = 1] {tex}= \int \frac { 1 } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x - 3 \int \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x - 3 \int 1 d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \left[ \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } + \frac { \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } \right] d x - 3 \int 1 d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \left( \sec ^ { 2 } x + \cot ^ { 2 } x \right) d x - 3 \int 1 d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \sec ^ { 2 } x d x + \int \cot ^ { 2 } x d x - 3 \int 1 d x{/tex} {tex}= tan\ x - cot x -3x +C{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}I = \int \frac { \sin ^ { 6 } x + \cos ^ { 6 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \int \frac { \left( \sin ^ { 2 } x \right) ^ { 3 } + \left( \cos ^ { 2 } x \right) ^ { 3 } } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { \left[ \begin{array} { l } { \left( \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x \right) ^ { 3 } } \\ { - 3 \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x \left( \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x \right) } \end{array} \right] } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}[\because a^3 + b^3 = (a + b)^3 - 3ab(a + b)]{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { ( 1 ) ^ { 3 } - 3 \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} [ {tex} \because{/tex} sin 2 x + cos 2 x = 1] {tex}= \int \frac { 1 } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x - 3 \int \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x - 3 \int 1 d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \left[ \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } + \frac { \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } \right] d x - 3 \int 1 d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \left( \sec ^ { 2 } x + \cot ^ { 2 } x \right) d x - 3 \int 1 d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \sec ^ { 2 } x d x + \int \cot ^ { 2 } x d x - 3 \int 1 d x{/tex} {tex}= tan\ x - cot x -3x +C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int \frac { \sin ^ { 6 } x + \cos ^ { 6 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \int \frac { \left( \sin ^ { 2 } x \right) ^ { 3 } + \left( \cos ^ { 2 } x \right) ^ { 3 } } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \frac { \left[ \begin{array} { l } { \left( \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x \right) ^ { 3 } } \\ { - 3 \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x \left( \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x \right) } \end{array} \right] } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
{tex}[\because a^3 + b^3 = (a + b)^3 - 3ab(a + b)]{/tex}
{tex}= \int \frac { ( 1 ) ^ { 3 } - 3 \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
[{tex} \because{/tex}sin2x + cos2 x = 1]
{tex}= \int \frac { 1 } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x - 3 \int \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x - 3 \int 1 d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \left[ \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } + \frac { \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } \right] d x - 3 \int 1 d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \left( \sec ^ { 2 } x + \cot ^ { 2 } x \right) d x - 3 \int 1 d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \sec ^ { 2 } x d x + \int \cot ^ { 2 } x d x - 3 \int 1 d x{/tex}
{tex}= tan\ x - cot x -3x +C{/tex}

question_html: Find {tex}\int \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x - \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x.{/tex}
question_text: Find {tex}\int \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x - \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x.{/tex}
explanation: Let I = {tex}\int \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x - \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x - \int \frac { \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \sec ^ { 2 } x d x - \int \cos e c ^ { 2 } x d x{/tex} = tan x + cot x + C
solution: Let I = {tex}\int \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x - \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x - \int \frac { \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \sec ^ { 2 } x d x - \int \cos e c ^ { 2 } x d x{/tex} = tan x + cot x + C
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x - \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \frac { \sin ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x - \int \frac { \cos ^ { 2 } x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \sec ^ { 2 } x d x - \int \cos e c ^ { 2 } x d x{/tex}
= tan x + cot x + C

solution: Let {tex}I = \int \frac { d x } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x }{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { \left( \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x \right) } { \sin ^ { 2 } x \cdot \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}\left[ \because \sin ^ { 2 } \theta + \cos ^ { 2 } \theta = 1 \right]{/tex} {tex}= \int \left( \sec ^ { 2 } x + \ cosec ^ { 2 } x \right) d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \sec ^ { 2 } x d x + \int \csc ^ { 2 } x d x{/tex} = tan x - cot x + C

solution: Let I {tex} = \int \cos ^ { - 1 } ( \sin x ) d x{/tex} {tex} = \int \cos ^ { - 1 } \left[ \cos \left( \frac { \pi } { 2 } - x \right) \right] d x{/tex} {tex} = \int \left( \frac { \pi } { 2 } - x \right) d x{/tex} {tex} \left[ \because \cos ^ { - 1 } ( \cos \theta ) = \theta \right]{/tex} {tex} = \frac { \pi } { 2 } \int d x - \int x d x{/tex} {tex} = \frac { \pi } { 2 } x - \frac { x ^ { 2 } } { 2 } + C{/tex}

question_html: Write the anti-derivative of {tex}\left( 3 \sqrt { x } + \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { x } } \right).{/tex}
question_text: Write the anti-derivative of {tex}\left( 3 \sqrt { x } + \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { x } } \right).{/tex}
explanation: Anti-derivative of {tex}\left( 3 \sqrt { x } + \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { x } } \right){/tex} {tex}= \int \left( 3 \sqrt { x } + \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { x } } \right) d x = 3 \int \sqrt { x } d x + \int \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { x } } d x{/tex} {tex}=3 \left( \frac { x ^ { \frac{1 }{ 2} + 1 } } { \frac{1}{ 2} + 1 } \right) + \left[ \frac { x ^ {\frac{ - 1} { 2} + 1 } } { \frac{- 1}{ 2} + 1 } \right] + C = 2 \left( x ^ { 3 / 2 } + x ^ { 1 / 2 } \right) + C{/tex}
solution: Anti-derivative of {tex}\left( 3 \sqrt { x } + \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { x } } \right){/tex} {tex}= \int \left( 3 \sqrt { x } + \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { x } } \right) d x = 3 \int \sqrt { x } d x + \int \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { x } } d x{/tex} {tex}=3 \left( \frac { x ^ { \frac{1 }{ 2} + 1 } } { \frac{1}{ 2} + 1 } \right) + \left[ \frac { x ^ {\frac{ - 1} { 2} + 1 } } { \frac{- 1}{ 2} + 1 } \right] + C = 2 \left( x ^ { 3 / 2 } + x ^ { 1 / 2 } \right) + C{/tex}
solution_html:
Anti-derivative of {tex}\left( 3 \sqrt { x } + \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { x } } \right){/tex}
{tex}= \int \left( 3 \sqrt { x } + \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { x } } \right) d x = 3 \int \sqrt { x } d x + \int \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { x } } d x{/tex}
{tex}=3 \left( \frac { x ^ { \frac{1 }{ 2} + 1 } } { \frac{1}{ 2} + 1 } \right) + \left[ \frac { x ^ {\frac{ - 1} { 2} + 1 } } { \frac{- 1}{ 2} + 1 } \right] + C = 2 \left( x ^ { 3 / 2 } + x ^ { 1 / 2 } \right) + C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Evaluate the definite integral {tex}\int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } x e ^ { x ^ { 2 } } d x.{/tex}
question_text: Evaluate the definite integral {tex}\int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } x e ^ { x ^ { 2 } } d x.{/tex}
explanation: Let {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } x e ^ { x ^ { 2 } } d x{/tex} Put {tex}x ^ { 2 } = t \Rightarrow 2 x d x = d t \Rightarrow d x = \frac { d t } { 2 x }{/tex} Lower limit when x = 0, then t = 0 Upper limit when x = 1, then t = 1. {tex}\therefore \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } x e ^ { t } \frac { d t } { 2 x } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } e ^ { t } d t{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left[ e ^ { t } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left[ e ^ { 1 } - e ^ { 0 } \right] = \frac { 1 } { 2 } [ e - 1 ]{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } x e ^ { x ^ { 2 } } d x{/tex} Put {tex}x ^ { 2 } = t \Rightarrow 2 x d x = d t \Rightarrow d x = \frac { d t } { 2 x }{/tex} Lower limit when x = 0, then t = 0 Upper limit when x = 1, then t = 1. {tex}\therefore \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } x e ^ { t } \frac { d t } { 2 x } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } e ^ { t } d t{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left[ e ^ { t } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left[ e ^ { 1 } - e ^ { 0 } \right] = \frac { 1 } { 2 } [ e - 1 ]{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } x e ^ { x ^ { 2 } } d x{/tex}
Put {tex}x ^ { 2 } = t \Rightarrow 2 x d x = d t \Rightarrow d x = \frac { d t } { 2 x }{/tex}
Lower limit when x = 0, then t = 0
Upper limit when x = 1, then t = 1.
{tex}\therefore \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } x e ^ { t } \frac { d t } { 2 x } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } e ^ { t } d t{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left[ e ^ { t } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left[ e ^ { 1 } - e ^ { 0 } \right] = \frac { 1 } { 2 } [ e - 1 ]{/tex}

explanation: According to the question , {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 1 - x ^ { 2 } } } d x{/tex} = {tex}\left[ \sin ^ { - 1 } x \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \quad \left[ \because \int_b^a \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 1 - x ^ { 2 } } } d x = \sin ^ { - 1 } (a) - \sin ^ { - 1 } (b) \right]{/tex} {tex}= sin^{-1}(1)-sin^{-1}(0){/tex} {tex}= \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \sin \frac { \pi } { 2 } \right) - \sin ^ { - 1 } ( \sin 0 ) {/tex} {tex}= \frac { \pi } { 2 } - 0 = \frac { \pi } { 2 }{/tex}
solution: According to the question , {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 1 - x ^ { 2 } } } d x{/tex} = {tex}\left[ \sin ^ { - 1 } x \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \quad \left[ \because \int_b^a \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 1 - x ^ { 2 } } } d x = \sin ^ { - 1 } (a) - \sin ^ { - 1 } (b) \right]{/tex} {tex}= sin^{-1}(1)-sin^{-1}(0){/tex} {tex}= \sin ^ { - 1 } \left( \sin \frac { \pi } { 2 } \right) - \sin ^ { - 1 } ( \sin 0 ) {/tex} {tex}= \frac { \pi } { 2 } - 0 = \frac { \pi } { 2 }{/tex}
solution_html:
According to the question , {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 1 - x ^ { 2 } } } d x{/tex}
= {tex}\left[ \sin ^ { - 1 } x \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \quad \left[ \because \int_b^a \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 1 - x ^ { 2 } } } d x = \sin ^ { - 1 } (a) - \sin ^ { - 1 } (b) \right]{/tex}
{tex}= sin^{-1}(1)-sin^{-1}(0){/tex}

{tex}= \frac { \pi } { 2 } - 0 = \frac { \pi } { 2 }{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If f(x) = {tex}\int _ { 0 } ^ { x }{/tex} t sin t dt, then write the value of f' (x).
question_text: If f(x) = {tex}\int _ { 0 } ^ { x }{/tex} t sin t dt, then write the value of f' (x).
explanation: Given, {tex}f ( x ) = \int _ { 0 } ^ { x } t \sin t d t{/tex} {tex}= \left[ t \int \sin t d t - \int \left[ \frac { d } { d t } ( t ) \int \sin t d t \right] d t \right] _ { 0 } ^ { x }{/tex} [using integration by parts] {tex}= [ t ( - \cos t ) ] _ { 0 } ^ { x } - \int _ { 0 } ^ { x } ( - \cos t ) d t{/tex} {tex}= [ - t \cos t ] _ { 0 } ^ { x } + [ \sin t ] _ { 0 } ^ { x }{/tex} = -x cos x + 0 + sin x - 0 = sin x - x cos x Thus, f(x) = sin x -x cos x On differentiating both sides w.r.t.x, we get {tex}f ^ { \prime } ( x ) = \cos x - \left[ x \frac { d } { d x } ( \cos x ) + \cos x \frac { d } { d x } ( x ) \right]{/tex} [by product rule of derivative] = cos x - [x ( (-sin x) + cos x] = cos x + x sin x - cos x = x sin x
solution: Given, {tex}f ( x ) = \int _ { 0 } ^ { x } t \sin t d t{/tex} {tex}= \left[ t \int \sin t d t - \int \left[ \frac { d } { d t } ( t ) \int \sin t d t \right] d t \right] _ { 0 } ^ { x }{/tex} [using integration by parts] {tex}= [ t ( - \cos t ) ] _ { 0 } ^ { x } - \int _ { 0 } ^ { x } ( - \cos t ) d t{/tex} {tex}= [ - t \cos t ] _ { 0 } ^ { x } + [ \sin t ] _ { 0 } ^ { x }{/tex} = -x cos x + 0 + sin x - 0 = sin x - x cos x Thus, f(x) = sin x -x cos x On differentiating both sides w.r.t.x, we get {tex}f ^ { \prime } ( x ) = \cos x - \left[ x \frac { d } { d x } ( \cos x ) + \cos x \frac { d } { d x } ( x ) \right]{/tex} [by product rule of derivative] = cos x - [x ( (-sin x) + cos x] = cos x + x sin x - cos x = x sin x
solution_html:
Given, {tex}f ( x ) = \int _ { 0 } ^ { x } t \sin t d t{/tex}
{tex}= \left[ t \int \sin t d t - \int \left[ \frac { d } { d t } ( t ) \int \sin t d t \right] d t \right] _ { 0 } ^ { x }{/tex}  [using integration by parts]
{tex}= [ t ( - \cos t ) ] _ { 0 } ^ { x } - \int _ { 0 } ^ { x } ( - \cos t ) d t{/tex}
{tex}= [ - t \cos t ] _ { 0 } ^ { x } + [ \sin t ] _ { 0 } ^ { x }{/tex}
= -x cos x + 0 + sin x - 0
= sin x - x cos x
Thus, f(x) = sin x -x cos x
On differentiating both sides w.r.t.x, we get
{tex}f ^ { \prime } ( x ) = \cos x - \left[ x \frac { d } { d x } ( \cos x ) + \cos x \frac { d } { d x } ( x ) \right]{/tex}
[by product rule of derivative]
= cos x - [x ( (-sin x) + cos x]
= cos x + x sin x - cos x = x sin x

subject: Mathematics
explanation: Let {tex}I = \int _ { 2 } ^ { 4 } \frac { x } { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } d x{/tex} Put x 2 + 1 = t {tex}\Rightarrow 2 x d x = d t \Rightarrow x d x = \frac { d t } { 2 }{/tex} Lower limit when x = 2, then t = 2 2 + 1 = 5 Upper limit when x = 4, then t = 4 2 + 1 = 17. {tex}\therefore \quad I = \int _ { 5 } ^ { 17 } \frac { d t } { 2t } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int _ { 5 } ^ { 17 } \frac { 1 } { t } d t = \frac { 1 } { 2 } [ \log | t | ] _ { 5 } ^ { 17 }{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } [ \log 17 - \log 5 ] = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \left( \frac { 17 } { 5 } \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ \because \log m - \log n = \log \left( \frac { m } { n } \right) \right]{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}I = \int _ { 2 } ^ { 4 } \frac { x } { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } d x{/tex} Put x 2 + 1 = t {tex}\Rightarrow 2 x d x = d t \Rightarrow x d x = \frac { d t } { 2 }{/tex} Lower limit when x = 2, then t = 2 2 + 1 = 5 Upper limit when x = 4, then t = 4 2 + 1 = 17. {tex}\therefore \quad I = \int _ { 5 } ^ { 17 } \frac { d t } { 2t } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int _ { 5 } ^ { 17 } \frac { 1 } { t } d t = \frac { 1 } { 2 } [ \log | t | ] _ { 5 } ^ { 17 }{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } [ \log 17 - \log 5 ] = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \left( \frac { 17 } { 5 } \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ \because \log m - \log n = \log \left( \frac { m } { n } \right) \right]{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int _ { 2 } ^ { 4 } \frac { x } { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } d x{/tex}
Put x2 + 1 = t {tex}\Rightarrow 2 x d x = d t \Rightarrow x d x = \frac { d t } { 2 }{/tex}
Lower limit when x = 2, then t = 22 + 1 = 5
Upper limit when x = 4, then t = 42 + 1 = 17.
{tex}\therefore \quad I = \int _ { 5 } ^ { 17 } \frac { d t } { 2t } = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int _ { 5 } ^ { 17 } \frac { 1 } { t } d t = \frac { 1 } { 2 } [ \log | t | ] _ { 5 } ^ { 17 }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } [ \log 17 - \log 5 ] = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \left( \frac { 17 } { 5 } \right){/tex}{tex}\left[ \because \log m - \log n = \log \left( \frac { m } { n } \right) \right]{/tex}

explanation: Let {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 3 } \frac { d x } { 9 + x ^ { 2 } } \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 3 } \frac { d x } { x ^ { 2 } + ( 3 ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \left[ \frac { 1 } { 3 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \frac { x } { 3 } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 3 } \left[ \because \int \frac { d x } { x ^ { 2 } + a ^ { 2 } } = \frac { 1 } { a } \tan ^ { - 1 } \frac { x } { a } + C \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { 1 } { 3 } \left[ \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { 3 } { 3 } \right) - \tan ^ { - 1 } ( 0 ) \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 3 } \left[ \tan ^ { - 1 } ( 1 ) - 0 \right] = \frac { 1 } { 3 } \left( \frac { \pi } { 4 } \right) = \frac { \pi } { 12 }{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 3 } \frac { d x } { 9 + x ^ { 2 } } \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 3 } \frac { d x } { x ^ { 2 } + ( 3 ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \left[ \frac { 1 } { 3 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \frac { x } { 3 } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 3 } \left[ \because \int \frac { d x } { x ^ { 2 } + a ^ { 2 } } = \frac { 1 } { a } \tan ^ { - 1 } \frac { x } { a } + C \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { 1 } { 3 } \left[ \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { 3 } { 3 } \right) - \tan ^ { - 1 } ( 0 ) \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 3 } \left[ \tan ^ { - 1 } ( 1 ) - 0 \right] = \frac { 1 } { 3 } \left( \frac { \pi } { 4 } \right) = \frac { \pi } { 12 }{/tex}

Let {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 3 } \frac { d x } { 9 + x ^ { 2 } } \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 3 } \frac { d x } { x ^ { 2 } + ( 3 ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \left[ \frac { 1 } { 3 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \frac { x } { 3 } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 3 } \left[ \because \int \frac { d x } { x ^ { 2 } + a ^ { 2 } } = \frac { 1 } { a } \tan ^ { - 1 } \frac { x } { a } + C \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { 1 } { 3 } \left[ \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { 3 } { 3 } \right) - \tan ^ { - 1 } ( 0 ) \right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Evaluate {tex}\int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } e ^ { x } ( \sin x - \cos x ) d x.{/tex}
question_text: Evaluate {tex}\int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } e ^ { x } ( \sin x - \cos x ) d x.{/tex}
explanation: Let {tex}I={/tex} {tex}\int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } e ^ { x }{/tex} (sin x - cos x) dx {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I = - \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 }{/tex} e x (cos x - sin x) dx Now, consider, f(x) = cos x then f'(x) = -sin x Now, by using {tex}\int e ^ { x } \left[ f ( x ) + f ^ { \prime } ( x ) \right] d x = e ^ { x } f ( x ) + C,{/tex} we get, {tex}I={/tex} - {tex}\left[ e ^ { x } \cos x \right] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 }{/tex} {tex}= - e ^ { \pi / 2 } \cos \frac { \pi } { 2 } + e ^ { 0 } \cos ( 0 ){/tex} = 0 + 1(1) = 1
solution: Let {tex}I={/tex} {tex}\int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } e ^ { x }{/tex} (sin x - cos x) dx {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I = - \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 }{/tex} e x (cos x - sin x) dx Now, consider, f(x) = cos x then f'(x) = -sin x Now, by using {tex}\int e ^ { x } \left[ f ( x ) + f ^ { \prime } ( x ) \right] d x = e ^ { x } f ( x ) + C,{/tex} we get, {tex}I={/tex} - {tex}\left[ e ^ { x } \cos x \right] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 }{/tex} {tex}= - e ^ { \pi / 2 } \cos \frac { \pi } { 2 } + e ^ { 0 } \cos ( 0 ){/tex} = 0 + 1(1) = 1
solution_html:
Let {tex}I={/tex} {tex}\int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } e ^ { x }{/tex} (sin x - cos x) dx
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I = - \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 }{/tex}ex (cos x - sin x) dx
Now, consider, f(x) = cos x
then f'(x) = -sin x
Now, by using {tex}\int e ^ { x } \left[ f ( x ) + f ^ { \prime } ( x ) \right] d x = e ^ { x } f ( x ) + C,{/tex}
we get,  {tex}I={/tex}  -{tex}\left[ e ^ { x } \cos x \right] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 }{/tex}
{tex}= - e ^ { \pi / 2 } \cos \frac { \pi } { 2 } + e ^ { 0 } \cos ( 0 ){/tex}
= 0 + 1(1) = 1

question_html: Evaluate {tex} \int _ { e } ^ { e ^ { 2 } } \frac { d x } { x \log x }.{/tex}
question_text: Evaluate {tex} \int _ { e } ^ { e ^ { 2 } } \frac { d x } { x \log x }.{/tex}
explanation: Let {tex}I= \int _ { e } ^ { e ^ { 2 } } \frac { d x } { x \log x }{/tex} Put log x = {tex}t \Rightarrow \frac { 1 } { x } d x = d t{/tex} Lower limit when x = e, then t = log e = 1 Upper limit when x = e 2 , then t = log e 2 = 2 {tex}\therefore I = \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \frac { d t } { t } = [ \log | t | ] _ { 1 } ^ { 2 }{/tex} = log 2 - log1 = log2
solution: Let {tex}I= \int _ { e } ^ { e ^ { 2 } } \frac { d x } { x \log x }{/tex} Put log x = {tex}t \Rightarrow \frac { 1 } { x } d x = d t{/tex} Lower limit when x = e, then t = log e = 1 Upper limit when x = e 2 , then t = log e 2 = 2 {tex}\therefore I = \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \frac { d t } { t } = [ \log | t | ] _ { 1 } ^ { 2 }{/tex} = log 2 - log1 = log2
solution_html:
Let {tex}I= \int _ { e } ^ { e ^ { 2 } } \frac { d x } { x \log x }{/tex}
Put log x = {tex}t \Rightarrow \frac { 1 } { x } d x = d t{/tex}
Lower limit when x = e, then t = log e = 1
Upper limit when x = e2, then t = log e2 = 2
{tex}\therefore I = \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \frac { d t } { t } = [ \log | t | ] _ { 1 } ^ { 2 }{/tex}= log 2 - log1 = log2

According to the question, {tex}I=\int ( x - 3 ) \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } + 3 x - 18 } d x{/tex}
x - 3  can be written as
x - 3 {tex} = A \frac { d } { d x } {/tex}(x2 + 3 x - 18) + B
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x - 3 = A(2x + 3) + B
comparing the coefficients of x and constant terms from both sides,
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} 2A = 1
and 3A + B = -3
{tex}\Rightarrow A = \frac { 1 } { 2 } {/tex} and {tex}3 \times \frac { 1 } { 2 } + B {/tex} = -3
{tex}\Rightarrow A = \frac { 1 } { 2 } {/tex} and B = {tex} -\frac { 3 } { 2 }{/tex} - 3
{tex}\Rightarrow A = \frac { 1 } { 2 } {/tex} and {tex}B = - \frac { 9 } { 2 }{/tex}
The given integral reduces in the following form :
{tex}I = \int \left\{ \frac { 1 } { 2 } ( 2 x + 3 ) - \frac { 9 } { 2 } \right\} \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } + 3 x - 18 } d x{/tex}

Put x2 + 3x - 18 = t

{tex}= \int \sqrt { \left( x + \frac { 3 } { 2 } \right) ^ { 2 } - \left( \frac { 9 } { 2 } \right) ^ { 2 } } d x{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { \left( x + \frac { 3 } { 2 } \right) } { 2 } \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } + 3 x - 18 }{/tex}{tex}- \frac { 81 } { 8 } \log \left| \left( x + \frac { 3 } { 2 } \right) + \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } + 3 x - 18 } \right| + C _ { 2 }{/tex}{tex}{\left[ {\because \int {\sqrt {{x^2} - {a^2}} } dx} \right.}{/tex} {tex}\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\left. { = \frac{x}{2}\sqrt {{x^2} - {a^2}} - \frac{{{a^2}}}{2}\log \left| {x + \sqrt {{x^2} - {a^2}} } \right| + c} \right]} \end{array}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 2 x + 3 } { 4 } \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } + 3 x - 18 }{/tex}{tex}- \frac { 81 } { 8 } \log \left| \frac { 2 x + 3 } { 2 } + \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } + 3 x - 18 } \right| + C _ { 2 }{/tex}
Putting the values of {tex}I_1{/tex} and {tex}I_2{/tex} in Eq. (i),
{tex} \Rightarrow I = \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\frac{2}{3}{{\left( {{x^2} + 3x - 18} \right)}^{\frac{3}{2}}} + {C_1}} \right]{/tex}{tex}- \frac { 9 } { 2 } \left[ \frac { 2 x + 3 } { 4 } \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } + 3 x - 18 }\right.{/tex}{tex}- \frac { 81 } { 8 } \log \left| \frac { 2 x + 3 } { 2 } + \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } + 3 x - 18 } \right| + C _ { 2 } \Big]{/tex}

question_html: Find {tex}\int \frac { x ^ { 3 } } { x ^ { 4 } + 3 x ^ { 2 } + 2 } d x.{/tex}
question_text: Find {tex}\int \frac { x ^ { 3 } } { x ^ { 4 } + 3 x ^ { 2 } + 2 } d x.{/tex}
explanation: According to the question, {tex}I = \int \frac { x ^ { 3 } } { x ^ { 4 } + 3 x ^ { 2 } + 2 } d x{/tex} {tex}I = \int \frac { x ^ { 2 }.x } { x ^ { 4 } + 3 x ^ { 2 } + 2 } d x{/tex} Let {tex}x ^ { 2 } = t \Rightarrow 2 x dx= { d t }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad x d x = \frac { d t } { 2 }{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad I = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { t } { t ^ { 2 } + 3 t + 2 } d t {/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { t } { ( t + 2 )( t + 1 ) } d t{/tex} By using partial fractions, {tex} \frac { t } { ( t + 2 )( t + 1 ) } = \frac {A}{t+2} +\frac {B}{t+1}{/tex} {tex}I= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { t } { ( t + 2 )( t + 1 ) } d t = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac {A}{t+2} +\frac {B}{t+1} dt{/tex} .(i) {tex}t = A(t+1)+B(t+2){/tex} if {tex}\ \ t = -2\Rightarrow -2=A(-1) {/tex} {tex}, \therefore A=2{/tex} if {tex}\ \ t = -1\Rightarrow -1=B(1) , \therefore B=-1{/tex} put values of A and B in (i) {tex}I= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left[ \int \frac { 2 } { t + 2 } d t - \int \frac { 1 } { t + 1 } d t \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } [ 2 \log | t + 2 | - \log | t + 1 | ] + C{/tex} {tex}= \log | t + 2 | - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log | t + 1 | + C{/tex} {tex}= \log | t + 2 | - \log \sqrt { t + 1 } + C{/tex} {tex}= \log \left| \frac { t + 2 } { \sqrt { t + 1 } } \right| + C {/tex} {tex}put\ t = x^2{/tex} {tex}I= \log \left| \frac { x ^ { 2 } + 2 } { \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } } \right| + C{/tex}
solution: According to the question, {tex}I = \int \frac { x ^ { 3 } } { x ^ { 4 } + 3 x ^ { 2 } + 2 } d x{/tex} {tex}I = \int \frac { x ^ { 2 }.x } { x ^ { 4 } + 3 x ^ { 2 } + 2 } d x{/tex} Let {tex}x ^ { 2 } = t \Rightarrow 2 x dx= { d t }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad x d x = \frac { d t } { 2 }{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad I = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { t } { t ^ { 2 } + 3 t + 2 } d t {/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { t } { ( t + 2 )( t + 1 ) } d t{/tex} By using partial fractions, {tex} \frac { t } { ( t + 2 )( t + 1 ) } = \frac {A}{t+2} +\frac {B}{t+1}{/tex} {tex}I= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { t } { ( t + 2 )( t + 1 ) } d t = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac {A}{t+2} +\frac {B}{t+1} dt{/tex} .(i) {tex}t = A(t+1)+B(t+2){/tex} if {tex}\ \ t = -2\Rightarrow -2=A(-1) {/tex} {tex}, \therefore A=2{/tex} if {tex}\ \ t = -1\Rightarrow -1=B(1) , \therefore B=-1{/tex} put values of A and B in (i) {tex}I= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left[ \int \frac { 2 } { t + 2 } d t - \int \frac { 1 } { t + 1 } d t \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } [ 2 \log | t + 2 | - \log | t + 1 | ] + C{/tex} {tex}= \log | t + 2 | - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log | t + 1 | + C{/tex} {tex}= \log | t + 2 | - \log \sqrt { t + 1 } + C{/tex} {tex}= \log \left| \frac { t + 2 } { \sqrt { t + 1 } } \right| + C {/tex} {tex}put\ t = x^2{/tex} {tex}I= \log \left| \frac { x ^ { 2 } + 2 } { \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } } \right| + C{/tex}
solution_html:
According to the question,  {tex}I = \int \frac { x ^ { 3 } } { x ^ { 4 } + 3 x ^ { 2 } + 2 } d x{/tex}

{tex}I = \int \frac { x ^ { 2 }.x } { x ^ { 4 } + 3 x ^ { 2 } + 2 } d x{/tex}
Let {tex}x ^ { 2 } = t \Rightarrow 2 x dx= { d t }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x d x = \frac { d t } { 2 }{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad I = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { t } { t ^ { 2 } + 3 t + 2 } d t {/tex}

{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { t } { ( t + 2 )( t + 1 ) } d t{/tex}

By using partial fractions,

{tex} \frac { t } { ( t + 2 )( t + 1 ) } = \frac {A}{t+2} +\frac {B}{t+1}{/tex}

{tex}I= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { t } { ( t + 2 )( t + 1 ) } d t = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac {A}{t+2} +\frac {B}{t+1} dt{/tex}    .(i)

{tex}t = A(t+1)+B(t+2){/tex}

if  {tex}\ \ t = -2\Rightarrow -2=A(-1) {/tex}   {tex}, \therefore A=2{/tex}

if {tex}\ \ t = -1\Rightarrow -1=B(1) , \therefore B=-1{/tex}

put values of A and B in (i)
{tex}I= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left[ \int \frac { 2 } { t + 2 } d t - \int \frac { 1 } { t + 1 } d t \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } [ 2 \log | t + 2 | - \log | t + 1 | ] + C{/tex}
{tex}= \log | t + 2 | - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log | t + 1 | + C{/tex}
{tex}= \log | t + 2 | - \log \sqrt { t + 1 } + C{/tex}
{tex}= \log \left| \frac { t + 2 } { \sqrt { t + 1 } } \right| + C {/tex}

{tex}put\ t = x^2{/tex}

{tex}I= \log \left| \frac { x ^ { 2 } + 2 } { \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } } \right| + C{/tex}

2 + 2 + 1 } d t {/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { 1 + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } } { \left( t - \frac { 1 } { t } \right) ^ { 2 } + 3 } d t{/tex} Now, put {tex}t - \frac { 1 } { t } = u{/tex} , {tex}\Rightarrow \left( 1 + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } \right) d t = d u{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad I = \int \frac { d u } { u ^ { 2 } + ( \sqrt { 3 } ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I = \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 3 } } \tan ^ { - solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int \frac { 1 } { \sin ^ { 4 } x + \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 4 } x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \int \frac { dx } { \sin ^ { 4 } x + \sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 4 } x } {/tex}
Dividing numerator and denominator by cos4 x , we get
{tex}I = \int \frac { \frac{1}{cos^4x} } { \frac{\sin ^ { 4 } x }{cos^4x}+ \frac {\sin ^ { 2 } x \cos ^ { 2 } x }{cos^4x}+ \frac {\cos ^ { 4 } x } {cos^4x}}d x{/tex}
{tex}I = \int \frac { \sec ^ { 4 } x } { \tan ^ { 4 } x + \tan ^ { 2 } x + 1 } d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \frac { \left( \sec ^ { 2 } x \right) \left( \sec ^ { 2 } x \right) } { \tan ^ { 4 } x + \tan ^ { 2 } x + 1 } d x{/tex}
Put , tan x = t 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}sec^2x{/tex}dx = dt
we know that ,
sec2 x = 1 + tan2 x
= 1 + t2
{tex}\therefore I = \int \frac { 1 + t ^ { 2 } } { t ^ { 4 } + t ^ { 2 } + 1 } d t {/tex}
Dividing numerator and denominator by t2 , we get 
{tex}\therefore ~I= \int \frac { 1 + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } } { t ^ { 2 } + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } + 1 } d t{/tex}
{tex}= \int \frac { 1 + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } } { t ^ { 2 } + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } - 2 + 2 + 1 } d t {/tex}
{tex}= \int \frac { 1 + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } } { \left( t - \frac { 1 } { t } \right) ^ { 2 } + 3 } d t{/tex}
Now, put {tex}t - \frac { 1 } { t } = u{/tex} ,
{tex}\Rightarrow \left( 1 + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } \right) d t = d u{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad I = \int \frac { d u } { u ^ { 2 } + ( \sqrt { 3 } ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I = \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 3 } } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { u } { \sqrt { 3 } } \right) + C{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I = \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 3 } } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { t ^ { 2 } - 1 } { \sqrt { 3 } \ t} \right) + C{/tex}  {tex}\left[ \because u = t - \frac { 1 } { t } \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I = \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 3 } } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { \tan ^ { 2 } x - 1 } { \sqrt { 3 } \tan x } \right) + C{/tex}  {tex}[\because tan x = t ]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad I = \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 3 } } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { \tan ^ { 2 } x - 1 } { \sqrt { 3 } \tan x } \right) + C{/tex}

2 } d t{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { 1 + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } } { \left( t - \frac { 1 } { t } \right) ^ { 2 } + 2 }dt{/tex} Put , {tex} t - \frac { 1 } { t } = u{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \left( 1 + \frac { 1 } { t ^ { 2 } } \right) d t = d u{/tex} Then , {tex} I = \int \frac { d u } { u ^ { 2 } + ( \sqrt { 2 } ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { u } { \sqrt { 2 } } \right) + C{/tex} {tex} \left[ \because \int \frac { d x } { \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } + a ^ { 2 } } } = \frac { 1 } { a } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { x } { a } \right) + C \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 2 } } \tan ^ { -
