explanation: Let I = {tex}\int \frac{\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{\left(x^{4}+16\right)} d x{/tex} , then {tex}I=\int \frac{\left(1+\frac{4}{x^{2}}\right)}{\left(x^{2}+\frac{16}{x^{2}}\right)} d x{/tex} [dividing num. and den. by x 2 ] {tex}=\int \frac{\left(1+\frac{4}{x^{2}}\right)}{\left(x-\frac{4}{x}\right)^{2}+8} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{d t}{\left(t^{2}+8\right)}{/tex} Putting {tex}\left(x-\frac{4}{x}\right)=t{/tex} and {tex}\left(1+\frac{4}{x^{2}}\right) d x=d t{/tex} {tex}I=\int \frac{d t}{t^{2}+(\sqrt{8})^{2}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{8}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{t}{\sqrt{8}}\right)+C{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{8}} \tan ^{-1} \frac{\left(x-\frac{4}{x}\right)}{\sqrt{8}}+C{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{2}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x^{2}-4}{2 \sqrt{2 x}}\right)+C{/tex}
solution: Let I = {tex}\int \frac{\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{\left(x^{4}+16\right)} d x{/tex} , then {tex}I=\int \frac{\left(1+\frac{4}{x^{2}}\right)}{\left(x^{2}+\frac{16}{x^{2}}\right)} d x{/tex} [dividing num. and den. by x 2 ] {tex}=\int \frac{\left(1+\frac{4}{x^{2}}\right)}{\left(x-\frac{4}{x}\right)^{2}+8} d x{/tex} {tex}=\int \frac{d t}{\left(t^{2}+8\right)}{/tex} Putting {tex}\left(x-\frac{4}{x}\right)=t{/tex} and {tex}\left(1+\frac{4}{x^{2}}\right) d x=d t{/tex} {tex}I=\int \frac{d t}{t^{2}+(\sqrt{8})^{2}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{8}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{t}{\sqrt{8}}\right)+C{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{8}} \tan ^{-1} \frac{\left(x-\frac{4}{x}\right)}{\sqrt{8}}+C{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{2}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x^{2}-4}{2 \sqrt{2 x}}\right)+C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int \frac{\left(x^{2}+4\right)}{\left(x^{4}+16\right)} d x{/tex}, then 
{tex}I=\int \frac{\left(1+\frac{4}{x^{2}}\right)}{\left(x^{2}+\frac{16}{x^{2}}\right)} d x{/tex}        [dividing num. and den. by x2]
{tex}=\int \frac{\left(1+\frac{4}{x^{2}}\right)}{\left(x-\frac{4}{x}\right)^{2}+8} d x{/tex}
{tex}=\int \frac{d t}{\left(t^{2}+8\right)}{/tex} 
Putting {tex}\left(x-\frac{4}{x}\right)=t{/tex} and {tex}\left(1+\frac{4}{x^{2}}\right) d x=d t{/tex}
{tex}I=\int \frac{d t}{t^{2}+(\sqrt{8})^{2}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{8}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{t}{\sqrt{8}}\right)+C{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{8}} \tan ^{-1} \frac{\left(x-\frac{4}{x}\right)}{\sqrt{8}}+C{/tex}{tex}=\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{2}} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x^{2}-4}{2 \sqrt{2 x}}\right)+C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: To solve this we use substitution let {tex}x=a \cos 2 \theta{/tex} Differentiating w.r.t. x, we get {tex}d x=-2 a \sin 2 \theta{/tex} Now, {tex}x=-a \Rightarrow \theta=\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} {tex}x=a \Rightarrow \theta=0{/tex} {tex}\therefore \int_{-a}^{a} \sqrt{\frac{a-x}{a+x}} d x=\int_{\frac{-}{2}}^{0} \sqrt{\frac{a(1-\cos 2 \theta)}{a(1+\cos 2 \theta)}}(-2 \sin 2 \theta) d \theta{/tex} {tex}=2 a \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin \theta}{\cos \theta} \cdot \sin 2 \theta d \theta{/tex} [ {tex}\because 1-\cos 2 \theta=2 \sin ^{2} \theta{/tex} ., {tex}1+\cos 2 \theta=2 \cos ^{2} \theta{/tex} {tex}-\int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x=\int_{b}^{a} f(x) d x{/tex} ] {tex}=2 a \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin \theta \cdot 2 \sin \theta \cos \theta}{\cos \theta}{/tex} {tex}=4 a \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} s \operatorname{in}^{2} \theta d \theta{/tex} {tex}=2 a \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(1-\cos 2 \theta) d \theta{/tex} {tex}=2 a\left[\theta-\frac{\sin 2 \theta}{2}\right]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex} {tex}=2 a\left[\theta-\frac{\sin 2 \theta}{2}\right]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex} {tex}=2 a\left[\frac{\pi}{2}-0-0+0\right]=\pi a{/tex} {tex}\therefore \int_{-2}^{2} \sqrt{\frac{a-x}{a+x}} d x=\pi a{/tex}
solution: To solve this we use substitution let {tex}x=a \cos 2 \theta{/tex} Differentiating w.r.t. x, we get {tex}d x=-2 a \sin 2 \theta{/tex} Now, {tex}x=-a \Rightarrow \theta=\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} {tex}x=a \Rightarrow \theta=0{/tex} {tex}\therefore \int_{-a}^{a} \sqrt{\frac{a-x}{a+x}} d x=\int_{\frac{-}{2}}^{0} \sqrt{\frac{a(1-\cos 2 \theta)}{a(1+\cos 2 \theta)}}(-2 \sin 2 \theta) d \theta{/tex} {tex}=2 a \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin \theta}{\cos \theta} \cdot \sin 2 \theta d \theta{/tex} [ {tex}\because 1-\cos 2 \theta=2 \sin ^{2} \theta{/tex} ., {tex}1+\cos 2 \theta=2 \cos ^{2} \theta{/tex} {tex}-\int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x=\int_{b}^{a} f(x) d x{/tex} ] {tex}=2 a \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin \theta \cdot 2 \sin \theta \cos \theta}{\cos \theta}{/tex} {tex}=4 a \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} s \operatorname{in}^{2} \theta d \theta{/tex} {tex}=2 a \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(1-\cos 2 \theta) d \theta{/tex} {tex}=2 a\left[\theta-\frac{\sin 2 \theta}{2}\right]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex} {tex}=2 a\left[\theta-\frac{\sin 2 \theta}{2}\right]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex} {tex}=2 a\left[\frac{\pi}{2}-0-0+0\right]=\pi a{/tex} {tex}\therefore \int_{-2}^{2} \sqrt{\frac{a-x}{a+x}} d x=\pi a{/tex}
solution_html:
To solve this we use substitution

let {tex}x=a \cos 2 \theta{/tex}
Differentiating w.r.t. x, we get
{tex}d x=-2 a \sin 2 \theta{/tex}
Now, {tex}x=-a \Rightarrow \theta=\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}
{tex}x=a \Rightarrow \theta=0{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \int_{-a}^{a} \sqrt{\frac{a-x}{a+x}} d x=\int_{\frac{-}{2}}^{0} \sqrt{\frac{a(1-\cos 2 \theta)}{a(1+\cos 2 \theta)}}(-2 \sin 2 \theta) d \theta{/tex}
{tex}=2 a \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin \theta}{\cos \theta} \cdot \sin 2 \theta d \theta{/tex} [{tex}\because 1-\cos 2 \theta=2 \sin ^{2} \theta{/tex}., {tex}1+\cos 2 \theta=2 \cos ^{2} \theta{/tex} {tex}-\int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x=\int_{b}^{a} f(x) d x{/tex}]
{tex}=2 a \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\sin \theta \cdot 2 \sin \theta \cos \theta}{\cos \theta}{/tex}
{tex}=4 a \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} s \operatorname{in}^{2} \theta d \theta{/tex}
{tex}=2 a \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(1-\cos 2 \theta) d \theta{/tex}
{tex}=2 a\left[\theta-\frac{\sin 2 \theta}{2}\right]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=2 a\left[\theta-\frac{\sin 2 \theta}{2}\right]_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=2 a\left[\frac{\pi}{2}-0-0+0\right]=\pi a{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \int_{-2}^{2} \sqrt{\frac{a-x}{a+x}} d x=\pi a{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Prove that: {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x \tan x}{(\sec x \csc x)} d x=\frac{\pi^{2}}{4}{/tex}
question_text: Prove that: {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x \tan x}{(\sec x \csc x)} d x=\frac{\pi^{2}}{4}{/tex}
explanation: Let the given integral be, {tex}y=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x \tan x}{\sec x \csc x} d x{/tex} .... (i) Using theorem of definite integral we have, {tex}\int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x=\int_{a}^{b} f(a+b-x) d x{/tex} {tex}y=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{(\pi-x) \tan (\pi-x)}{\sec (\pi-x) \csc (\pi-x)} d x{/tex} {tex}y=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{-(\pi-x) \tan x}{-\sec x \csc x} d x{/tex} {tex}y=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\pi \tan x-x \tan x}{\sec x \csc x} d x{/tex} ..... (ii) Adding eq. (i) and eq.(ii) {tex}2 y=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x \tan x}{\sec x \csc x} d x{/tex} {tex}+\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\pi \tan x-x \tan x}{\sec x \csc x} d x{/tex} {tex}2 y=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\pi \tan x}{\sec x \csc x} d x{/tex} {tex}y=\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\frac{\sin x}{\cos x}}{\frac{1}{\cos x} \times \frac{1}{\sin x}} d x{/tex} {tex}y=\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{1-\cos 2 x}{2} d x{/tex} {tex}y=\frac{\pi}{2}\left(\frac{x}{2}-\frac{\sin 2 x}{4}\right)_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} {tex}y=\frac{\pi}{2}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\frac{\sin 2 \pi}{4}\right)=\frac{\pi^{2}}{4}{/tex} Hence proved.
solution: Let the given integral be, {tex}y=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x \tan x}{\sec x \csc x} d x{/tex} .... (i) Using theorem of definite integral we have, {tex}\int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x=\int_{a}^{b} f(a+b-x) d x{/tex} {tex}y=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{(\pi-x) \tan (\pi-x)}{\sec (\pi-x) \csc (\pi-x)} d x{/tex} {tex}y=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{-(\pi-x) \tan x}{-\sec x \csc x} d x{/tex} {tex}y=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\pi \tan x-x \tan x}{\sec x \csc x} d x{/tex} ..... (ii) Adding eq. (i) and eq.(ii) {tex}2 y=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x \tan x}{\sec x \csc x} d x{/tex} {tex}+\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\pi \tan x-x \tan x}{\sec x \csc x} d x{/tex} {tex}2 y=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\pi \tan x}{\sec x \csc x} d x{/tex} {tex}y=\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\frac{\sin x}{\cos x}}{\frac{1}{\cos x} \times \frac{1}{\sin x}} d x{/tex} {tex}y=\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{1-\cos 2 x}{2} d x{/tex} {tex}y=\frac{\pi}{2}\left(\frac{x}{2}-\frac{\sin 2 x}{4}\right)_{0}^{\pi}{/tex} {tex}y=\frac{\pi}{2}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\frac{\sin 2 \pi}{4}\right)=\frac{\pi^{2}}{4}{/tex} Hence proved.
solution_html:
Let the given integral be, 

{tex}y=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x \tan x}{\sec x \csc x} d x{/tex} .... (i)
Using theorem of definite integral we have, 
{tex}\int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x=\int_{a}^{b} f(a+b-x) d x{/tex}
{tex}y=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{(\pi-x) \tan (\pi-x)}{\sec (\pi-x) \csc (\pi-x)} d x{/tex}
{tex}y=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{-(\pi-x) \tan x}{-\sec x \csc x} d x{/tex}
{tex}y=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\pi \tan x-x \tan x}{\sec x \csc x} d x{/tex} ..... (ii)
Adding eq. (i) and eq.(ii)
{tex}2 y=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x \tan x}{\sec x \csc x} d x{/tex}{tex}+\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\pi \tan x-x \tan x}{\sec x \csc x} d x{/tex}
{tex}2 y=\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\pi \tan x}{\sec x \csc x} d x{/tex}
{tex}y=\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\frac{\sin x}{\cos x}}{\frac{1}{\cos x} \times \frac{1}{\sin x}} d x{/tex}
{tex}y=\frac{\pi}{2} \int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{1-\cos 2 x}{2} d x{/tex}
{tex}y=\frac{\pi}{2}\left(\frac{x}{2}-\frac{\sin 2 x}{4}\right)_{0}^{\pi}{/tex}
{tex}y=\frac{\pi}{2}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\frac{\sin 2 \pi}{4}\right)=\frac{\pi^{2}}{4}{/tex}

Hence proved.

explanation: Let I = {tex}\int \frac { ( 1 + \log x ) ^ { 2 } } { x } d x{/tex} Put 1 +log x = t {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { 1 } { x } d x = d t{/tex} {tex}\therefore I = \int \frac { ( 1 + \log x ) ^ { 2 } } { x } d x = \int t ^ { 2 } d t = \frac { t ^ { 3 } } { 3 } + C{/tex} {tex}= \frac { ( 1 + \log x ) ^ { 3 } } { 3 } + C{/tex}
solution: Let I = {tex}\int \frac { ( 1 + \log x ) ^ { 2 } } { x } d x{/tex} Put 1 +log x = t {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { 1 } { x } d x = d t{/tex} {tex}\therefore I = \int \frac { ( 1 + \log x ) ^ { 2 } } { x } d x = \int t ^ { 2 } d t = \frac { t ^ { 3 } } { 3 } + C{/tex} {tex}= \frac { ( 1 + \log x ) ^ { 3 } } { 3 } + C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int \frac { ( 1 + \log x ) ^ { 2 } } { x } d x{/tex}
Put 1 +log x = t

explanation: According to the question, {tex}I = \int \frac { \cos \sqrt { x } } { \sqrt { x } } d x{/tex} Let, {tex}\sqrt x = t {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { 1 } { 2 \sqrt { x } } d x = d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { x } } d x = 2 d t{/tex} {tex} I = \int \frac { \cos \sqrt { x } } { \sqrt { x } } d x {/tex} {tex}= 2 \int \cos t d t{/tex} {tex}= 2 \sin t + C {/tex} {tex}= 2 \sin \sqrt { x } + C {/tex}
solution: According to the question, {tex}I = \int \frac { \cos \sqrt { x } } { \sqrt { x } } d x{/tex} Let, {tex}\sqrt x = t {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { 1 } { 2 \sqrt { x } } d x = d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { x } } d x = 2 d t{/tex} {tex} I = \int \frac { \cos \sqrt { x } } { \sqrt { x } } d x {/tex} {tex}= 2 \int \cos t d t{/tex} {tex}= 2 \sin t + C {/tex} {tex}= 2 \sin \sqrt { x } + C {/tex}
solution_html:
According to the question, {tex}I = \int \frac { \cos \sqrt { x } } { \sqrt { x } } d x{/tex}
Let,  {tex}\sqrt x = t {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac { 1 } { 2 \sqrt { x } } d x = d t{/tex}

{tex} I = \int \frac { \cos \sqrt { x } } { \sqrt { x } } d x {/tex}
{tex}= 2 \int \cos t d t{/tex}
{tex}= 2 \sin t + C {/tex}

explanation: Given, {tex} I = \int \frac { y ^ { 2 } + 4 } { y ^ { 4 } + 16 } d y{/tex} Dividing numerator and denominator by {tex}y^2{/tex} , {tex}\Rightarrow I = \int \frac { \left( 1 + \frac { 4 } { y ^ { 2 } } \right) } { \left( y ^ { 2 } + \frac { 16 } { y ^ { 2 } } \right) } d y {/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { \left( 1 + \frac { 4 } { y ^ { 2 } } \right) } { y ^ { 2 } + \left( \frac { 4 } { y } \right) ^ { 2 } - 8 + 8 } d y{/tex} {tex} = \int \frac { \left( 1 + \frac { 4 } { y ^ { 2 } } \right) } { \left( y - \frac { 4 } { y } \right) ^ { 2 } + 8 } d y{/tex} Let, {tex} y - \frac { 4 } { y } = a{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad \left( 1 + \frac { 4 } { y ^ { 2 } } \right) d y = d a{/tex} {tex} \therefore \quad I = \int \frac { d a } { a ^ { 2 } + 8 } {/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { d a } { a ^ { 2 } + ( 2 \sqrt { 2 } ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 \sqrt { 2 } } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { a } { 2 \sqrt { 2 } } \right) + C{/tex} {tex} \left[ \because \int \frac { d y } { b ^ { 2 } + y ^ { 2 } } = \frac { 1 } { b } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { y } { b } \right) + c \right]{/tex} {tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 \sqrt { 2 } } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { y - \frac { 4 } { y } } { 2 \sqrt { 2 } } \right) + C \left[ \text { put } a = y - \frac { 4 } { y } \right]{/tex} {tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 \sqrt { 2 } } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { y ^ { 2 } - 4 } { 2 \sqrt { 2 } y } \right) + C{/tex}
solution: Given, {tex} I = \int \frac { y ^ { 2 } + 4 } { y ^ { 4 } + 16 } d y{/tex} Dividing numerator and denominator by {tex}y^2{/tex} , {tex}\Rightarrow I = \int \frac { \left( 1 + \frac { 4 } { y ^ { 2 } } \right) } { \left( y ^ { 2 } + \frac { 16 } { y ^ { 2 } } \right) } d y {/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { \left( 1 + \frac { 4 } { y ^ { 2 } } \right) } { y ^ { 2 } + \left( \frac { 4 } { y } \right) ^ { 2 } - 8 + 8 } d y{/tex} {tex} = \int \frac { \left( 1 + \frac { 4 } { y ^ { 2 } } \right) } { \left( y - \frac { 4 } { y } \right) ^ { 2 } + 8 } d y{/tex} Let, {tex} y - \frac { 4 } { y } = a{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad \left( 1 + \frac { 4 } { y ^ { 2 } } \right) d y = d a{/tex} {tex} \therefore \quad I = \int \frac { d a } { a ^ { 2 } + 8 } {/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { d a } { a ^ { 2 } + ( 2 \sqrt { 2 } ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 \sqrt { 2 } } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { a } { 2 \sqrt { 2 } } \right) + C{/tex} {tex} \left[ \because \int \frac { d y } { b ^ { 2 } + y ^ { 2 } } = \frac { 1 } { b } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { y } { b } \right) + c \right]{/tex} {tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 \sqrt { 2 } } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { y - \frac { 4 } { y } } { 2 \sqrt { 2 } } \right) + C \left[ \text { put } a = y - \frac { 4 } { y } \right]{/tex} {tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 \sqrt { 2 } } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { y ^ { 2 } - 4 } { 2 \sqrt { 2 } y } \right) + C{/tex}
solution_html:
Given, {tex} I = \int \frac { y ^ { 2 } + 4 } { y ^ { 4 } + 16 } d y{/tex}
Dividing numerator and denominator by {tex}y^2{/tex}, 
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \int \frac { \left( 1 + \frac { 4 } { y ^ { 2 } } \right) } { \left( y ^ { 2 } + \frac { 16 } { y ^ { 2 } } \right) } d y {/tex}

{tex}= \int \frac { \left( 1 + \frac { 4 } { y ^ { 2 } } \right) } { y ^ { 2 } + \left( \frac { 4 } { y } \right) ^ { 2 } - 8 + 8 } d y{/tex}
{tex} = \int \frac { \left( 1 + \frac { 4 } { y ^ { 2 } } \right) } { \left( y - \frac { 4 } { y } \right) ^ { 2 } + 8 } d y{/tex}
Let, {tex} y - \frac { 4 } { y } = a{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad \left( 1 + \frac { 4 } { y ^ { 2 } } \right) d y = d a{/tex}
{tex} \therefore \quad I = \int \frac { d a } { a ^ { 2 } + 8 } {/tex}{tex}= \int \frac { d a } { a ^ { 2 } + ( 2 \sqrt { 2 } ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex}
{tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 \sqrt { 2 } } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { a } { 2 \sqrt { 2 } } \right) + C{/tex}
{tex} \left[ \because \int \frac { d y } { b ^ { 2 } + y ^ { 2 } } = \frac { 1 } { b } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { y } { b } \right) + c \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 \sqrt { 2 } } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { y - \frac { 4 } { y } } { 2 \sqrt { 2 } } \right) + C \left[ \text { put } a = y - \frac { 4 } { y } \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 \sqrt { 2 } } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { y ^ { 2 } - 4 } { 2 \sqrt { 2 } y } \right) + C{/tex}

explanation: Given, {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { x ^ { 4 } + 1 } { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } d x \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { \left( x ^ { 4 } - 1 \right) + 2 } { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } d x{/tex} {tex}= \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { \left( x ^ { 2 } - 1 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 1 \right) + 2 } { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } d x{/tex} [ {tex}\because (a^2 - b^2) = (a - b) (a + b){/tex} {tex}= \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \left[ \frac { \left( x ^ { 2 } - 1 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 1 \right) } { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } + \frac { 2 } { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } \right] d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \left[ x ^ { 2 } - 1 + \frac { 2 } { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } \right] d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \left[ \frac { x ^ { 3 } } { 3 } - x + 2 \tan ^ { - 1 } x \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 1 }{/tex} {tex}\therefore I = \frac { 1 } { 3 } - 1 + 2 \tan ^ { - 1 } 1 - 0 = - \frac { 2 } { 3 } + 2 \times \frac { \pi } { 4 } = \frac { 3 \pi - 4 } { 6 }{/tex}
solution: Given, {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { x ^ { 4 } + 1 } { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } d x \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { \left( x ^ { 4 } - 1 \right) + 2 } { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } d x{/tex} {tex}= \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { \left( x ^ { 2 } - 1 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 1 \right) + 2 } { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } d x{/tex} [ {tex}\because (a^2 - b^2) = (a - b) (a + b){/tex} {tex}= \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \left[ \frac { \left( x ^ { 2 } - 1 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 1 \right) } { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } + \frac { 2 } { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } \right] d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \left[ x ^ { 2 } - 1 + \frac { 2 } { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } \right] d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \left[ \frac { x ^ { 3 } } { 3 } - x + 2 \tan ^ { - 1 } x \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 1 }{/tex} {tex}\therefore I = \frac { 1 } { 3 } - 1 + 2 \tan ^ { - 1 } 1 - 0 = - \frac { 2 } { 3 } + 2 \times \frac { \pi } { 4 } = \frac { 3 \pi - 4 } { 6 }{/tex}
solution_html:
Given, {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { x ^ { 4 } + 1 } { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } d x \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { \left( x ^ { 4 } - 1 \right) + 2 } { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { \left( x ^ { 2 } - 1 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 1 \right) + 2 } { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } d x{/tex}
[{tex}\because  (a^2 - b^2) = (a - b) (a + b){/tex} 
{tex}= \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \left[ \frac { \left( x ^ { 2 } - 1 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 1 \right) } { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } + \frac { 2 } { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } \right] d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \left[ x ^ { 2 } - 1 + \frac { 2 } { x ^ { 2 } + 1 } \right] d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \left[ \frac { x ^ { 3 } } { 3 } - x + 2 \tan ^ { - 1 } x \right] _ { 0 } ^ { 1 }{/tex}
{tex}\therefore I = \frac { 1 } { 3 } - 1 + 2 \tan ^ { - 1 } 1 - 0 = - \frac { 2 } { 3 } + 2 \times \frac { \pi } { 4 } = \frac { 3 \pi - 4 } { 6 }{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Evaluate {tex}\int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \frac { x + \sin x } { 1 + \cos x } d x.{/tex}
question_text: Evaluate {tex}\int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \frac { x + \sin x } { 1 + \cos x } d x.{/tex}
explanation: Given I = {tex}\int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \frac { x + \sin x } { 1 + \cos x } d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \frac { x + 2 \sin \frac { x } { 2 } \cos \frac { x } { 2 } } { 2 \cos ^ { 2 } \frac { x } { 2 } } d x{/tex} {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c } { \because \sin x = 2 \sin \frac { x } { 2 } \cos \frac { x } { 2 } } \\ { \text { and } 1 + \cos x = 2 \cos ^ { 2 } \frac { x } { 2 } } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow I = \frac{1}{2}\int_0^{\pi /2} {\mathop x\limits_I } \sec \mathop {^2}\limits_{II} \frac{x}{2}dx + \int_0^{\pi /2} {\tan } \frac{x}{2}dx{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left\{ \left[ x \int \sec ^ { 2 } \frac { x } { 2 } d x \right] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 }\right.- \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \left[ \frac { d } { d x } ( x ) \int \left( \sec ^ { 2 } \frac { x } { 2 } d x \right) \right] d x \}+ \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \tan \frac { x } { 2 } d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left\{ \left[ x \cdot \frac { \tan \frac { x } { 2 } } { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } - \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \frac { \tan \frac { x } { 2 } } { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } d x \right\}{/tex} {tex}+ \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \tan \frac { x } { 2 } d x{/tex} [ Integration by parts] {tex}= \left[ x \cdot \tan \frac { x } { 2 } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } - \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \tan \frac { x } { 2 } d x + \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \tan \frac { x } { 2 } d x {/tex} {tex}= \frac { \pi } { 2 } \cdot \tan \frac { \pi } { 4 } - 0{/tex} {tex}\therefore I = \frac { \pi } { 2 }{/tex} {tex}\left[ \because \tan \frac { \pi } { 4 } = 1 \right]{/tex}
solution: Given I = {tex}\int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \frac { x + \sin x } { 1 + \cos x } d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \frac { x + 2 \sin \frac { x } { 2 } \cos \frac { x } { 2 } } { 2 \cos ^ { 2 } \frac { x } { 2 } } d x{/tex} {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c } { \because \sin x = 2 \sin \frac { x } { 2 } \cos \frac { x } { 2 } } \\ { \text { and } 1 + \cos x = 2 \cos ^ { 2 } \frac { x } { 2 } } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow I = \frac{1}{2}\int_0^{\pi /2} {\mathop x\limits_I } \sec \mathop {^2}\limits_{II} \frac{x}{2}dx + \int_0^{\pi /2} {\tan } \frac{x}{2}dx{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left\{ \left[ x \int \sec ^ { 2 } \frac { x } { 2 } d x \right] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 }\right.- \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \left[ \frac { d } { d x } ( x ) \int \left( \sec ^ { 2 } \frac { x } { 2 } d x \right) \right] d x \}+ \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \tan \frac { x } { 2 } d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left\{ \left[ x \cdot \frac { \tan \frac { x } { 2 } } { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } - \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \frac { \tan \frac { x } { 2 } } { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } d x \right\}{/tex} {tex}+ \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \tan \frac { x } { 2 } d x{/tex} [ Integration by parts] {tex}= \left[ x \cdot \tan \frac { x } { 2 } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } - \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \tan \frac { x } { 2 } d x + \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \tan \frac { x } { 2 } d x {/tex} {tex}= \frac { \pi } { 2 } \cdot \tan \frac { \pi } { 4 } - 0{/tex} {tex}\therefore I = \frac { \pi } { 2 }{/tex} {tex}\left[ \because \tan \frac { \pi } { 4 } = 1 \right]{/tex}
solution_html:
Given I = {tex}\int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \frac { x + \sin x } { 1 + \cos x } d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \frac { x + 2 \sin \frac { x } { 2 } \cos \frac { x } { 2 } } { 2 \cos ^ { 2 } \frac { x } { 2 } } d x{/tex}
{tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c } { \because \sin x = 2 \sin \frac { x } { 2 } \cos \frac { x } { 2 } } \\ { \text { and } 1 + \cos x = 2 \cos ^ { 2 } \frac { x } { 2 } } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow I = \frac{1}{2}\int_0^{\pi /2} {\mathop x\limits_I } \sec \mathop {^2}\limits_{II} \frac{x}{2}dx + \int_0^{\pi /2} {\tan } \frac{x}{2}dx{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left\{ \left[ x \int \sec ^ { 2 } \frac { x } { 2 } d x \right] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 }\right.- \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \left[ \frac { d } { d x } ( x ) \int \left( \sec ^ { 2 } \frac { x } { 2 } d x \right) \right] d x \}+ \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \tan \frac { x } { 2 } d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \left\{ \left[ x \cdot \frac { \tan \frac { x } { 2 } } { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } - \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \frac { \tan \frac { x } { 2 } } { \frac { 1 } { 2 } } d x \right\}{/tex}
{tex}+ \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \tan \frac { x } { 2 } d x{/tex}
[ Integration by parts]
{tex}= \left[ x \cdot \tan \frac { x } { 2 } \right] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } - \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \tan \frac { x } { 2 } d x + \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 2 } \tan \frac { x } { 2 } d x {/tex}
{tex}= \frac { \pi } { 2 } \cdot \tan \frac { \pi } { 4 } - 0{/tex}
{tex}\therefore I = \frac { \pi } { 2 }{/tex} {tex}\left[ \because \tan \frac { \pi } { 4 } = 1 \right]{/tex}

question_html: Evaluate the definite integral {tex} \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \frac { 5 x ^ { 2 } } { x ^ { 2 } + 4 x + 3 }{/tex}
question_text: Evaluate the definite integral {tex} \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \frac { 5 x ^ { 2 } } { x ^ { 2 } + 4 x + 3 }{/tex}
explanation: According to the question, {tex} I = \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \frac { 5 x ^ { 2 } } { x ^ { 2 } + 4 x + 3 } d x{/tex} {tex} I = 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \left( 1 + \frac { - 4 x - 3 } { x ^ { 2 } + 4 x + 3 } \right) d x{/tex} {tex} = 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } d x - 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \frac { 4 x + 3 } { x ^ { 2 } + 4 x + 3 } d x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = 5 [ x ] _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } - 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \frac { 4 x + 3 } { ( x + 3 ) ( x + 1 ) } d x{/tex} Using partial fraction, let {tex} \frac { 4 x + 3 } { ( x + 3 ) ( x + 1 ) } = \frac { A } { x + 3 } + \frac { B } { x + 1 }{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad \frac { 4 x + 3 } { ( x + 3 ) ( x + 1 ) } = \frac { A ( x + 1 ) + B ( x + 3 ) } { ( x + 3 ) ( x + 1 ) }{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}4x + 3 = A(x + 1) + B(x + 3){/tex} Comparing the coefficients of like from both sides, {tex}\Rightarrow A + B = 4 \Rightarrow A = 4 - B{/tex} and A + 3B = 3 {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}4 - B + 3B = 3{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad B = - \frac { 1 } { 2 },{/tex} then {tex} A = 4 + \frac { 1 } { 2 } = \frac { 9 } { 2 }{/tex} Now, from Equation (i), we get {tex} I = 5 ( 2 - 1 ) - 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \left( \frac { 9 / 2 } { x + 3 } + \frac { - 1 / 2 } { x + 1 } \right) d x{/tex} {tex} = 5 - 5 \left[ \frac { 9 } { 2 } \log | x + 3 | - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log | x + 1 | \right] _ { 1 } ^ { 2 }{/tex} {tex} = 5 - 5\left. {\left[ {\left( {\frac{9}{2}\log 5 - \frac{1}{2}\log 3} \right)} \right. - \left( {\frac{9}{2}\log 4 - \frac{1}{2}\log 2} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex} = 5 - 5 \left[ \frac { 9 } { 2 } ( \log 5 - \log 4 ) - \frac { 1 } { 2 } ( \log 3 - \log 4 ]\right.{/tex} {tex} = 5 - 5 \left[ \frac { 9 } { 2 } \log \frac { 5 } { 4 } - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \frac { 3 } { 2 } \right]{/tex} {tex} = 5 - \frac { 45 } { 2 } \log \frac { 5 } { 4 } + \frac { 5 } { 2 } \log \frac { 3 } { 2 }{/tex}
solution: According to the question, {tex} I = \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \frac { 5 x ^ { 2 } } { x ^ { 2 } + 4 x + 3 } d x{/tex} {tex} I = 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \left( 1 + \frac { - 4 x - 3 } { x ^ { 2 } + 4 x + 3 } \right) d x{/tex} {tex} = 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } d x - 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \frac { 4 x + 3 } { x ^ { 2 } + 4 x + 3 } d x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = 5 [ x ] _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } - 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \frac { 4 x + 3 } { ( x + 3 ) ( x + 1 ) } d x{/tex} Using partial fraction, let {tex} \frac { 4 x + 3 } { ( x + 3 ) ( x + 1 ) } = \frac { A } { x + 3 } + \frac { B } { x + 1 }{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad \frac { 4 x + 3 } { ( x + 3 ) ( x + 1 ) } = \frac { A ( x + 1 ) + B ( x + 3 ) } { ( x + 3 ) ( x + 1 ) }{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}4x + 3 = A(x + 1) + B(x + 3){/tex} Comparing the coefficients of like from both sides, {tex}\Rightarrow A + B = 4 \Rightarrow A = 4 - B{/tex} and A + 3B = 3 {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}4 - B + 3B = 3{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad B = - \frac { 1 } { 2 },{/tex} then {tex} A = 4 + \frac { 1 } { 2 } = \frac { 9 } { 2 }{/tex} Now, from Equation (i), we get {tex} I = 5 ( 2 - 1 ) - 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \left( \frac { 9 / 2 } { x + 3 } + \frac { - 1 / 2 } { x + 1 } \right) d x{/tex} {tex} = 5 - 5 \left[ \frac { 9 } { 2 } \log | x + 3 | - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log | x + 1 | \right] _ { 1 } ^ { 2 }{/tex} {tex} = 5 - 5\left. {\left[ {\left( {\frac{9}{2}\log 5 - \frac{1}{2}\log 3} \right)} \right. - \left( {\frac{9}{2}\log 4 - \frac{1}{2}\log 2} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex} = 5 - 5 \left[ \frac { 9 } { 2 } ( \log 5 - \log 4 ) - \frac { 1 } { 2 } ( \log 3 - \log 4 ]\right.{/tex} {tex} = 5 - 5 \left[ \frac { 9 } { 2 } \log \frac { 5 } { 4 } - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \frac { 3 } { 2 } \right]{/tex} {tex} = 5 - \frac { 45 } { 2 } \log \frac { 5 } { 4 } + \frac { 5 } { 2 } \log \frac { 3 } { 2 }{/tex}
solution_html:
According to the question, {tex} I = \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \frac { 5 x ^ { 2 } } { x ^ { 2 } + 4 x + 3 } d x{/tex}
{tex} I = 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \left( 1 + \frac { - 4 x - 3 } { x ^ { 2 } + 4 x + 3 } \right) d x{/tex}
{tex} = 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } d x - 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \frac { 4 x + 3 } { x ^ { 2 } + 4 x + 3 } d x{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = 5 [ x ] _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } - 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \frac { 4 x + 3 } { ( x + 3 ) ( x + 1 ) } d x{/tex}
Using partial fraction,
let {tex} \frac { 4 x + 3 } { ( x + 3 ) ( x + 1 ) } = \frac { A } { x + 3 } + \frac { B } { x + 1 }{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad \frac { 4 x + 3 } { ( x + 3 ) ( x + 1 ) } = \frac { A ( x + 1 ) + B ( x + 3 ) } { ( x + 3 ) ( x + 1 ) }{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}4x + 3 = A(x + 1) + B(x + 3){/tex}
Comparing the coefficients of like from both sides,
{tex}\Rightarrow A + B = 4 \Rightarrow A = 4 - B{/tex}
and A + 3B  = 3{tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}4 - B + 3B = 3{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad B = - \frac { 1 } { 2 },{/tex}then {tex} A = 4 + \frac { 1 } { 2 } = \frac { 9 } { 2 }{/tex}
Now, from Equation (i), we get
{tex} I = 5 ( 2 - 1 ) - 5 \int _ { 1 } ^ { 2 } \left( \frac { 9 / 2 } { x + 3 } + \frac { - 1 / 2 } { x + 1 } \right) d x{/tex}
{tex} = 5 - 5 \left[ \frac { 9 } { 2 } \log | x + 3 | - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log | x + 1 | \right] _ { 1 } ^ { 2 }{/tex}
{tex} = 5 - 5\left. {\left[ {\left( {\frac{9}{2}\log 5 - \frac{1}{2}\log 3} \right)} \right. - \left( {\frac{9}{2}\log 4 - \frac{1}{2}\log 2} \right)} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = 5 - 5 \left[ \frac { 9 } { 2 } ( \log 5 - \log 4 ) - \frac { 1 } { 2 } ( \log 3 - \log 4 ]\right.{/tex}
{tex} = 5 - 5 \left[ \frac { 9 } { 2 } \log \frac { 5 } { 4 } - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \frac { 3 } { 2 } \right]{/tex}
{tex} = 5 - \frac { 45 } { 2 } \log \frac { 5 } { 4 } + \frac { 5 } { 2 } \log \frac { 3 } { 2 }{/tex}

question_html: Write the value of {tex} \int \frac { 2 - 3 \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x.{/tex}
question_text: Write the value of {tex} \int \frac { 2 - 3 \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x.{/tex}
explanation: Let {tex}I = \int \frac { 2 - 3 \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x = \int \left( \frac { 2 } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } - \frac { 3 \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } \right) d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \left( 2 \sec ^ { 2 } x - 3 \sec x \tan x \right) d x{/tex} {tex} = 2 \int \sec ^ { 2 } x d x - 3 \int \sec x \tan x d x{/tex} = 2 tan x - 3sec x + C.
solution: Let {tex}I = \int \frac { 2 - 3 \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x = \int \left( \frac { 2 } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } - \frac { 3 \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } \right) d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \left( 2 \sec ^ { 2 } x - 3 \sec x \tan x \right) d x{/tex} {tex} = 2 \int \sec ^ { 2 } x d x - 3 \int \sec x \tan x d x{/tex} = 2 tan x - 3sec x + C.
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int \frac { 2 - 3 \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x = \int \left( \frac { 2 } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } - \frac { 3 \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } \right) d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \left( 2 \sec ^ { 2 } x - 3 \sec x \tan x \right) d x{/tex}
{tex} = 2 \int \sec ^ { 2 } x d x - 3 \int \sec x \tan x d x{/tex}
= 2 tan x - 3sec x + C.

explanation: Let I = {tex}\int \sec x ( \sec x + \tan x ) d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \left( \sec ^ { 2 } x + \sec x \tan x \right) d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \sec ^ { 2 } x d x + \int \sec x \tan x d x{/tex} = tan x + secx + C
solution: Let I = {tex}\int \sec x ( \sec x + \tan x ) d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \left( \sec ^ { 2 } x + \sec x \tan x \right) d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \sec ^ { 2 } x d x + \int \sec x \tan x d x{/tex} = tan x + secx + C

explanation: Let {tex}I=\int \frac { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } { x } d x{/tex} Put {tex}log \ x = t \Rightarrow \frac { 1 } { x } d x = d t{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad I = \int \frac { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } { x } d x{/tex} {tex} = \int t ^ { 2 } d t{/tex} {tex}= \frac { t ^ { 3 } } { 3 } + C {/tex} {tex}= \frac { ( \log x ) ^ { 3 } } { 3 } + C {/tex} {tex}\therefore I= \frac { ( \log x ) ^ { 3 } } { 3 } + C{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}I=\int \frac { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } { x } d x{/tex} Put {tex}log \ x = t \Rightarrow \frac { 1 } { x } d x = d t{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad I = \int \frac { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } { x } d x{/tex} {tex} = \int t ^ { 2 } d t{/tex} {tex}= \frac { t ^ { 3 } } { 3 } + C {/tex} {tex}= \frac { ( \log x ) ^ { 3 } } { 3 } + C {/tex} {tex}\therefore I= \frac { ( \log x ) ^ { 3 } } { 3 } + C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let  {tex}I=\int \frac { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } { x } d x{/tex}
Put {tex}log \ x = t \Rightarrow \frac { 1 } { x } d x = d t{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad I = \int \frac { ( \log x ) ^ { 2 } } { x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \int t ^ { 2 } d t{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { t ^ { 3 } } { 3 } + C {/tex}
{tex}= \frac { ( \log x ) ^ { 3 } } { 3 } + C {/tex}
{tex}\therefore I= \frac { ( \log x ) ^ { 3 } } { 3 } + C{/tex}

question_html: Evaluate {tex}\int \frac { e ^ { \tan ^ { - 1 } x } } { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } d x.{/tex}
question_text: Evaluate {tex}\int \frac { e ^ { \tan ^ { - 1 } x } } { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } d x.{/tex}
explanation: Let {tex}I = \int \frac { e ^ { \tan ^ { - 1 } x } } { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } d x{/tex} Put tan -1 x = t {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { 1 } { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } d x = d t{/tex} {tex}\therefore I = \int \frac { e ^ { \tan ^ { - 1 } x } } { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } d x = \int e ^ { t } d t{/tex} = e t + C {tex}\left[ \because \int e ^ { x } d x = e ^ { x } + C \right]{/tex} {tex}= e ^ { \tan ^ { - 1 } x } + C{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}I = \int \frac { e ^ { \tan ^ { - 1 } x } } { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } d x{/tex} Put tan -1 x = t {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { 1 } { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } d x = d t{/tex} {tex}\therefore I = \int \frac { e ^ { \tan ^ { - 1 } x } } { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } d x = \int e ^ { t } d t{/tex} = e t + C {tex}\left[ \because \int e ^ { x } d x = e ^ { x } + C \right]{/tex} {tex}= e ^ { \tan ^ { - 1 } x } + C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int \frac { e ^ { \tan ^ { - 1 } x } } { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } d x{/tex}
Put tan-1 x = t {tex}\Rightarrow \frac { 1 } { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } d x = d t{/tex}
{tex}\therefore I = \int \frac { e ^ { \tan ^ { - 1 } x } } { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } d x = \int e ^ { t } d t{/tex}
= et + C  {tex}\left[ \because \int e ^ { x } d x = e ^ { x } + C \right]{/tex}
{tex}= e ^ { \tan ^ { - 1 } x } + C{/tex}

question_html: Integrated the function: {tex}\int \frac { e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } } { e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } } d x.{/tex}
question_text: Integrated the function: {tex}\int \frac { e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } } { e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } } d x.{/tex}
explanation: Let {tex}I = \int \frac { e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } } { e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } } d x{/tex} Put {tex}e^{2x} + e^{-2x} = t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left( 2 e ^ { 2 x } - 2 e ^ { - 2 x } \right) d x = d t{/tex} {tex}\left[ \because \frac { d } { d x } \left( e ^ { a x } \right) = a e ^ { a x } \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2\left( e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } \right) d x = { d t } {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left( e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } \right) d x = \frac { d t } { 2 }{/tex} {tex}\therefore I = \int \frac { e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } } { e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } } d x{/tex} {tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { d t } { t } {/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log | t | + C{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \left| e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } \right| + C \quad \text { [ put } t = e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } ]{/tex} {tex}\therefore I =\frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \left| e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } \right| + C{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}I = \int \frac { e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } } { e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } } d x{/tex} Put {tex}e^{2x} + e^{-2x} = t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left( 2 e ^ { 2 x } - 2 e ^ { - 2 x } \right) d x = d t{/tex} {tex}\left[ \because \frac { d } { d x } \left( e ^ { a x } \right) = a e ^ { a x } \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2\left( e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } \right) d x = { d t } {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left( e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } \right) d x = \frac { d t } { 2 }{/tex} {tex}\therefore I = \int \frac { e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } } { e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } } d x{/tex} {tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { d t } { t } {/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log | t | + C{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \left| e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } \right| + C \quad \text { [ put } t = e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } ]{/tex} {tex}\therefore I =\frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \left| e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } \right| + C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int \frac { e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } } { e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } } d x{/tex}
Put {tex}e^{2x} + e^{-2x} = t{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \left( 2 e ^ { 2 x } - 2 e ^ { - 2 x } \right) d x = d t{/tex} {tex}\left[ \because \frac { d } { d x } \left( e ^ { a x } \right) = a e ^ { a x } \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 2\left( e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } \right) d x = { d t } {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \left( e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } \right) d x = \frac { d t } { 2 }{/tex}
{tex}\therefore I = \int \frac { e ^ { 2 x } - e ^ { - 2 x } } { e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { d t } { t } {/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log | t | + C{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \left| e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } \right| + C \quad \text { [ put } t = e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } ]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore I =\frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \left| e ^ { 2 x } + e ^ { - 2 x } \right| + C{/tex}

question_html: Evaluate {tex}\int \frac { x ^ { 3 } - x ^ { 2 } + x - 1 } { x - 1 } d x.{/tex}
question_text: Evaluate {tex}\int \frac { x ^ { 3 } - x ^ { 2 } + x - 1 } { x - 1 } d x.{/tex}
explanation: Let {tex}I = \int \frac { x ^ { 3 } - x ^ { 2 } + x - 1 } { x - 1 } d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { x ^ { 2 } ( x - 1 ) + 1 ( x - 1 ) } { x - 1 } d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { \left( x ^ { 2 } + 1 \right) ( x - 1 ) } { x - 1 } d x{/tex} {tex} = \int \left( x ^ { 2 } + 1 \right) d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac { x ^ { 3 } } { 3 } + x + C{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}I = \int \frac { x ^ { 3 } - x ^ { 2 } + x - 1 } { x - 1 } d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { x ^ { 2 } ( x - 1 ) + 1 ( x - 1 ) } { x - 1 } d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { \left( x ^ { 2 } + 1 \right) ( x - 1 ) } { x - 1 } d x{/tex} {tex} = \int \left( x ^ { 2 } + 1 \right) d x{/tex} {tex}= \frac { x ^ { 3 } } { 3 } + x + C{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I = \int \frac { x ^ { 3 } - x ^ { 2 } + x - 1 } { x - 1 } d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \frac { x ^ { 2 } ( x - 1 ) + 1 ( x - 1 ) } { x - 1 } d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \frac { \left( x ^ { 2 } + 1 \right) ( x - 1 ) } { x - 1 } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \int \left( x ^ { 2 } + 1 \right) d x{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { x ^ { 3 } } { 3 } + x + C{/tex}

question_html: Write the value of {tex}\int \frac { 1 - \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x.{/tex}
question_text: Write the value of {tex}\int \frac { 1 - \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x.{/tex}
explanation: Let I = {tex}\int \frac { 1 - \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x {/tex} {tex}= \int \left( \frac { 1 } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } - \frac { \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } \right) d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \sec ^ { 2 } x d x - \int \sec x \tan x d x{/tex} = tan x - sec x + C
solution: Let I = {tex}\int \frac { 1 - \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x {/tex} {tex}= \int \left( \frac { 1 } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } - \frac { \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } \right) d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \sec ^ { 2 } x d x - \int \sec x \tan x d x{/tex} = tan x - sec x + C
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int \frac { 1 - \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x {/tex}
{tex}= \int \left( \frac { 1 } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } - \frac { \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } \right) d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \sec ^ { 2 } x d x - \int \sec x \tan x d x{/tex}
= tan x - sec x + C

explanation: We have to evaluate {tex}\int _ { 1 } ^ { \sqrt { 3 } } \frac { d x } { 1 + x ^ { 2 } }.{/tex} {tex}\int _ { 1 } ^ { \sqrt { 3 } } \frac { d x } { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } = \left[ \tan ^ { - 1 } x \right] _ { 1 } ^ { \sqrt { 3 } } \left[ \because \int \frac { d x } { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } = \tan ^ { - 1 } x \right]{/tex} {tex}= \tan ^ { - 1 } \sqrt { 3 } - \tan ^ { - 1 } 1{/tex} {tex}= \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \tan \frac { \pi } { 3 } \right) - \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \tan \frac { \pi } { 4 } \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ \because \sqrt { 3 } = \tan \frac { \pi } { 3 } \text { and } 1 = \tan \frac { \pi } { 4 } \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac { \pi } { 3 } - \frac { \pi } { 4 } = \frac { 4 \pi - 3 \pi } { 12 } = \frac { \pi } { 12 }{/tex}
solution: We have to evaluate {tex}\int _ { 1 } ^ { \sqrt { 3 } } \frac { d x } { 1 + x ^ { 2 } }.{/tex} {tex}\int _ { 1 } ^ { \sqrt { 3 } } \frac { d x } { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } = \left[ \tan ^ { - 1 } x \right] _ { 1 } ^ { \sqrt { 3 } } \left[ \because \int \frac { d x } { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } = \tan ^ { - 1 } x \right]{/tex} {tex}= \tan ^ { - 1 } \sqrt { 3 } - \tan ^ { - 1 } 1{/tex} {tex}= \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \tan \frac { \pi } { 3 } \right) - \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \tan \frac { \pi } { 4 } \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ \because \sqrt { 3 } = \tan \frac { \pi } { 3 } \text { and } 1 = \tan \frac { \pi } { 4 } \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac { \pi } { 3 } - \frac { \pi } { 4 } = \frac { 4 \pi - 3 \pi } { 12 } = \frac { \pi } { 12 }{/tex}
solution_html:
We have to evaluate  {tex}\int _ { 1 } ^ { \sqrt { 3 } } \frac { d x } { 1 + x ^ { 2 } }.{/tex}

{tex}\int _ { 1 } ^ { \sqrt { 3 } } \frac { d x } { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } = \left[ \tan ^ { - 1 } x \right] _ { 1 } ^ { \sqrt { 3 } } \left[ \because \int \frac { d x } { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } = \tan ^ { - 1 } x \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \tan ^ { - 1 } \sqrt { 3 } - \tan ^ { - 1 } 1{/tex}
 {tex}= \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \tan \frac { \pi } { 3 } \right) - \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \tan \frac { \pi } { 4 } \right){/tex}

question_html: Evaluate {tex} \int \frac { \sin x - \cos x } { \sqrt { \sin 2 x } } d x.{/tex}
question_text: Evaluate {tex} \int \frac { \sin x - \cos x } { \sqrt { \sin 2 x } } d x.{/tex}
explanation: According to the question , {tex} I =\int \frac { \sin x - \cos x } { \sqrt { \sin 2 x } } d x{/tex} {tex} = \int \frac { \sin x - \cos x } { \sqrt { 1 + \sin 2 x - 1 } } d x{/tex} {tex} = \int \frac { \sin x - \cos x } { \sqrt { \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x + 2 \sin x \cos x - 1 } } d x{/tex} {tex} \left[ \because \sin ^ { 2 } \theta + \cos ^ { 2 } \theta = 1 \right]{/tex} {tex} = \int \frac { \sin x - \cos x } { \sqrt { ( \sin x + \cos x ) ^ { 2 } - 1 } } d x{/tex} Put sin x + cos x = t {tex} \Rightarrow (cos x - sin x) dx = dt{/tex} {tex} \therefore \quad I = \int \frac { - d t } { \sqrt { t ^ { 2 } - 1 } }{/tex} {tex} = - \log \left| t + \sqrt { t ^ { 2 } - 1 } \right| + C{/tex} {tex} \left[ \because \int \frac { d x } { \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } - a ^ { 2 } } } = \log \left| x + \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } - a ^ { 2 } } \right| + c \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow I = -log | (sin x + cos x){/tex} {tex} + \sqrt { ( \sin x + \cos x ) ^ { 2 } - 1 } | + C{/tex} {tex}[\because t = sin x + cos x]{/tex} {tex} = -log|(sin x + cos x)+ \sqrt { \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x + 2 \sin x \cos x - 1 } | + C{/tex} {tex} = - \log | ( \sin x + \cos x ) + \sqrt { \sin 2 x } | + C{/tex} {tex}\therefore I = - \log | ( \sin x + \cos x ) + \sqrt { \sin 2 x } | + C{/tex}
solution: According to the question , {tex} I =\int \frac { \sin x - \cos x } { \sqrt { \sin 2 x } } d x{/tex} {tex} = \int \frac { \sin x - \cos x } { \sqrt { 1 + \sin 2 x - 1 } } d x{/tex} {tex} = \int \frac { \sin x - \cos x } { \sqrt { \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x + 2 \sin x \cos x - 1 } } d x{/tex} {tex} \left[ \because \sin ^ { 2 } \theta + \cos ^ { 2 } \theta = 1 \right]{/tex} {tex} = \int \frac { \sin x - \cos x } { \sqrt { ( \sin x + \cos x ) ^ { 2 } - 1 } } d x{/tex} Put sin x + cos x = t {tex} \Rightarrow (cos x - sin x) dx = dt{/tex} {tex} \therefore \quad I = \int \frac { - d t } { \sqrt { t ^ { 2 } - 1 } }{/tex} {tex} = - \log \left| t + \sqrt { t ^ { 2 } - 1 } \right| + C{/tex} {tex} \left[ \because \int \frac { d x } { \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } - a ^ { 2 } } } = \log \left| x + \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } - a ^ { 2 } } \right| + c \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow I = -log | (sin x + cos x){/tex} {tex} + \sqrt { ( \sin x + \cos x ) ^ { 2 } - 1 } | + C{/tex} {tex}[\because t = sin x + cos x]{/tex} {tex} = -log|(sin x + cos x)+ \sqrt { \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x + 2 \sin x \cos x - 1 } | + C{/tex} {tex} = - \log | ( \sin x + \cos x ) + \sqrt { \sin 2 x } | + C{/tex} {tex}\therefore I = - \log | ( \sin x + \cos x ) + \sqrt { \sin 2 x } | + C{/tex}
solution_html:
According to the question ,  {tex} I =\int \frac { \sin x - \cos x } { \sqrt { \sin 2 x } } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \int \frac { \sin x - \cos x } { \sqrt { 1 + \sin 2 x - 1 } } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \int \frac { \sin x - \cos x } { \sqrt { \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x + 2 \sin x \cos x - 1 } } d x{/tex}{tex} \left[ \because \sin ^ { 2 } \theta + \cos ^ { 2 } \theta = 1 \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \int \frac { \sin x - \cos x } { \sqrt { ( \sin x + \cos x ) ^ { 2 } - 1 } } d x{/tex}
Put sin x + cos x = t
{tex} \Rightarrow (cos x - sin x) dx = dt{/tex} 
{tex} \therefore \quad I = \int \frac { - d t } { \sqrt { t ^ { 2 } - 1 } }{/tex}
{tex} = - \log \left| t + \sqrt { t ^ { 2 } - 1 } \right| + C{/tex}{tex} \left[ \because \int \frac { d x } { \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } - a ^ { 2 } } } = \log \left| x + \sqrt { x ^ { 2 } - a ^ { 2 } } \right| + c \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow I = -log | (sin x + cos x){/tex} {tex} + \sqrt { ( \sin x + \cos x ) ^ { 2 } - 1 } | + C{/tex} {tex}[\because t = sin x + cos x]{/tex}
{tex} = -log|(sin x + cos x)+ \sqrt { \sin ^ { 2 } x + \cos ^ { 2 } x + 2 \sin x \cos x - 1 } | + C{/tex}
{tex} = - \log | ( \sin x + \cos x ) + \sqrt { \sin 2 x } | + C{/tex}
{tex}\therefore I = - \log | ( \sin x + \cos x ) + \sqrt { \sin 2 x } | + C{/tex}

explanation: Given {tex} I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log \left| \frac { 1 } { x } - 1 \right| d x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log \left| \frac { 1 - x } { x } \right| d x{/tex} ...(i) {tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log \left| \frac { 1 - ( 1 - x ) } { 1 - x } \right| d x{/tex} {tex} \left[ \because \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( x ) d x = \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( a - x ) d x \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log \left| \frac { x } { 1 - x } \right| d x{/tex} ...(ii) Adding Equations. (i) and (ii), {tex} 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log \left| \frac { 1 - x } { x } \right| d x + \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log \left| \frac { x } { 1 - x } \right| d x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \left[ \log \left| \frac { 1 - x } { x } \right| + \log \left| \frac { x } { 1 - x } \right| d x \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log \left| \left( \frac { 1 - x } { x } \times \frac { x } { 1 - x } \right) \right| d x{/tex} {tex} [ \because \log m + \log n = \log ( m \times n ) ]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log 1 d x{/tex} [ {tex} \because {/tex} log 1 = 0] {tex} \Rightarrow \quad 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } 0 d x = 0{/tex} {tex} \therefore I = 0{/tex}
solution: Given {tex} I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log \left| \frac { 1 } { x } - 1 \right| d x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log \left| \frac { 1 - x } { x } \right| d x{/tex} ...(i) {tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log \left| \frac { 1 - ( 1 - x ) } { 1 - x } \right| d x{/tex} {tex} \left[ \because \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( x ) d x = \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( a - x ) d x \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log \left| \frac { x } { 1 - x } \right| d x{/tex} ...(ii) Adding Equations. (i) and (ii), {tex} 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log \left| \frac { 1 - x } { x } \right| d x + \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log \left| \frac { x } { 1 - x } \right| d x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \left[ \log \left| \frac { 1 - x } { x } \right| + \log \left| \frac { x } { 1 - x } \right| d x \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log \left| \left( \frac { 1 - x } { x } \times \frac { x } { 1 - x } \right) \right| d x{/tex} {tex} [ \because \log m + \log n = \log ( m \times n ) ]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log 1 d x{/tex} [ {tex} \because {/tex} log 1 = 0] {tex} \Rightarrow \quad 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } 0 d x = 0{/tex} {tex} \therefore I = 0{/tex}
solution_html:
Given {tex} I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log \left| \frac { 1 } { x } - 1 \right| d x{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log \left| \frac { 1 - x } { x } \right| d x{/tex}  ...(i)
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log \left| \frac { 1 - ( 1 - x ) } { 1 - x } \right| d x{/tex}{tex} \left[ \because \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( x ) d x = \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( a - x ) d x \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log \left| \frac { x } { 1 - x } \right| d x{/tex}...(ii)
Adding Equations. (i) and (ii), 
{tex} 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log \left| \frac { 1 - x } { x } \right| d x + \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log \left| \frac { x } { 1 - x } \right| d x{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \left[ \log \left| \frac { 1 - x } { x } \right| + \log \left| \frac { x } { 1 - x } \right| d x \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log \left| \left( \frac { 1 - x } { x } \times \frac { x } { 1 - x } \right) \right| d x{/tex}{tex} [ \because \log m + \log n = \log ( m \times n ) ]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \log 1 d x{/tex} [{tex} \because {/tex}log 1 = 0]
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } 0 d x = 0{/tex}

{tex}  \therefore I = 0{/tex}

question_html: Evaluate {tex}\int ( a x + b ) ^ { 3 } d x.{/tex}
question_text: Evaluate {tex}\int ( a x + b ) ^ { 3 } d x.{/tex}
explanation: Let I = {tex}\int ( a x + b ) ^ { 3 } d x{/tex} Put t = ax + b {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d t } { d x } = a \Rightarrow \frac { d t } { a } = d x{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad I = \int \frac { t ^ { 3 } } { a } d t = \frac { 1 } { a } \cdot \frac { t ^ { 4 } } { 4 } + C{/tex} {tex}= \frac { ( a x + b ) ^ { 4 } } { 4 a } + C{/tex} [put t = ax + b]
solution: Let I = {tex}\int ( a x + b ) ^ { 3 } d x{/tex} Put t = ax + b {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d t } { d x } = a \Rightarrow \frac { d t } { a } = d x{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad I = \int \frac { t ^ { 3 } } { a } d t = \frac { 1 } { a } \cdot \frac { t ^ { 4 } } { 4 } + C{/tex} {tex}= \frac { ( a x + b ) ^ { 4 } } { 4 a } + C{/tex} [put t = ax + b]
solution_html:
Let I = {tex}\int ( a x + b ) ^ { 3 } d x{/tex}
Put t = ax + b
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac { d t } { d x } = a \Rightarrow \frac { d t } { a } = d x{/tex}
 {tex}\therefore \quad I = \int \frac { t ^ { 3 } } { a } d t = \frac { 1 } { a } \cdot \frac { t ^ { 4 } } { 4 } + C{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { ( a x + b ) ^ { 4 } } { 4 a } + C{/tex} [put t = ax + b]

question_html: Evaluate {tex} \int \frac { 2 x } { \left( x ^ { 2 } + 1 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 3 \right) } d x.{/tex}
question_text: Evaluate {tex} \int \frac { 2 x } { \left( x ^ { 2 } + 1 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 3 \right) } d x.{/tex}
explanation: Given {tex} I = \int \frac { 2 x } { \left( 1 + x ^ { 2 } \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 3 \right) } d x{/tex} Put x 2 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2xdx = dt {tex} \therefore I = \int \frac { d t } { ( t + 1 ) ( 3 + t ) }{/tex} Using partial fractions, {tex} \frac { 1 } { ( t + 1 ) ( 3 + t ) } = \frac { A } { ( 1 + t ) } + \frac { B } { ( 3 + t ) }{/tex} ...(i) {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{/tex} 1 = A(3 + t) + B(1 + t) Putting t = -3, we get {tex} \Rightarrow 1 = - 2 B \Rightarrow B = - \frac { 1 } { 2 }{/tex} Putting t = -1, we get {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} 1 = 2A {tex} \Rightarrow A={1\over2}{/tex} On putting A = {tex} \frac { 1 } { 2 }{/tex} and B = {tex} - \frac { 1 } { 2 }{/tex} in Eq. (i), we get {tex} \frac { 1 } { ( 1 + t ) ( 3 + t ) } = \frac { 1 \over 2 } { 1 + t } + \frac { (- 1) \over 2 } { 3 + t }{/tex} Integrating both sides w.r.t. to t , {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac { 1 } { ( 1 + t ) ( 3 + t ) } d t = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { 1 } { 1 + t } d t - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { 1 } { 3 + t } d t{/tex} {tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log | 1 + t | - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log | 3 + t | +C{/tex} {tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log| 1 + x ^ { 2 } |- \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log|3 + x ^ { 2 }| + C [ \text { put } t = x ^ { 2 } ]{/tex} {tex} \therefore \quad I = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \left| \frac { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } { 3 + x ^ { 2 } } \right| + C{/tex} [ {tex}\because{/tex} log m - log n = log {tex}\frac{m}{n}{/tex} ]
solution: Given {tex} I = \int \frac { 2 x } { \left( 1 + x ^ { 2 } \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 3 \right) } d x{/tex} Put x 2 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2xdx = dt {tex} \therefore I = \int \frac { d t } { ( t + 1 ) ( 3 + t ) }{/tex} Using partial fractions, {tex} \frac { 1 } { ( t + 1 ) ( 3 + t ) } = \frac { A } { ( 1 + t ) } + \frac { B } { ( 3 + t ) }{/tex} ...(i) {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{/tex} 1 = A(3 + t) + B(1 + t) Putting t = -3, we get {tex} \Rightarrow 1 = - 2 B \Rightarrow B = - \frac { 1 } { 2 }{/tex} Putting t = -1, we get {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} 1 = 2A {tex} \Rightarrow A={1\over2}{/tex} On putting A = {tex} \frac { 1 } { 2 }{/tex} and B = {tex} - \frac { 1 } { 2 }{/tex} in Eq. (i), we get {tex} \frac { 1 } { ( 1 + t ) ( 3 + t ) } = \frac { 1 \over 2 } { 1 + t } + \frac { (- 1) \over 2 } { 3 + t }{/tex} Integrating both sides w.r.t. to t , {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac { 1 } { ( 1 + t ) ( 3 + t ) } d t = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { 1 } { 1 + t } d t - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { 1 } { 3 + t } d t{/tex} {tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log | 1 + t | - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log | 3 + t | +C{/tex} {tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log| 1 + x ^ { 2 } |- \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log|3 + x ^ { 2 }| + C [ \text { put } t = x ^ { 2 } ]{/tex} {tex} \therefore \quad I = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \left| \frac { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } { 3 + x ^ { 2 } } \right| + C{/tex} [ {tex}\because{/tex} log m - log n = log {tex}\frac{m}{n}{/tex} ]
solution_html:
Given {tex} I = \int \frac { 2 x } { \left( 1 + x ^ { 2 } \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 3 \right) } d x{/tex}
Put x2 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2xdx = dt
{tex} \therefore I = \int \frac { d t } { ( t + 1 ) ( 3 + t ) }{/tex}
Using partial fractions,
{tex} \frac { 1 } { ( t + 1 ) ( 3 + t ) } = \frac { A } { ( 1 + t ) } + \frac { B } { ( 3 + t ) }{/tex} ...(i)
{tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{/tex}1 = A(3 + t) + B(1 + t)
Putting t = -3, we get
{tex} \Rightarrow 1 = - 2 B \Rightarrow B = - \frac { 1 } { 2 }{/tex}
Putting t = -1, we get
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex}1 = 2A {tex} \Rightarrow A={1\over2}{/tex}
On putting A = {tex} \frac { 1 } { 2 }{/tex} and B = {tex} - \frac { 1 } { 2 }{/tex} in Eq. (i), we get
{tex} \frac { 1 } { ( 1 + t ) ( 3 + t ) } = \frac { 1 \over 2 } { 1 + t } + \frac { (- 1) \over 2 } { 3 + t }{/tex}
Integrating both sides w.r.t. to t ,
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac { 1 } { ( 1 + t ) ( 3 + t ) } d t = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { 1 } { 1 + t } d t - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \int \frac { 1 } { 3 + t } d t{/tex}
{tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log | 1 + t | - \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log | 3 + t | +C{/tex}
{tex} = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log| 1 + x ^ { 2 } |- \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log|3 + x ^ { 2 }| + C [ \text { put } t = x ^ { 2 } ]{/tex}
{tex} \therefore \quad I = \frac { 1 } { 2 } \log \left| \frac { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } { 3 + x ^ { 2 } } \right| + C{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} log m - log n = log{tex}\frac{m}{n}{/tex}]

solution_html:
According to the question, {tex} \int _ { 0 } ^ { 1 } \frac { \log | 1 + x | } { 1 + x ^ { 2 } } d x{/tex}
Let , x = tan {tex} \theta{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad d x = \sec ^ { 2 } \theta d \theta{/tex}
Lower limit, when x = 0, then {tex} \theta= tan^{-1} 0= 0{/tex} 
Upper limit, when x = 1, then {tex} \theta = tan^{-1} ={/tex}  {tex} \frac { \pi } { 4 }.{/tex}
{tex} \therefore \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 4 } \frac { \log | 1 + \tan \theta | } { 1 + \tan ^ { 2 } \theta } \sec ^ { 2 } \theta d \theta{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 4 } \frac { \log | 1 + \tan \theta | \sec ^ { 2 } \theta } { \sec ^ { 2 } \theta } d \theta{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 4 } \log | 1 + \tan \theta | d \theta{/tex}...(i)
{tex} = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 4 } \log \left| 1 + \tan \left( \frac { \pi } { 4 } - \theta \right) \right| d \theta{/tex}{tex} \left[ \because \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f \left( x) d x = \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( a - x ) d x \right]\right.{/tex}
{tex} = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 4 } \log \left| 1 + \frac { 1 - \tan \theta } { 1 + \tan \theta } \right| d \theta{/tex}
{tex} = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 4 } \log \left| \frac { 2 } { 1 + \tan \theta } \right| d \theta{/tex}
{tex} = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 4 } \log 2 d \theta - \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 4 } \log | 1 + \tan \theta | d \theta{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \log 2 \cdot [ \theta ] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi / 4 } - I{/tex} [from Equation (i)]
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad 2 I = \frac { \pi } { 4 } \log 2{/tex}
{tex} \therefore \quad I = \frac { \pi } { 8 } \log 2{/tex}

solution: {tex}I=\int_{\pi /6}^{\pi /3} {\frac{{dx}}{{1 + \sqrt {\tan x} }}} {/tex} {tex}I = \int_{\pi /6}^{\pi /3} {\frac{{dx}}{{1 + \sqrt {\frac{{\sin x}}{{\cos x}}} }} = \int_{\pi /6}^{\pi /3} {\frac{{\sqrt {\cos x} }}{{\sqrt {\cos x} + \sqrt {\sin x} }}dx} } {/tex} ...(1) {tex}I = \int_{\pi /6}^{\pi /3} {\frac{{\sqrt {\cos \left( {\frac{\pi }{3} + \frac{\pi }{6} - x} \right)} }}{{\sqrt {\cos \left( {\frac{\pi }{3} + \frac{\pi }{6} - x} \right)} + \sqrt {\sin \left( {\frac{\pi }{3} + \frac{\pi }{6} - x} \right)} }}dx} {/tex} {tex}\left[ {\because \int_a^b {f\left( x \right)dx = \int_a^b {f\left( {a + b - x} \right)dx} } } \right]{/tex} {tex}I = \int_{\pi /6}^{\pi /3} {\frac{{\sqrt {\cos \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)} }}{{\sqrt {\cos \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)} + \sqrt {\sin \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)} }}dx} {/tex} {tex}I = \int_{\pi /6}^{\pi /3} {\frac{{\sqrt {\sin x} }}{{\sqrt {\sin x} + \sqrt {\cos x} }}dx} {/tex} ...(2) Adding (1) and (2), we get {tex}2I = \int_{\pi /6}^{\pi /3} {1dx} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ x \right]_{\pi /6}^{\pi /3}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{\pi }{3} - \frac{\pi }{6} = \frac{\pi }{6}{/tex} {tex}I = \frac{\pi }{{12}}{/tex}

{tex}I=\int_{\pi /6}^{\pi /3} {\frac{{dx}}{{1 + \sqrt {\tan x} }}} {/tex}
{tex}I = \int_{\pi /6}^{\pi /3} {\frac{{dx}}{{1 + \sqrt {\frac{{\sin x}}{{\cos x}}} }} = \int_{\pi /6}^{\pi /3} {\frac{{\sqrt {\cos x} }}{{\sqrt {\cos x} + \sqrt {\sin x} }}dx} } {/tex}...(1)
{tex}I = \int_{\pi /6}^{\pi /3} {\frac{{\sqrt {\cos \left( {\frac{\pi }{3} + \frac{\pi }{6} - x} \right)} }}{{\sqrt {\cos \left( {\frac{\pi }{3} + \frac{\pi }{6} - x} \right)} + \sqrt {\sin \left( {\frac{\pi }{3} + \frac{\pi }{6} - x} \right)} }}dx} {/tex}
{tex}\left[ {\because \int_a^b {f\left( x \right)dx = \int_a^b {f\left( {a + b - x} \right)dx} } } \right]{/tex}
{tex}I = \int_{\pi /6}^{\pi /3} {\frac{{\sqrt {\cos \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)} }}{{\sqrt {\cos \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)} + \sqrt {\sin \left( {\frac{\pi }{2} - x} \right)} }}dx} {/tex}
{tex}I = \int_{\pi /6}^{\pi /3} {\frac{{\sqrt {\sin x} }}{{\sqrt {\sin x} + \sqrt {\cos x} }}dx} {/tex} ...(2)
Adding (1) and (2), we get
{tex}2I = \int_{\pi /6}^{\pi /3} {1dx} {/tex}
{tex}= \left[ x \right]_{\pi /6}^{\pi /3}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{\pi }{3} - \frac{\pi }{6} = \frac{\pi }{6}{/tex}
{tex}I = \frac{\pi }{{12}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
explanation: let the given integral be, I = {tex}\int \frac{3 x+5}{\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}} \cdot d x{/tex} I = {tex}3 \int \frac{x+5 / 3}{\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}} \cdot d x{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{3}{2} \int \frac{2 x+10 / 3}{\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}} \cdot d x{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{3}{2} \int \frac{2 x-8+8+10 / 3}{\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}} \cdot d x{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{3}{2} \int \frac{2 x-8}{\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}} \cdot d x{/tex} + {tex}\frac{3}{2} \int \frac{34/{3}}{\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}} \cdot d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{3}{2}{/tex} (I 1 + I 2 ) ...(A) x 2 - 8x + 7 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2x - 8) dx = dt I 1 = {tex}\int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{t}}{/tex} I 1 = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{t}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}{/tex} + C 1 ...(i) Now, I 2 = {tex}\frac{34}{3} \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}} \cdot d x{/tex} I 2 = {tex}\frac{34}{3} \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{(x-4)^{2}-9}} \cdot d x{/tex} I 2 = {tex}\frac{34}{3}{/tex} log(|(x - 4) + {tex}\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}{/tex} |) + C 2 ...(ii) Using (i), and (ii) in (A) we get I = {tex}\frac{3}{2}\left[\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}\right.{/tex} + {tex}\frac{34}{3} \log (|(x-4)+\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}|){/tex} + C]
solution: let the given integral be, I = {tex}\int \frac{3 x+5}{\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}} \cdot d x{/tex} I = {tex}3 \int \frac{x+5 / 3}{\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}} \cdot d x{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{3}{2} \int \frac{2 x+10 / 3}{\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}} \cdot d x{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{3}{2} \int \frac{2 x-8+8+10 / 3}{\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}} \cdot d x{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{3}{2} \int \frac{2 x-8}{\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}} \cdot d x{/tex} + {tex}\frac{3}{2} \int \frac{34/{3}}{\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}} \cdot d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{3}{2}{/tex} (I 1 + I 2 ) ...(A) x 2 - 8x + 7 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2x - 8) dx = dt I 1 = {tex}\int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{t}}{/tex} I 1 = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{t}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}{/tex} + C 1 ...(i) Now, I 2 = {tex}\frac{34}{3} \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}} \cdot d x{/tex} I 2 = {tex}\frac{34}{3} \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{(x-4)^{2}-9}} \cdot d x{/tex} I 2 = {tex}\frac{34}{3}{/tex} log(|(x - 4) + {tex}\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}{/tex} |) + C 2 ...(ii) Using (i), and (ii) in (A) we get I = {tex}\frac{3}{2}\left[\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}\right.{/tex} + {tex}\frac{34}{3} \log (|(x-4)+\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}|){/tex} + C]
solution_html:
let the given integral be,

I = {tex}\int \frac{3 x+5}{\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}} \cdot d x{/tex} 
I = {tex}3 \int \frac{x+5 / 3}{\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}} \cdot d x{/tex} 
I = {tex}\frac{3}{2} \int \frac{2 x+10 / 3}{\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}} \cdot d x{/tex} 
I = {tex}\frac{3}{2} \int \frac{2 x-8+8+10 / 3}{\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}} \cdot d x{/tex} 
I = {tex}\frac{3}{2} \int \frac{2 x-8}{\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}} \cdot d x{/tex} + {tex}\frac{3}{2} \int \frac{34/{3}}{\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}} \cdot d x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{3}{2}{/tex} (I1 + I2)   ...(A) 
x2 - 8x + 7 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2x - 8) dx = dt
I1 = {tex}\int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{t}}{/tex} 
I1 = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{t}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}{/tex} + C1 ...(i)
Now,
I2 = {tex}\frac{34}{3} \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}} \cdot d x{/tex} 
I2 = {tex}\frac{34}{3} \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{(x-4)^{2}-9}} \cdot d x{/tex} 
I2 = {tex}\frac{34}{3}{/tex} log(|(x - 4) + {tex}\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}{/tex} |) + C2  ...(ii)
Using (i), and (ii) in (A) we get
I = {tex}\frac{3}{2}\left[\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}\right.{/tex} + {tex}\frac{34}{3} \log (|(x-4)+\sqrt{x^{2}-8 x+7}|){/tex} + C]

explanation: To find: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{\left(x^{2}+16\right)}{/tex} Formed Used: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{a^{2}+x^{2}}=\frac{1}{a} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)+C{/tex} Rewriting the given equation, {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{d x}{4^{2}+x^{2}}{/tex} Here a = 4 {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{4} \times \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{4}\right)+c{/tex} Therefore, {tex}\frac{d x}{\left(x^{2}+16\right)}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{4} \times \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{4}\right)+C{/tex}
solution: To find: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{\left(x^{2}+16\right)}{/tex} Formed Used: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{a^{2}+x^{2}}=\frac{1}{a} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)+C{/tex} Rewriting the given equation, {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{d x}{4^{2}+x^{2}}{/tex} Here a = 4 {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{4} \times \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{4}\right)+c{/tex} Therefore, {tex}\frac{d x}{\left(x^{2}+16\right)}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{4} \times \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{4}\right)+C{/tex}
solution_html:
To find: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{\left(x^{2}+16\right)}{/tex}
Formed Used: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{a^{2}+x^{2}}=\frac{1}{a} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)+C{/tex}
Rewriting the given equation,
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{d x}{4^{2}+x^{2}}{/tex}
Here a = 4
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{4} \times \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{4}\right)+c{/tex}
Therefore,
{tex}\frac{d x}{\left(x^{2}+16\right)}{/tex}{tex}=\frac{1}{4} \times \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{4}\right)+C{/tex}

\limits_{h \rightarrow 0} h{/tex} {tex}\left\{6 n+7 h \times \frac{n(n-1)}{2}+h^{2} \times \frac{n(n-1)(2 n-1)}{6}\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\lim \limits_{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{2}{n}\left\{6 n+\frac{14}{n} \times \frac{n(n-1)}{2}+\frac{4}{n^{2}} \times \frac{n(n-1)(2 n-1)}{6}\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\lim \limits_{n \rightarrow \infty}\left\{12+14\left(\frac{n-1}{n}\right)+\frac{8}{6} \frac{(n-1)(2 n-1)}{n^{2}}\right\}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\lim \limits_{n \rightarrow \infty}\left\{12+14\left(1-\frac{1}{n}\right)+\frac{4}{3}\left(1-\frac{1}{n}\right)\left(2-\frac{1}{n}\right)\right\}={/tex} {tex}12+14+\frac{4}{3} \times 2=12+14+\frac{8}{3}=\frac{86}{3}{/tex}
solution_html:
Solving this integral as a limit of sum we have{tex}\int_{a}^{b} f(x) d x=\lim \limits_{h \rightarrow 0}{/tex} {tex}h[f(a)+f(a+h)+f(a+2 h)+\ldots+f(a+(n-1) h)]{/tex} Where, {tex}h=\frac{b-a}{n}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad I=\int_{1}^{3}\left(x^{2}+5 x\right) d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\lim \limits_{h \rightarrow 0} h{/tex}{tex}[f(1)+f(1+h)+f(1+2 h)+\ldots+f(1+(n-1) h)]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\lim \limits_{h \rightarrow 0} h{/tex}{tex}=\left[\left\{\left(1^{2}+5 \times 1\right)\right\}+\left\{(1+h)^{2}+5(1+h)\right\}+\left\{(1+2 h)^{2}+5(1+2 h)\right\}\right.{/tex} {tex}\left.+\ldots \ldots+\left\{(1+(n-1) h)^{2}+5(1+(n-1) h)\right\}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\lim \limits_{h \rightarrow 0} h{/tex}{tex}\left[\left\{\left(1^{2}+(1+h)^{2}+(1+2 h)^{2}+\ldots+(1+(n-1) h)^{2}\right\}\right.\right.{/tex}{tex}+5\{1+(1+h)+(1+2 h)+\ldots+(1+(n-1) h)\}]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\lim \limits _{h \rightarrow 0} h{/tex}{tex}=\left[\left\{n+2 h\left(1+2+3+\ldots+(n-1)+h^{2}\left(1^{2}+2^{2}+\ldots+(n-1)^{2}\right)\right\}\right.\right.{/tex}{tex}+5\{n+h(1+2+\ldots+(n-1))\}]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\lim \limits_{h \rightarrow 0} h{/tex}{tex}=\left[6 n+7 h(1+2+3+\ldots+(n-1))+h^{2}\left(1^{2}+2^{2}+\ldots+\ldots+(n-1)^{2}\right)\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\lim \limits_{h \rightarrow 0} h{/tex}{tex}\left\{6 n+7 h \times \frac{n(n-1)}{2}+h^{2} \times \frac{n(n-1)(2 n-1)}{6}\right\}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\lim \limits_{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{2}{n}\left\{6 n+\frac{14}{n} \times \frac{n(n-1)}{2}+\frac{4}{n^{2}} \times \frac{n(n-1)(2 n-1)}{6}\right\}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\lim \limits_{n \rightarrow \infty}\left\{12+14\left(\frac{n-1}{n}\right)+\frac{8}{6} \frac{(n-1)(2 n-1)}{n^{2}}\right\}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I=\lim \limits_{n \rightarrow \infty}\left\{12+14\left(1-\frac{1}{n}\right)+\frac{4}{3}\left(1-\frac{1}{n}\right)\left(2-\frac{1}{n}\right)\right\}={/tex}{tex}12+14+\frac{4}{3} \times 2=12+14+\frac{8}{3}=\frac{86}{3}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Prove that: {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x \tan x}{(\sec x+\tan x)} d x=\pi\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-1\right){/tex}.
question_text: Prove that: {tex}\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x \tan x}{(\sec x+\tan x)} d x=\pi\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-1\right){/tex} .
explanation: Let {tex}I=\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x \tan x}{(\sec x+\tan x)} d x{/tex} ......... (i) Then, {tex}I=\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{(\pi-x) \tan (\pi-x)}{[\sec (\pi-x)+\tan (\pi-x)]} d x{/tex} {tex}\left[\because \int \limits_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int \limits_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right]{/tex} or {tex}I=\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{(\pi-x) \tan x}{(\sec x+\tan x)} d x{/tex} ........... (ii) Adding (i) and (ii), we get {tex}2 I=\pi \int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\tan x}{(\sec x+\tan x)} d x{/tex} {tex}=\pi \int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\tan x(\sec x-\tan x)}{\left(\sec ^{2} x-\tan ^{2} x\right)} d x{/tex} {tex}=\pi \cdot\left[\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \sec x \tan x d x-\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \tan ^{2} x d x\right]{/tex} {tex}=\pi \cdot\left\{[\sec x]_{0}^{\pi}-\int_{0}^{\pi}\left(\sec ^{2} x-1\right) d x\right\}{/tex} {tex}=\pi \cdot\left\{-2-[\tan x]_{0}^{\pi}+[x]_{0}^{\pi}\right]=\pi(\pi-2){/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad I=\pi\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-1\right){/tex} i.e., {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x \tan x}{\sec x+\tan x} d x=\pi\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-1\right){/tex}
solution: Let {tex}I=\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x \tan x}{(\sec x+\tan x)} d x{/tex} ......... (i) Then, {tex}I=\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{(\pi-x) \tan (\pi-x)}{[\sec (\pi-x)+\tan (\pi-x)]} d x{/tex} {tex}\left[\because \int \limits_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int \limits_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right]{/tex} or {tex}I=\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{(\pi-x) \tan x}{(\sec x+\tan x)} d x{/tex} ........... (ii) Adding (i) and (ii), we get {tex}2 I=\pi \int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\tan x}{(\sec x+\tan x)} d x{/tex} {tex}=\pi \int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\tan x(\sec x-\tan x)}{\left(\sec ^{2} x-\tan ^{2} x\right)} d x{/tex} {tex}=\pi \cdot\left[\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \sec x \tan x d x-\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \tan ^{2} x d x\right]{/tex} {tex}=\pi \cdot\left\{[\sec x]_{0}^{\pi}-\int_{0}^{\pi}\left(\sec ^{2} x-1\right) d x\right\}{/tex} {tex}=\pi \cdot\left\{-2-[\tan x]_{0}^{\pi}+[x]_{0}^{\pi}\right]=\pi(\pi-2){/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad I=\pi\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-1\right){/tex} i.e., {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x \tan x}{\sec x+\tan x} d x=\pi\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-1\right){/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}I=\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x \tan x}{(\sec x+\tan x)} d x{/tex} ......... (i)
Then, {tex}I=\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{(\pi-x) \tan (\pi-x)}{[\sec (\pi-x)+\tan (\pi-x)]} d x{/tex} {tex}\left[\because \int \limits_{0}^{a} f(x) d x=\int \limits_{0}^{a} f(a-x) d x\right]{/tex}
or {tex}I=\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{(\pi-x) \tan x}{(\sec x+\tan x)} d x{/tex} ........... (ii)
Adding (i) and (ii), we get
{tex}2 I=\pi \int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\tan x}{(\sec x+\tan x)} d x{/tex} {tex}=\pi \int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \frac{\tan x(\sec x-\tan x)}{\left(\sec ^{2} x-\tan ^{2} x\right)} d x{/tex}
{tex}=\pi \cdot\left[\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \sec x \tan x d x-\int \limits_{0}^{\pi} \tan ^{2} x d x\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\pi \cdot\left\{[\sec x]_{0}^{\pi}-\int_{0}^{\pi}\left(\sec ^{2} x-1\right) d x\right\}{/tex}
{tex}=\pi \cdot\left\{-2-[\tan x]_{0}^{\pi}+[x]_{0}^{\pi}\right]=\pi(\pi-2){/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad I=\pi\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-1\right){/tex} i.e., {tex}\int_{0}^{\pi} \frac{x \tan x}{\sec x+\tan x} d x=\pi\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-1\right){/tex}
